1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
8	)
9	
10	// HelpEntry is one row in a screen's keymap. Keys is the displayed shortcut
11	// (e.g. "Enter/→/l"), Desc the human-readable action.
12	type HelpEntry struct {
13		Keys string
14		Desc string
15	}
16	
17	// helpHeader is the optional title row shown above the keymap. Empty for
18	// most screens; some screens use it to clarify mode-specific keys.
19	type helpSection struct {
20		Heading string
21		Entries []HelpEntry
22	}
23	
24	// screenHelp maps a Screen constant to the keys we want the help overlay
25	// to surface. Mirrors the inline help-line strings rendered at the bottom
26	// of each screen, with extra notes on globals (Ctrl+U, Ctrl+C, ?) so the
27	// overlay is self-contained.
28	//
29	// Form screens (compose, edit, password change, board-banner edit) are
30	// intentionally absent: they need to pass `?` through to textinput so the
31	// user can type it as a literal character. Their bottom help-line already
32	// surfaces every key they bind.
33	var screenHelp = map[Screen][]helpSection{
34		ScreenMainMenu: {{
35			Heading: "Main menu",
36			Entries: []HelpEntry{

40				{"q", "quit"},

1	package tui

4		"fmt"

11	
12	type menuItem struct {
13		label string
14		hint  string
15		to    Screen
16	}
17	
18	type mainMenuModel struct {
19		deps   Deps
20		items  []menuItem
21		cursor int
22	}
23	
24	func newMainMenuModel(deps Deps) mainMenuModel {
25		items := []menuItem{
26			{label: "看板列表 Boards", hint: "browse and read articles", to: ScreenBoardList},
27			{label: "水球 Water Balloons", hint: "private messages with online users", to: ScreenWBInbox},
28			{label: "線上使用者 Online", hint: "see who's logged in", to: ScreenOnline},
29			{label: "信箱 Mail", hint: "persistent threaded mail", to: ScreenMailInbox},
30			{label: "個人設定 User settings", hint: "password / bio / notifications", to: ScreenUserSettings},
31		}
32		// Admin gets an extra entry; positioned before the Quit row so the
33		// numeric shortcut for Quit always lands on the LAST item regardless
34		// of role (the "5 quits" shortcut becomes "6 quits" for admin).
35		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
36			items = append(items, menuItem{
37				label: "管理 Admin",
38				hint:  "manage user roles",
39				to:    ScreenAdminUsers,
40			})
41		}
42		items = append(items, menuItem{label: "離線 Quit", hint: "disconnect", to: -1})
43		return mainMenuModel{deps: deps, items: items}
44	}
45	
46	func (m mainMenuModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
47	
48	func (m mainMenuModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
49		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
50			switch k.String() {
51			case "q", "esc":
52				return m, tea.Quit
53			case "up", "k":
54				if m.cursor > 0 {
55					m.cursor--
56				}
57				return m, nil
58			case "down", "j":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62				return m, nil
63			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
64				it := m.items[m.cursor]
65				if it.to == -1 {
66					return m, tea.Quit
67				}
68				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
69			case "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7":
70				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
71				if idx < len(m.items) {
72					it := m.items[idx]
73					if it.to == -1 {
74						return m, tea.Quit
75					}
76					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
77				}
78			}
79		}
80		return m, nil
81	}
82	
83	func (m mainMenuModel) View() string {
84		u := m.deps.User
85		nick := u.Nickname
86		if nick == "" {
87			nick = u.UserID
88		}
89	
90		var b strings.Builder
91		b.WriteString("\n")
92		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" SSH-BBS · %s (%s) ", nick, u.UserID)))
93		b.WriteString("\n\n")
94	
95		if u.LastLoginAt.Valid {
96			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("上次登入 %s · 累計登入 %d 次 · 發文 %d 篇",
97				u.LastLoginAt.Time.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
98				u.NumLogins,
99				u.NumPosts,
100			)))
101			b.WriteString("\n\n")
102		}
103	
104		b.WriteString("  ")
105		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 主選單 Main Menu "))
106		b.WriteString("\n\n")
107	
108		for i, it := range m.items {
109			marker := "  "
110			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
111			if i == m.cursor {
112				marker = "▸ "
113				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-32s ", i+1, it.label))
114			}
115			b.WriteString(marker + row)
116			if it.hint != "" {
117				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
118			}
119			b.WriteString("\n")
120		}
121	
122		b.WriteString("\n  ")
123		b.WriteString(StyleHelp.Render(fmt.Sprintf("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-%d jump · ? help · q quit", len(m.items))))
124		b.WriteString("\n")
125		return b.String()
126	}
127

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 12:09:47Z)**_

技術上不難，但有幾個問題值得先想清楚。我掃了一下整個 codebase：

**現況**
- 真正用戶可見的 CJK 字串集中在 `internal/tui/`，約 **150 行非註解字串**，分布在 ~20 個 screen 檔案裡。
- `internal/store/`、`internal/chat/`、`internal/notify/`、`internal/markdown/` 裡的中文幾乎全是**註解**，不用翻譯。例外只有四個：
  - `store/boards.go:85-87` 的 seed board 標題（「歡迎來到 SSH-BBS / 測試板 / 閒聊」）—— 這是**內容**不是 UI，i18n 套不到。
  - `store/pushes.go:36-38` 回傳 `"推"`/`"噓"` —— 被 markdown export 用，是**檔案格式**，翻了會破壞 export 的可解析性（`markdown.go:297` 要 parse 回來）。
  - `tui/notify_helpers.go` webhook 推播標題（「丟了水球給你」「寄信給你」「了你的文章」）—— 這個受用戶 locale 設定影響合理。
- 已經有不少 label 是 **「中文 English」雙語並排**的格式：`"看板列表 Boards"`、`"水球 Water Balloons"`、`"離線 Quit"`。本身已經半 i18n 了。

**機械抽取的工作量：中等**
- 沒有現成的 string catalog，所有字串都 inline 在 `View()` 和 `fmt.Sprintf` 裡。要做就要：建一個 `internal/i18n` 包（簡單的 `T(key) string` + 內嵌 `locales/zh-TW.json` / `locales/en.json`），逐個 screen 把字串改成 `i18n.T("main_menu.title")`。約 1-2 天的純機械替換 + 少量正則。
- locale 來源：可以加 `users.locale` column（per-user 設定，搭配通知設定那個 sub-menu），或讀 SSH client 的 `LANG` env。
- 測試影響不大：keybinding-parity 表都是按 key 不按 label，主要是 `help_test.go` 跟少數斷言渲染輸出的測試要參數化。

**真正該想清楚的不是技術，是產品**
1. `推/噓/水球/板規/置頂/雙開/爆/[鎖][箭]` 是 PTT 文化標誌。翻成 `Push/Boo/Whisper/Pinned/God-tier` 會把 BBS 的味道抽掉。我建議這些**當專有名詞保留**，不進 i18n table。
2. 既然超過一半的標題已經是雙語並排格式，**最划算的路線可能是：保留雙語 chrome，只把純中文的 error/confirmation/placeholder 抽出來做 i18n**（例如 `"目前密碼錯誤"`、`"✓ 已儲存事件偏好"`、`"確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"` 這類）。這樣工作量降到約 30-40 個字串、半天可完成，而且不破壞「這是個台味 BBS」的識別。

**建議**
先做輕量版：`internal/i18n` + 抽純中文錯誤/狀態字串 + `users.locale` 欄位。如果之後真的要做完整 en-only mode 再回頭把雙語 label 拆成兩份 table。要不要我先把 `i18n` 骨架和遷移計畫寫成 `backlog/i18n.md` + 一條 P3 TODO？

---

```text
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## i18n Scaffolding Report

### 1. **Users Table Schema & Struct** (internal/store/users.go:10–25, 0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql)

**User struct fields:**
- ID, UserID, PasswordHash, Nickname, RealName, Email
- NumLogins, NumPosts, LastLoginAt, LastHost, CreatedAt
- Role, MustChangePassword, **Bio** (string)

**Migration pattern:** The most recent is `0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`. Bio was added via `ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';` at that migration's top level. The pattern is: one-off `ALTER TABLE` + `CREATE TABLE` for related entities (here: `user_notif_prefs` + `user_notif_targets`).

**Next migration would be:** `0010_add_user_locale.sql` (integers increment: 0001, 0002, ..., 0009 in use; next is **0010**).

**Column addition pattern:** For a `locale` TEXT column, use:
```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW';
```
Then update `userColumns` const (users.go:34–35) and `scanUser()` (line 40–46) to include the new field.

---

### 2. **Notification Prefs Pattern** (internal/store/notify.go, internal/tui/screen_notify_settings.go)

**Storage model:**
- **NotifyPrefs struct** (notify.go:15–21): Five boolean fields (`OnPush`, `OnWB`, `OnMail`, `OnReply`, `OnlyWhenOffline`).
- **Lazy-loaded defaults:** `DefaultNotifyPrefs()` (line 26–28) mirrors column DEFAULTs; **absence from DB = all defaults**.
- **Upsert pattern:** `GetPrefs()` (line 64–84) returns defaults if row absent; `SetPrefs()` (line 87–103) **upserts via `ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE`**.

**TUI load & update:**
- Screen loads prefs in constructor `newNotifySettingsModel()` (screen_notify_settings.go:72–97):
  ```go
  p, err := deps.Store.Notify().GetPrefs(ctx, deps.User.ID)
  m.prefs = p
  ```
- Toggle tracked with `prefsDirty` flag (line 46); saved via `Ctrl+S` → `savePrefs()` (line 229–246):
  ```go
  m.deps.Store.Notify().SetPrefs(context.Background(), m.deps.User.ID, m.prefs)
  ```
- **No User struct refresh** — prefs live in separate table, don't mutate `User` object.

---

### 3. **User Settings Sub-Menu Architecture** (internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go:1–105)

**Sub-screen registry:** Static menuItem list in `newUserSettingsModel()` (lines 21–28). Currently four entries: password change, bio edit, notification settings, back.

**Navigation:** Each item has a `to: Screen` constant (ScreenPasswordChange, ScreenBioEdit, ScreenNotifySettings). When user presses Enter, emit `NavigateMsg{To: it.to}` (lines 50–51).

**Pattern for adding a toggle:** The settings sub-menu itself is purely **navigational**; it doesn't host toggles. Real toggles live in dedicated sub-screens (see bio-edit, notify-settings). **For a locale toggle, add a new ScreenLocaleSettings screen** or integrate inline (see below).

**Inline toggle pattern does NOT exist here.** Every editable setting gets its own Screen. However, for a simple 2-option toggle (zh-TW ↔ en), you could:
- Add a new `menuItem` in screen_user_settings.go pointing to a minimal `ScreenLocaleSettings`
- Or reuse notify-settings pattern: add `Locale` field to User struct, create `screen_locale_settings.go` with a single toggle similar to `flipPref()` in notify-settings (line 214–227).

---

### 4. **User Object in Deps: Load & Refresh** (internal/tui/root.go:17–24, screen_bio_edit.go:64–86)

**Deps struct** (root.go:17–24):
```go
type Deps struct {
    Store              *store.Store
    Broker             *chat.Broker
    Notify             *notify.Manager
    User               *store.User     // pointer; nil iff IsRegister
    IsRegister         bool
    MustChangePassword bool
}
```

**Refresh pattern** (screen_bio_edit.go:79–83):
```go
if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
    *m.deps.User = *fresh  // in-place mutation of the Deps.User pointer
}
```

**Impact:** This mutates the User object **in place** via pointer dereference. All screens sharing `deps` see the updated User immediately (no re-login needed). **For locale, follow this exact pattern** in your locale-settings save handler.

---

### 5. **Migration Version Numbers Currently in Use**

```
0001_init.sql (users, boards, articles, pushes, water_balloons)
0002_seed_boards.sql
0003_add_mail.sql
0004_add_user_roles.sql
0005_articles_updated_at.sql
0006_boards_banner.sql
0007_articles_pinned_at.sql
0008_articles_comments_mode.sql
0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql (bio + user_notif_prefs + user_notif_targets)
```

**Next:** Use `0010_add_user_locale.sql` for the locale column.
```

```text
1	-- User settings (bio) plus a per-user notification system.
2	-- bio is a free-form profile blurb. Empty default keeps existing rows valid.
3	-- user_notif_prefs holds the four event toggles (push / wb / mail / reply)
4	-- plus only_when_offline. Row is upserted lazily; absence ≡ all-default.
5	-- user_notif_targets stores per-user webhook URLs that the dispatcher
6	-- POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to. Compatible with caronc/apprise-api's
7	-- /notify/<key> endpoint and any other simple webhook (Discord, ntfy.sh,
8	-- Slack, custom). The BBS deliberately does NOT parse apprise:// URLs —
9	-- that is delegated to whatever webhook target the user points us at.
10	
11	ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';
12	
13	CREATE TABLE user_notif_prefs (
14	    user_id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
15	    on_push           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 推/噓/→ on my article
16	    on_wb             INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 水球
17	    on_mail           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 站內信
18	    on_reply          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 文章被回 (Re:) — fired when someone posts a Re: reply targeting one of my articles
19	    only_when_offline INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0  -- skip dispatch if any live session exists
20	);
21	
22	CREATE TABLE user_notif_targets (
23	    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
24	    user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
25	    label      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
26	    url        TEXT NOT NULL,
27	    enabled    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
28	    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
29	);
30	CREATE INDEX idx_notif_targets_user ON user_notif_targets(user_id, enabled);
31
```

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type User struct {
11		ID                 int64
12		UserID             string
13		PasswordHash       string
14		Nickname           string
15		RealName           string
16		Email              string
17		NumLogins          int64
18		NumPosts           int64
19		LastLoginAt        sql.NullTime
20		LastHost           sql.NullString
21		CreatedAt          time.Time
22		Role               Role
23		MustChangePassword bool
24		Bio                string
25	}
26	
27	type UserRepo struct{ s *Store }
28	
29	var (
30		ErrUserNotFound = errors.New("user not found")
31		ErrInvalidRole  = errors.New("invalid role")
32	)
33	
34	const userColumns = `id, user_id, password_hash, nickname, realname, email,
35		num_logins, num_posts, last_login_at, last_host, created_at, role, must_change_password, bio`
36	
37	func scanUser(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*User, error) {
38		u := &User{}
39		var mustChange int64
40		err := row.Scan(
41			&u.ID, &u.UserID, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Nickname, &u.RealName, &u.Email,
42			&u.NumLogins, &u.NumPosts, &u.LastLoginAt, &u.LastHost, &u.CreatedAt,
43			&u.Role, &mustChange, &u.Bio,
44		)
45		u.MustChangePassword = mustChange != 0
46		return u, err
47	}
48	
49	func (r *UserRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash, nickname, email string) (*User, error) {
50		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
51		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
52		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
53			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, nickname, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
54			userID, passwordHash, nickname, email,
55		)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, err
58		}
59		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
60		if err != nil {
61			return nil, err
62		}
63		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
64	}
65	
66	func (r *UserRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*User, error) {
67		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id)
68		u, err := scanUser(row)
69		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
70			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
71		}
72		return u, err
73	}
74	
75	func (r *UserRepo) GetByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (*User, error) {
76		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE user_id = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, userID)
77		u, err := scanUser(row)
78		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
79			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
80		}
81		return u, err
82	}
83	
84	func (r *UserRepo) NoteLogin(ctx context.Context, id int64, host string) error {
85		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
86		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
87		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
88			`UPDATE users SET last_login_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, last_host = ?, num_logins = num_logins + 1 WHERE id = ?`,
89			host, id,
90		)
91		return err
92	}
93	
94	func (r *UserRepo) IncrementPosts(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
95		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
96		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
97		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
98			`UPDATE users SET num_posts = num_posts + 1 WHERE id = ?`, id,
99		)
100		return err
101	}
102	
103	// InsertSystemAccount creates a privileged user (admin or guest) with the
104	// given role and must_change_password flag, bypassing the regular Create
105	// path which always defaults role='user'. Used only by the bootstrap seed.
106	func (r *UserRepo) InsertSystemAccount(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash string, role Role, mustChangePassword bool) (*User, error) {
107		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
108		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
109		mc := int64(0)
110		if mustChangePassword {
111			mc = 1
112		}
113		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
114			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, role, must_change_password) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
115			userID, passwordHash, string(role), mc,
116		)
117		if err != nil {
118			return nil, err
119		}
120		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
121		if err != nil {
122			return nil, err
123		}
124		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
125	}
126	
127	// SetPassword updates the password hash and clears must_change_password
128	// in one statement. The TUI password-change screen calls this after
129	// validating the current password and the new-password rules.
130	func (r *UserRepo) SetPassword(ctx context.Context, id int64, passwordHash string) error {
131		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
132		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
133		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
134			`UPDATE users SET password_hash = ?, must_change_password = 0 WHERE id = ?`,
135			passwordHash, id,
136		)
137		return err
138	}
139	
140	// SetBio updates the user's profile bio. Callers (the bio-edit screen) are
141	// responsible for length validation via auth.ValidateBio.
142	func (r *UserRepo) SetBio(ctx context.Context, id int64, bio string) error {
143		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
144		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
145		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
146			`UPDATE users SET bio = ? WHERE id = ?`, bio, id,
147		)
148		return err
149	}
150	
151	// SetRole updates the role of the given user. Callers (admin user-management
152	// screen) are responsible for enforcing higher-level invariants like the
153	// last-admin guard via CountByRole.
154	func (r *UserRepo) SetRole(ctx context.Context, id int64, role Role) error {
155		if !role.Valid() {
156			return ErrInvalidRole
157		}
158		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
159		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
160		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
161			`UPDATE users SET role = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(role), id,
162		)
163		return err
164	}
165	
166	// ListAll returns users ordered by id, paginated. limit<=0 defaults to 20.
167	func (r *UserRepo) ListAll(ctx context.Context, limit, offset int) ([]*User, error) {
168		if limit <= 0 {
169			limit = 20
170		}
171		if offset < 0 {
172			offset = 0
173		}
174		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
175			`SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`,
176			limit, offset,
177		)
178		if err != nil {
179			return nil, err
180		}
181		defer rows.Close()
182		var out []*User
183		for rows.Next() {
184			u, err := scanUser(rows)
185			if err != nil {
186				return nil, err
187			}
188			out = append(out, u)
189		}
190		return out, rows.Err()
191	}
192	
193	// CountByRole returns the number of users currently assigned a given role.
194	// Used by the admin screen's last-admin guard.
195	func (r *UserRepo) CountByRole(ctx context.Context, role Role) (int, error) {
196		var n int
197		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
198			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE role = ?`, string(role),
199		).Scan(&n)
200		return n, err
201	}

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// NotifyPrefs is the per-user notification toggle bag. The boolean fields map
12	// 1:1 to the `on_*` columns in user_notif_prefs. Defaults (all event toggles
13	// on, only_when_offline off) are returned when the row is absent — the row is
14	// upserted lazily on the first SetPrefs call.
15	type NotifyPrefs struct {
16		OnPush          bool
17		OnWB            bool
18		OnMail          bool
19		OnReply         bool
20		OnlyWhenOffline bool
21	}
22	
23	// DefaultNotifyPrefs is what GetPrefs returns for users that have never
24	// touched the notify settings screen. Mirrors the column DEFAULTs in
25	// migrations/0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql.
26	func DefaultNotifyPrefs() NotifyPrefs {
27		return NotifyPrefs{OnPush: true, OnWB: true, OnMail: true, OnReply: true}
28	}
29	
30	// NotifyTarget is one configured webhook URL for a user. The dispatcher
31	// POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to URL when an event fires; Enabled lets the
32	// user pause a target without losing it.
33	type NotifyTarget struct {
34		ID        int64
35		UserID    int64
36		Label     string
37		URL       string
38		Enabled   bool
39		CreatedAt time.Time
40	}
41	
42	// NotifyRepo owns the user_notif_prefs and user_notif_targets tables.
43	type NotifyRepo struct{ s *Store }
44	
45	var (
46		ErrNotifyTargetNotFound = errors.New("notify target not found")
47		ErrInvalidNotifyURL     = errors.New("notify target url must be http(s)://...")
48	)
49	
50	// ValidateNotifyURL enforces the bare minimum: scheme must be http or https.
51	// Anything beyond that (DNS, reachability) is the dispatcher's problem at
52	// delivery time — failing here would block legitimate self-hosted endpoints
53	// behind VPNs / private DNS.
54	func ValidateNotifyURL(u string) error {
55		u = strings.TrimSpace(u)
56		if strings.HasPrefix(u, "http://") || strings.HasPrefix(u, "https://") {
57			return nil
58		}
59		return ErrInvalidNotifyURL
60	}
61	
62	// GetPrefs returns the user's stored prefs, or DefaultNotifyPrefs() when
63	// the row is absent.
64	func (r *NotifyRepo) GetPrefs(ctx context.Context, userID int64) (NotifyPrefs, error) {
65		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
66			`SELECT on_push, on_wb, on_mail, on_reply, only_when_offline
67			 FROM user_notif_prefs WHERE user_id = ?`, userID,
68		)
69		var onPush, onWB, onMail, onReply, onlyOffline int64
70		err := row.Scan(&onPush, &onWB, &onMail, &onReply, &onlyOffline)
71		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
72			return DefaultNotifyPrefs(), nil
73		}
74		if err != nil {
75			return NotifyPrefs{}, err
76		}
77		return NotifyPrefs{
78			OnPush:          onPush != 0,
79			OnWB:            onWB != 0,
80			OnMail:          onMail != 0,
81			OnReply:         onReply != 0,
82			OnlyWhenOffline: onlyOffline != 0,
83		}, nil
84	}
85	
86	// SetPrefs upserts the prefs row for a user.
87	func (r *NotifyRepo) SetPrefs(ctx context.Context, userID int64, p NotifyPrefs) error {
88		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
89		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
90		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
91			`INSERT INTO user_notif_prefs
92			   (user_id, on_push, on_wb, on_mail, on_reply, only_when_offline)
93			 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
94			 ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE SET
95			   on_push=excluded.on_push,
96			   on_wb=excluded.on_wb,
97			   on_mail=excluded.on_mail,
98			   on_reply=excluded.on_reply,
99			   only_when_offline=excluded.only_when_offline`,
100			userID, b2i(p.OnPush), b2i(p.OnWB), b2i(p.OnMail), b2i(p.OnReply), b2i(p.OnlyWhenOffline),
101		)
102		return err
103	}
104	
105	// ListTargets returns every target row for a user, oldest-first so the
106	// settings screen renders a stable list.
107	func (r *NotifyRepo) ListTargets(ctx context.Context, userID int64) ([]*NotifyTarget, error) {
108		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
109			`SELECT id, user_id, label, url, enabled, created_at
110			 FROM user_notif_targets WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, userID,
111		)
112		if err != nil {
113			return nil, err
114		}
115		defer rows.Close()
116		var out []*NotifyTarget
117		for rows.Next() {
118			t := &NotifyTarget{}
119			var enabled int64
120			if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.UserID, &t.Label, &t.URL, &enabled, &t.CreatedAt); err != nil {
121				return nil, err
122			}
123			t.Enabled = enabled != 0
124			out = append(out, t)
125		}
126		return out, rows.Err()
127	}
128	
129	// ListEnabledTargets is the dispatcher's hot path: only enabled rows.
130	func (r *NotifyRepo) ListEnabledTargets(ctx context.Context, userID int64) ([]*NotifyTarget, error) {
131		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
132			`SELECT id, user_id, label, url, enabled, created_at
133			 FROM user_notif_targets WHERE user_id = ? AND enabled = 1 ORDER BY id ASC`, userID,
134		)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, err
137		}
138		defer rows.Close()
139		var out []*NotifyTarget
140		for rows.Next() {
141			t := &NotifyTarget{}
142			var enabled int64
143			if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.UserID, &t.Label, &t.URL, &enabled, &t.CreatedAt); err != nil {
144				return nil, err
145			}
146			t.Enabled = enabled != 0
147			out = append(out, t)
148		}
149		return out, rows.Err()
150	}
151	
152	// AddTarget validates the URL and inserts a new row. Returns the assigned id.
153	func (r *NotifyRepo) AddTarget(ctx context.Context, userID int64, label, url string) (int64, error) {
154		if err := ValidateNotifyURL(url); err != nil {
155			return 0, err
156		}
157		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
158		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
159		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
160			`INSERT INTO user_notif_targets (user_id, label, url) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`,
161			userID, strings.TrimSpace(label), strings.TrimSpace(url),
162		)
163		if err != nil {
164			return 0, err
165		}
166		return res.LastInsertId()
167	}
168	
169	// UpdateTarget mutates label/url/enabled in place. URL is validated.
170	// requesterUserID is checked against the row's user_id so a malicious
171	// caller can't update someone else's target by guessing the id.
172	func (r *NotifyRepo) UpdateTarget(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64, label, url string, enabled bool) error {
173		if err := ValidateNotifyURL(url); err != nil {
174			return err
175		}
176		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
177		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
178		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
179			`UPDATE user_notif_targets SET label = ?, url = ?, enabled = ?
180			 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
181			strings.TrimSpace(label), strings.TrimSpace(url), b2i(enabled), id, requesterUserID,
182		)
183		if err != nil {
184			return err
185		}
186		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
187		if n == 0 {
188			return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
189		}
190		return nil
191	}
192	
193	// SetTargetEnabled is the lightweight toggle path used by the `t` shortcut
194	// in the settings screen — separate from UpdateTarget to avoid re-asserting
195	// label/url validation on every keystroke.
196	func (r *NotifyRepo) SetTargetEnabled(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64, enabled bool) error {
197		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
198		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
199		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
200			`UPDATE user_notif_targets SET enabled = ?
201			 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
202			b2i(enabled), id, requesterUserID,
203		)
204		if err != nil {
205			return err
206		}
207		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
208		if n == 0 {
209			return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
210		}
211		return nil
212	}
213	
214	// DeleteTarget removes a row, gated on user_id so the caller can only
215	// delete their own targets.
216	func (r *NotifyRepo) DeleteTarget(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64) error {
217		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
218		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
219		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
220			`DELETE FROM user_notif_targets WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
221			id, requesterUserID,
222		)
223		if err != nil {
224			return err
225		}
226		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
227		if n == 0 {
228			return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
229		}
230		return nil
231	}
232	
233	func b2i(b bool) int64 {
234		if b {
235			return 1
236		}
237		return 0
238	}
239

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	)
9	
10	// userSettingsModel is the "個人設定" sub-menu reached from the main menu.
11	// Three entries: change password, edit bio, edit notification settings.
12	// Layout and key handling mirror mainMenuModel deliberately so muscle memory
13	// transfers across BBS-internal menus.
14	type userSettingsModel struct {
15		deps   Deps
16		items  []menuItem
17		cursor int
18	}
19	
20	func newUserSettingsModel(deps Deps) userSettingsModel {
21		items := []menuItem{
22			{label: "修改密碼 Change password", hint: "current → new → confirm", to: ScreenPasswordChange},
23			{label: "修改 Bio Edit bio", hint: "free-form profile blurb", to: ScreenBioEdit},
24			{label: "通知設定 Notification settings", hint: "webhook targets + per-event toggles", to: ScreenNotifySettings},
25			{label: "返回 Back", hint: "main menu", to: ScreenMainMenu},
26		}
27		return userSettingsModel{deps: deps, items: items}
28	}
29	
30	func (m userSettingsModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
31	
32	func (m userSettingsModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
33		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
34			switch k.String() {
35			case "esc", "q", "left", "h", "backspace":
36				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
37			case "Q":
38				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
39			case "up", "k":
40				if m.cursor > 0 {
41					m.cursor--
42				}
43				return m, nil
44			case "down", "j":
45				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
46					m.cursor++
47				}
48				return m, nil
49			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
50				it := m.items[m.cursor]
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
52			case "1", "2", "3", "4":
53				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
54				if idx < len(m.items) {
55					it := m.items[idx]
56					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
57				}
58			}
59		}
60		return m, nil
61	}
62	
63	func (m userSettingsModel) View() string {
64		u := m.deps.User
65		var b strings.Builder
66		b.WriteString("\n")
67		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 個人設定 User Settings "))
68		b.WriteString("\n\n")
69	
70		if u != nil {
71			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("帳號 %s · 角色 %s", u.UserID, u.Role)))
72			b.WriteString("\n")
73			bio := strings.TrimSpace(u.Bio)
74			if bio == "" {
75				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(尚未填寫 bio)"))
76			} else {
77				// Show only the first line of bio in the header so the menu
78				// itself stays compact; the bio-edit screen owns the full view.
79				firstLine := bio
80				if i := strings.IndexByte(firstLine, '\n'); i >= 0 {
81					firstLine = firstLine[:i]
82				}
83				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio: ") + Truncate(firstLine, 60))
84			}
85			b.WriteString("\n\n")
86		}
87	
88		for i, it := range m.items {
89			marker := "  "
90			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
91			if i == m.cursor {
92				marker = "▸ "
93				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-40s ", i+1, it.label))
94			}
95			b.WriteString(marker + row)
96			if it.hint != "" {
97				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
98			}
99			b.WriteString("\n")
100		}
101	
102		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-4 jump · Esc/←/h back"))
103		b.WriteString("\n")
104		return b.String()
105	}
106

30	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.

65	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
66	// counter from DB.

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
16	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
17	type Deps struct {
18		Store              *store.Store
19		Broker             *chat.Broker    // nil during register
20		Notify             *notify.Manager // may be nil in tests; call sites must guard
21		User               *store.User     // nil iff IsRegister
22		IsRegister         bool
23		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
24	}
25	
26	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
27	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
28	// and the toast footer.
29	type Root struct {
30		deps   Deps
31		sub    tea.Model
32		width  int
33		height int
34	
35		toast      string
36		toastUntil time.Time
37	
38		// activeScreen tracks which Screen the current sub corresponds to, so
39		// the help overlay can render a context-aware keymap. Set in NewRoot
40		// for the bootstrap sub and in navigate for every subsequent swap.
41		activeScreen Screen
42		// helpVisible is true while the help overlay should render in place of
43		// the sub view. Toggled on '?' and dismissed by any key.
44		helpVisible bool
45	}
46	
47	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
48	
49	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
50	//
51	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
52	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
53	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
54	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
55		r := Root{deps: deps}
56		switch {
57		case deps.IsRegister:
58			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
59			// No Screen const for register; help is suppressed via deps.IsRegister.
60		case deps.MustChangePassword:
61			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
62			r.activeScreen = ScreenPasswordChange
63		default:
64			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
65			r.activeScreen = ScreenMainMenu
66		}
67		return r
68	}
69	
70	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
71		return m.sub.Init()
72	}
73	
74	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
75		switch msg := msg.(type) {
76		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
77			m.width = msg.Width
78			m.height = msg.Height
79			var cmd tea.Cmd
80			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
81			return m, cmd
82	
83		case tea.KeyMsg:
84			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
85			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
86				return m, tea.Quit
87			}
88			// Help overlay: any key dismisses, swallow the keypress so the
89			// sub doesn't act on it. ctrl+c above is the deliberate exception.
90			if m.helpVisible {
91				m.helpVisible = false
92				return m, nil
93			}
94			// '?' opens the help overlay on screens that aren't typing-forms.
95			if msg.String() == "?" && helpAvailable(m.deps, m.activeScreen) {
96				m.helpVisible = true
97				return m, nil
98			}
99			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
100			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
101				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
102			}
103	
104		case NavigateMsg:
105			return m.navigate(msg)
106	
107		case ErrorMsg:
108			if msg.Err != nil {
109				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
110			}
111			return m, nil
112	
113		case WBIncomingMsg:
114			// Suppress the toast when the active screen is a wbThreadModel
115			// pointed at this exact counterparty — the thread itself appends
116			// the message, so the toast would be redundant noise.
117			showToast := true
118			if m.activeScreen == ScreenWBThread {
119				if tm, ok := m.sub.(wbThreadModel); ok && tm.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
120					showToast = false
121				}
122			}
123			if showToast {
124				m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
125			}
126			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect. (The
127			// thread also marks it read — both paths are idempotent.)
128			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
129				go func(id int64) {
130					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
131				}(msg.ID)
132			}
133			// fall through so the active screen can also react
134		}
135	
136		var cmd tea.Cmd
137		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
138		return m, cmd
139	}
140	
141	func (m Root) View() string {
142		if m.helpVisible {
143			// Replace the sub view entirely so the keymap reads cleanly. Toast
144			// suppressed too — would distract from a help screen.
145			return renderHelp(m.activeScreen)
146		}
147		v := m.sub.View()
148		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
149			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
150		}
```

Perfect. Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## i18n Glyph Rendering & Round-Trip Analysis

### 1. **Push Glyph Rendering Call Sites**

All push kind glyphs (推/噓/→) are rendered from two central functions:

**TUI display:**
- `/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:665-675` — `renderPushKind(k)` function returns styled glyphs for UI rendering:
  - Line 668: `StylePushKind.Render("推")` for push
  - Line 670: `StyleBooKind.Render("噓")` for boo
  - Line 672: `StyleArrowKind.Render("→")` for arrow
- Used at line 606 to render each push in the article view table

**Notification titles (webhook):**
- `/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:677-690` — `kindLabel(k)` function returns plain text glyphs for webhooks/notifications:
  - Line 683: returns `"推"` for push
  - Line 685: returns `"噓"` for boo
  - Line 687: returns `"→"` for arrow
- Called at line 544 in notification dispatch: `fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章", u.UserID, kindLabel(m.pushKind))`

**Store layer:**
- `/internal/store/pushes.go:33-43` — `PushKind.Glyph()` method (mirrors renderPushKind/kindLabel):
  - Line 36: `"推"` for PushKindPush
  - Line 38: `"噓"` for PushKindBoo
  - Line 40: `"→"` for PushKindArrow
- Used by markdown export at line 143

**Markdown export (disk output):**
- `/internal/markdown/markdown.go:137-144` — push serialization in Format():
  - Line 143: `fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s [%s] %s  %s\n", p.Kind.Glyph(), ...)` — outputs the glyph to disk

**Inline help text:**
- `/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:639` — help line shows "+ 推 · - 噓 · = →"
- Line 492: error message "comment required for 推/噓"

---

### 2. **Markdown Export/Parse Round-Trip Analysis**

**CRITICAL: This IS persisted and round-tripped.**

The export flow:
1. User presses `y` → navigates to `ScreenArticleExport` 
2. `screen_article_export.go:66` calls `markdown.Format(m.article, m.pushes, FormatOpts{IncludePushes: true, ...})`
3. Export renders pushes at line 143: `"- %s [%s] %s  %s\n"` where first `%s` is `p.Kind.Glyph()` (推/噓/→)
4. User can save to `data/exports/<userid>/<id>-<unix>.md`

The import flow (round-trip):
1. CLI: `sshbbs import --file FILE.md --board NAME`
2. `/cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go:54` calls `markdown.Parse(string(body))`
3. `/internal/markdown/markdown.go:267-277` — `parsePushes()` parses the push block
4. `/internal/markdown/markdown.go:280-338` — `parsePushLine()` **EXPLICITLY PARSES THE GLYPH**:
   - Line 297: `case "推":` → `kind = store.PushKindPush`
   - Line 299: `case "噓":` → `kind = store.PushKindBoo`
   - Line 301: `case "→":` → `kind = store.PushKindArrow`
   - Line 303: `default: return ParsedPush{}, false` (unknown glyph fails parse)

**CONSEQUENCE: Changing the glyph in English mode WILL break the import round-trip.** If you export as "- 👍 [alice] ..." and later try to import that file, `parsePushLine()` won't recognize "👍" and will silently drop the push (line 303 returns `false`).

Note: `cmd_import.go:19-21` explicitly states: "Pushes in the markdown file are deliberately ignored — round-tripping pushes requires verifying each push's author exists in the users table, which is left for a future iteration." But the parsing infrastructure exists and *will parse* the glyphs if you call Parse directly.

---

### 3. **Column Width Validation**

**Good news: Width parity confirmed.**

`/internal/tui/runewidth.go` provides:
- Line 19-26: `PadRight(s, w)` uses `runewidth.StringWidth()` 
- Line 55-56: `Width(s)` public wrapper around `runewidth.StringWidth()`

Width measurements (using `go-runewidth`):
- CJK glyphs (推=2, 噓=2, 爆=2, 鎖=2, 箭=2)
- Emoji (👍=2, 👎=2, 💥=2)
- ASCII arrow (→=1) ← NOTE: renders as 1-cell, NOT 2!

**Problem discovered:** → is 1 cell wide, not 2. If you map → to 👍, you *gain* 1 cell. The score column and title truncation at line 284-290 uses computed widths, so a 1-cell vs 2-cell glyph WILL shift the table layout. You need to either:
1. Map 推→👍 but keep 噓→👎 and introduce a fixed-width alias for arrow (e.g., " →" with leading space = 2 cells)
2. Or accept that en-mode uses emojis and recalculate titleW in the scoring switch

No test suite exists for width assertions. You should add one to `*_test.go` that validates both CJK and emoji render correctly in the score column.

---

### 4. **"爆" (Explosion) Rendering**

`/internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:299-307`:
```
case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
  score = "爆"
```

**Threshold: RecommendScore ≥ 100.** Map to 💥 or "HOT" in English mode.
- Width of "爆" = 2 cells
- Width of "💥" = 2 cells  
- Width of "HOT" = 3 cells (width overflow!)

If you use "HOT", the score column expands. The config uses a fixed `scoreW = 4` (line 281), so "HOT" fits (3 chars) but "爆" or "💥" are both width=2. Recommendation: use 💥 for 1:1 width parity, or pad "HOT" to 2 cells (e.g., "HT" or use a 2-char marker).

---

### 5. **Lock/Arrow Badges**

`/internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:318-323`:
- Line 320: `prefix += "[鎖]"` for CommentsModeLocked (width of 鎖=2, total badge="[鎖]"=4 cells)
- Line 322: `prefix += "[箭]"` for CommentsModeArrowsOnly (width of 箭=2, total badge="[箭]"=4 cells)

Also rendered in article view:
- `screen_article_view.go:574` — `"[箭] 僅開放箭頭"` in the header
- `screen_article_view.go:576` — `"[鎖] 已關閉留言"` in the header

English equivalents need to maintain ≤4 cell width for title truncation not to break:
- `[🔒]` = 5 cells (bracket + emoji + bracket) — OVERFLOW
- `[L]` = 3 cells — OK
- `[→]` = 3 cells — OK (reusing arrow)

---

### 6. **Notification Titles for Webhooks**

`/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:537-546`:
```go
if m.deps.Notify != nil && m.article.AuthorID != u.ID {
  m.deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
    Title: fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章", u.UserID, kindLabel(m.pushKind)),
    ...
  })
}
```

**Issue: Notification titles are sent to user-configured external webhooks.** These titles contain the push verb rendered in Chinese (推/噓/→). 

**Decision needed:** Should these respect recipient locale? Currently:
- The dispatcher sends the same Chinese text to all webhooks
- Webhook consumers (Discord, Slack, Telegram, SMS) render it as-is
- Non-Chinese speakers see "推" / "噓" / "→" in their notifications

Two approaches:
1. **Locale-oblivious:** Keep Chinese in webhook titles (current state) — most users on a PTT-style BBS are Chinese speakers
2. **Locale-aware:** Require the store/article to track recipient's locale, then call `kindLabel()` at dispatch time with a target locale
   - This requires propagating locale through `notify.Event` or looking it up from `ToUserID`

For an MVP English mode, **webhook titles should probably respect the recipient's configured UI locale**, but this requires refactoring the notify dispatcher to accept a target locale and adding a translation layer for kindLabel(). Currently, `kindLabel()` is hardcoded to Chinese glyphs.

**Other notify call sites:**
- `/internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:13-24` — `notifyWB()` for water balloons, hardcoded Chinese: `"[BBS] %s 丟了水球給你"`
- `/internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:28-44` — `notifyMail()` for mail, hardcoded Chinese: `"[BBS] %s 寄信給你: %s"`

All three need localization for proper English mode support.

---

### Summary Table

| Item | File:Line | Status | Impact |
|------|-----------|--------|--------|
| Push glyph rendering (TUI) | screen_article_view.go:668,670,672 | Centralized in `renderPushKind()` | High — 1 function, easy to swap |
| Push glyph parsing (markdown) | markdown.go:297-303 | Hard-coded glyphs in switch | **BREAKING** — export/import round-trip depends on exact glyph |

| Lock/arrow badges | screen_board_view.go:320,322 | Hardcoded glyphs in prefix | Medium — title truncation depends on prefix width |

**Recommendation:** Create an `i18n.PushKind` function that returns a localized glyph/label, then update `renderPushKind()`, `kindLabel()`, `PushKind.Glyph()`, and the markdown parser to use it. For round-trip safety, **either persist the kind enum (not the glyph) to markdown export, or accept that localized exports cannot be re-imported.**

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
14	)
15	
16	// notifyTestResultMsg is delivered by the goroutine that ran SendTest.
17	// The screen renders msg.err in the error/flash row so the user sees
18	// the HTTP outcome inline.
19	type notifyTestResultMsg struct {
20		targetID int64
21		err      error
22	}
23	
24	// notifySettingsModel is the unified screen for the per-event toggles
25	// (user_notif_prefs) and webhook target list (user_notif_targets).
26	//
27	// Layout:
28	//
29	//   - Top: 5 boolean toggles (Push / WB / Mail / Reply / OnlyWhenOffline)
30	//     navigated with j/k, flipped with Space. Persisted via Ctrl+S.
31	//   - Bottom: target list, then "+ Add new target" entry. Targets toggle
32	//     enabled with `t`, edit with `e`, delete with `d`. Adds and edits
33	//     pop an inline two-field form (label + URL).
34	//
35	// State machine: mode == modeList during normal browsing; switches to
36	// modeEdit while the inline form is up. Mirrors the inline-textinput-
37	// inside-list pattern used by screen_wb.go.
38	type notifySettingsModel struct {
39		deps Deps
40	
41		prefs   store.NotifyPrefs
42		targets []*store.NotifyTarget
43		loadErr error
44	
45		cursor      int  // 0..4 = toggles, 5..5+N-1 = targets, 5+N = "+ add"
46		prefsDirty  bool // true if Ctrl+S would persist a change
47	
48		mode editMode
49	
50		editLabel textinput.Model
51		editURL   textinput.Model
52		editFocus int   // 0 = label, 1 = url
53		editingID int64 // 0 for new target, otherwise target.ID
54	
55		width  int
56		height int
57		err    string
58		flash  string // transient success message
59	}
60	
61	type editMode int
62	
63	const (
64		modeList editMode = iota
65		modeEdit
66	)
67	
68	const (
69		notifPrefCount = 5 // Push, WB, Mail, Reply, OnlyWhenOffline
70	)
71	
72	func newNotifySettingsModel(deps Deps) notifySettingsModel {
73		m := notifySettingsModel{deps: deps}
74		m.editLabel = textinput.New()
75		m.editLabel.Placeholder = "label (任意便於辨識的名稱)"
76		m.editLabel.CharLimit = 64
77		m.editLabel.Width = 60
78		m.editURL = textinput.New()
79		m.editURL.Placeholder = "Discord webhook URL / https://ntfy.sh/<topic> / 任何 HTTP(S) 端點"
80		m.editURL.CharLimit = 512
81		m.editURL.Width = 60
82	
83		if deps.User != nil && deps.Store != nil {
84			ctx := context.Background()
85			p, err := deps.Store.Notify().GetPrefs(ctx, deps.User.ID)
86			if err != nil {
87				m.loadErr = err
88			}
89			m.prefs = p
90			ts, err := deps.Store.Notify().ListTargets(ctx, deps.User.ID)
91			if err != nil && m.loadErr == nil {
92				m.loadErr = err
93			}
94			m.targets = ts
95		}
96		return m
97	}
98	
99	func (m notifySettingsModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
100	
101	// rowCount returns the total navigable row count: 5 prefs + N targets + 1 add.
102	func (m notifySettingsModel) rowCount() int {
103		return notifPrefCount + len(m.targets) + 1
104	}
105	
106	// addRowIndex returns the cursor index of the "+ add target" row.
107	func (m notifySettingsModel) addRowIndex() int {
108		return notifPrefCount + len(m.targets)
109	}
110	
111	func (m notifySettingsModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
112		switch msg := msg.(type) {
113		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
114			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
115			m.editLabel.Width = max(20, msg.Width-16)
116			m.editURL.Width = max(20, msg.Width-16)
117			return m, nil
118	
119		case tea.KeyMsg:
120			if m.mode == modeEdit {
121				return m.updateEdit(msg)
122			}
123			return m.updateList(msg)
124	
125		case notifyTestResultMsg:
126			if msg.err != nil {
127				m.err = fmt.Sprintf("target #%d test failed: %v", msg.targetID, msg.err)
128				m.flash = ""
129			} else {
130				m.flash = fmt.Sprintf("✓ target #%d test delivered — check your receiver", msg.targetID)
131				m.err = ""
132			}
133			return m, nil
134		}
135		return m, nil
136	}
137	
138	func (m notifySettingsModel) updateList(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
139		switch msg.String() {
140		case "esc", "left", "h", "backspace":
141			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
142		case "Q":
143			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
144		case "up", "k":
145			if m.cursor > 0 {
146				m.cursor--
147			}
148			m.err = ""
149			return m, nil
150		case "down", "j":
151			if m.cursor < m.rowCount()-1 {
152				m.cursor++
153			}
154			m.err = ""
155			return m, nil
156		case " ":
157			if m.cursor < notifPrefCount {
158				m.flipPref(m.cursor)
159				m.prefsDirty = true
160				m.err = ""
161			} else if m.cursor == m.addRowIndex() {
162				m.startAdd()
163			} else {
164				// Space on a target → toggle enabled (alias of `t`).
165				m.toggleTarget()
166			}
167			return m, nil
168		case "ctrl+s":
169			return m.savePrefs()
170		case "a":
171			m.startAdd()
172			return m, nil
173		case "t":
174			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
175				m.toggleTarget()
176			}
177			return m, nil
178		case "e":
179			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
180				m.startEdit()
181			}
182			return m, nil
183		case "d":
184			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
185				m.deleteTarget()
186			}
187			return m, nil
188		case "T":
189			// Send a synthetic test notification to the cursored target.
190			// Bypasses prefs (so users can test before flipping any toggles)
191			// and bypasses the dispatch queue (so we get a synchronous
192			// pass/fail to surface in the flash row).
193			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
194				return m, m.startTest()
195			}
196			return m, nil
197		case "enter", "right", "l":
198			if m.cursor == m.addRowIndex() {
199				m.startAdd()
200				return m, nil
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	type articleViewModel struct {
16		deps    Deps
17		article *store.Article
18		pushes  []*store.Push
19		width   int
20		height  int
21		scroll  int
22		loadErr error
23	
24		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
25		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
26		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
27		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
28		rendered      string
29		renderedWidth int
30	
31		// push input state
32		pushing  bool
33		pushKind store.PushKind
34		pushIn   textinput.Model
35		err      string
36	
37		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
38		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
39		pendingDelete bool
40		pushCursor    int
41	
42		// pickingCommentsMode: showing the 1/2/3 picker overlay for the
43		// mod-only M shortcut (open / arrows-only / locked).
44		pickingCommentsMode bool
45	}
46	
47	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
48	// to the current viewport.
49	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
50		if m.article == nil {
51			m.rendered = ""
52			m.renderedWidth = 0
53			return
54		}
55		width := m.width - 4
56		if width < 40 {
57			width = glamourFallbackWidth
58		}
59		m.rendered = renderMarkdown(m.article.Body, width)
60		m.renderedWidth = width
61	}
62	
63	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
64		ctx := context.Background()
65		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
66		if err != nil {
67			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
68		}
69		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
70	
71		ti := textinput.New()
72		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
73		ti.CharLimit = 80
74		ti.Width = 60
75	
76		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
77		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
78		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
79		m.renderBody()
80		return m
81	}
82	
83	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
84	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
85	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
86	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
87		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
88			return false
89		}
90		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
91			return true
92		}
93		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
94	}
95	
96	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
97	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
98		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
99			return false
100		}
101		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
102			return true
103		}
104		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
105	}
106	
107	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
108	// Guests can read but not write.
109	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
110		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
111	}
112	
113	// canEditArticle mirrors canDeleteArticle: the author may edit their own
114	// post, mods and admins may edit anyone's.
115	func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
116		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
117			return false
118		}
119		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
120			return true
121		}
122		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
123	}
124	
125	// canSetCommentsMode gates the M shortcut. Mirrors board-view canPin —
126	// pinning and locking comments are both moderation actions that do NOT
127	// admit the article author. Mod+ only.
128	func (m articleViewModel) canSetCommentsMode() bool {
129		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
130	}
131	
132	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
133	
134	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
135		switch msg := msg.(type) {
136		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
137			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
138			// Only re-render when the width target actually changes — height
139			// changes don't affect glamour wrap.
140			newWidth := max(msg.Width-4, 40)
141			if newWidth != m.renderedWidth {
142				m.renderBody()
143			}
144			return m, nil
145	
146		case PushAddedMsg:
147			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
148				ctx := context.Background()
149				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
150				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
151				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
152					m.pushes = pushes
153				}
154				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
155					m.article = a
156				}
157			}
158	
159		case ArticleUpdatedMsg:
160			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
161				// Edit happened in another session — refetch the canonical
162				// title/body so we render the new text.
163				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
164					m.article = a
165					m.renderBody()
166					// Reset scroll if the new body is shorter than where we
167					// were parked.
168					if m.scroll > m.bodyLineCount() {
169						m.scroll = 0
170					}
171				}
172			}
173			return m, nil
174	
175		case ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg:
176			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
177				// Mode flip happened in another session — refetch so the badge
178				// and the +/-/= gates pick up the new value. No body re-render
179				// needed (only the header changes).
180				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
181					m.article = a
182				}
183			}
184			return m, nil
185	
186		case tea.KeyMsg:
187			if m.pushing {
188				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
189			}
190			if m.pendingDelete {
191				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
192			}
193			if m.pickingCommentsMode {
194				return m.updateCommentsModePicker(msg)
195			}
196			switch msg.String() {
197			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
198				boardID := int64(0)
199				if m.article != nil {
200					boardID = m.article.BoardID
201				}
202				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
203			case "Q":
204				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
205			case "up", "k":
206				if m.scroll > 0 {
207					m.scroll--
208				}
209			case "down", "j":
210				m.scroll++
211			case "pgup", "b":
212				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
213			case "pgdown", " ":
214				m.scroll += 10
215			case "g":
216				m.scroll = 0
217			case "G":
218				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
219			case "[":
220				if m.article == nil {
221					return m, nil
222				}
223				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
224				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
225					return m, nil
226				}
227				return m, func() tea.Msg {
228					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
229				}
230			case "]":
231				if m.article == nil {
232					return m, nil
233				}
234				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
235				if err != nil || next == 0 {
236					return m, nil
237				}
238				return m, func() tea.Msg {
239					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
240				}
241			case "+":
242				if !m.canPush() {
243					return m, nil
244				}
245				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
246			case "-":
247				if !m.canPush() {
248					return m, nil
249				}
250				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
251			case "=":
252				if !m.canPush() {
253					return m, nil
254				}
255				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
256			case "p":
257				// Advance the push cursor. -1 → 0 → ... → len-1 → -1 (cycles
258				// back to "no selection, D targets the article").
259				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
260					return m, nil
261				}
262				m.pushCursor++
263				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
264					m.pushCursor = -1
265				}
266				m.err = ""
267				return m, nil
268			case "P":
269				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
270					return m, nil
271				}
272				if m.pushCursor < 0 {
273					m.pushCursor = len(m.pushes) - 1
274				} else {
275					m.pushCursor--
276				}
277				m.err = ""
278				return m, nil
279			case "D":
280				if m.pushCursor >= 0 {
281					if !m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
282						m.err = "權限不足 (only the push author or a mod can delete it)"
283						return m, nil
284					}
285				} else {
286					if !m.canDeleteArticle() {
287						m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can delete)"
288						return m, nil
289					}
290				}
291				m.pendingDelete = true
292				m.err = ""
293				return m, nil
294			case "E":
295				if m.article == nil {
296					return m, nil
297				}
298				if !m.canEditArticle() {
299					m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can edit)"
300					return m, nil
301				}
302				id := m.article.ID
303				boardID := m.article.BoardID
304				return m, func() tea.Msg {
305					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleEdit, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
306				}
307			case "y":
308				if m.article == nil {
309					return m, nil
310				}
311				id := m.article.ID
312				boardID := m.article.BoardID
313				return m, func() tea.Msg {
314					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleExport, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
315				}
316			case "r":
317				// Reply — opens the post-compose screen with the parent
318				// pre-filled (Re: title + blockquote body), same flow as mail
319				// reply. Guests can't reply (gated by guestWriteBlocked in
320				// Root.navigate).
321				if m.article == nil {
322					return m, nil
323				}
324				id := m.article.ID
325				boardID := m.article.BoardID
326				return m, func() tea.Msg {
327					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
328				}
329			case "M":
330				// Open the comments-mode picker. Mod+ only — silent no-op
331				// otherwise so non-mods don't get a confusing toast for a key
332				// the help line doesn't even surface to them (mirrors M on
333				// the board view).
334				if m.article == nil || !m.canSetCommentsMode() {
335					return m, nil
336				}
337				m.pickingCommentsMode = true
338				m.err = ""
339				return m, nil
340			}
341		}
342		return m, nil
343	}
344	
345	// updateDeleteConfirm handles y/n while the confirm overlay is up.
346	// y: dispatch the delete; if targeting a push, refresh the push list
347	// and the article (for the score). If targeting the article, navigate
348	// back to the board view.
349	// n / esc: cancel.
350	func (m articleViewModel) updateDeleteConfirm(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
351		switch msg.String() {
352		case "y", "Y":
353			if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
354				m.pendingDelete = false
355				return m, nil
356			}
357			ctx := context.Background()
358			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
359				pushID := m.pushes[m.pushCursor].ID
360				if err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Delete(ctx, pushID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
361					m.err = err.Error()
362					m.pendingDelete = false
363					return m, nil
364				}
365				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its
366				// recommend_score reflects the reverted delta.
367				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
368					m.pushes = pushes
369				}
370				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
371					m.article = a
372				}
373				// Clamp the cursor so the next D / p continues from a valid index.
374				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
375					m.pushCursor = -1
376				}
377				m.pendingDelete = false
378				return m, nil
379			}
380			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Delete(ctx, m.article.ID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
381				m.err = err.Error()
382				m.pendingDelete = false
383				return m, nil
384			}
385			boardID := m.article.BoardID
386			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
387		case "n", "N", "esc":
388			m.pendingDelete = false
389			return m, nil
390		}
391		return m, nil
392	}
393	
394	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
395	// loaded article. Uses the glamour-rendered body so g/G/PgDn agree
396	// with what's actually on screen; falls back to raw if rendering hasn't
397	// run yet (shouldn't happen post-construction but defensive).
398	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
399		if m.rendered != "" {
400			return len(strings.Split(m.rendered, "\n"))
401		}
402		if m.article == nil {
403			return 0
404		}
405		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
406	}
407	
408	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
409	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
410	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
411		return max(m.height-16, 5)
412	}
413	
414	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
415		// Early gate based on the cached article state. The store-side check
416		// in PushRepo.Create is the authoritative gate (handles races between
417		// sessions), but rejecting here gives a friendlier error than the
418		// raw sentinel and avoids opening the input box at all.
419		if m.article != nil {
420			switch m.article.CommentsMode {
421			case store.CommentsModeLocked:
422				m.err = "本文已鎖定留言"
423				return m
424			case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
425				if k != store.PushKindArrow {
426					m.err = "本文僅開放箭頭留言"
427					return m
428				}
429			}
430		}
431		m.pushing = true
432		m.pushKind = k
433		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
434		m.pushIn.Focus()
435		m.err = ""
436		return m
437	}
438	
439	// updateCommentsModePicker handles 1/2/3/Esc while the comments-mode
440	// picker overlay is up. On a successful flip it refetches the article
441	// locally and broadcasts ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg so other live
442	// sessions update their badges and gates.
443	func (m articleViewModel) updateCommentsModePicker(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
444		if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
445			m.pickingCommentsMode = false
446			return m, nil
447		}
448		var mode store.CommentsMode
449		switch msg.String() {
450		case "1":
451			mode = store.CommentsModeOpen
452		case "2":
453			mode = store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly
454		case "3":
455			mode = store.CommentsModeLocked
456		case "esc", "n", "N":
457			m.pickingCommentsMode = false
458			return m, nil
459		default:
460			return m, nil
461		}
462		ctx := context.Background()
463		u := m.deps.User
464		if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetCommentsMode(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.Role, mode); err != nil {
465			m.err = err.Error()
466			m.pickingCommentsMode = false
467			return m, nil
468		}
469		if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
470			m.article = a
471		}
472		m.pickingCommentsMode = false
473		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
474			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg{
475				BoardID:   m.article.BoardID,
476				ArticleID: m.article.ID,
477				Mode:      string(mode),
478			})
479		}
480		return m, nil
481	}
482	
483	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
484		switch msg.String() {
485		case "esc":
486			m.pushing = false
487			m.pushIn.Blur()
488			return m, nil
489		case "enter":
490			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
491			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
492				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
493				return m, nil
494			}
495			// Late gate: re-check comments_mode in case the cached article
496			// is stale. Server-side will also reject (PushRepo.Create), but
497			// catching here surfaces a friendlier message than the sentinel.
498			if m.article != nil {
499				switch m.article.CommentsMode {
500				case store.CommentsModeLocked:
501					m.err = "本文已鎖定留言"
502					m.pushing = false
503					m.pushIn.Blur()
504					return m, nil
505				case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
506					if m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
507						m.err = "本文僅開放箭頭留言"
508						m.pushing = false
509						m.pushIn.Blur()
510						return m, nil
511					}
512				}
513			}
514			ctx := context.Background()
515			u := m.deps.User
516			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
517			if err != nil {
518				m.err = err.Error()
519				return m, nil
520			}
521			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
522			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
523			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
524				m.article = a
525			}
526			m.pushing = false
527			m.pushIn.Blur()
528			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
529			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
530				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
531					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
532					UserUserID: u.UserID,
533					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
534					Body:       body,
535				})
536			}
537			// Webhook notification to the article's author. Skip self-pushes
538			// — pushing on your own article doesn't need to wake your phone.
539			if m.deps.Notify != nil && m.article.AuthorID != u.ID {
540				m.deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
541					Kind:       notify.KindPush,
542					ToUserID:   m.article.AuthorID,
543					FromUserID: u.UserID,
544					Title:      fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章", u.UserID, kindLabel(m.pushKind)),
545					Body:       fmt.Sprintf("%s\n\n%s", Truncate(m.article.Title, 60), body),
546				})
547			}
548			return m, nil
549		}
550		var cmd tea.Cmd
551		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
552		return m, cmd
553	}
554	
555	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
556		if m.loadErr != nil {
557			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
558		}
559		if m.article == nil {
560			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
561		}
562		a := m.article
563	
564		var b strings.Builder
565		b.WriteString("\n")
566		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
567		b.WriteString("\n\n")
568	
569		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
570		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
571		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
572		switch a.CommentsMode {
573		case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
574			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("留言:  ") + StyleError.Render("[箭] 僅開放箭頭") + "\n")
575		case store.CommentsModeLocked:
576			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("留言:  ") + StyleError.Render("[鎖] 已關閉留言") + "\n")
577		}
578		b.WriteString("\n")
579	
580		body := m.rendered
581		if body == "" {
582			body = a.Body
583		}
584		bodyLines := strings.Split(body, "\n")
585		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
586		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
587		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
588		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
589			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
590		}
591		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
592			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
593			b.WriteString("\n")
594		}
595	
596		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
597			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
598			for i, p := range m.pushes {
599				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
600				gutter := "  "
601				if i == m.pushCursor {
602					gutter = "▸ "
603				}
604				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
605					gutter,
606					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
607					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
608					p.Body,
609					StyleDim.Render(ts),
610				)
611				if i == m.pushCursor {
612					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
613				}
614				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
615			}
616		}
617	
618		if m.pushing {
619			b.WriteString("\n  ")
620			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
621			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
622			b.WriteString("\n")
623			if m.err != "" {
624				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
625			}
626			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
627		} else if m.pendingDelete {
628			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
629			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
630				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
631			}
632			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
633		} else if m.pickingCommentsMode {
634			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHeader.Render(" 留言模式 "))
635			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("1 開放  2 僅箭頭  3 鎖文  Esc 取消"))
636		} else {
637			help := "j/k scroll · y 匯出"
638			if m.canPush() {
639				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = → · r 回文"
640			}
641			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
642				help += " · p/P 選推文"
643			}
644			if m.canEditArticle() {
645				help += " · E 編輯"
646			}
647			if m.canSetCommentsMode() {
648				help += " · M 留言模式"
649			}
650			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
651				help += " · D 刪除推文"
652			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
653				help += " · D 刪除文章"
654			}
655			help += " · ? help · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
656			if m.err != "" {
657				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
658			}
659			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
660		}
661		b.WriteString("\n")
662		return b.String()
663	}
664	
665	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
666		switch k {
667		case store.PushKindPush:
668			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
669		case store.PushKindBoo:
670			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
671		case store.PushKindArrow:
672			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
673		}
674		return "?"
675	}
676	
677	// kindLabel renders a short verb for push notifications. Mirrors the
678	// renderPushKind glyphs (推 / 噓 / →) but as plain text so it survives
679	// non-color webhooks (Discord embeds, plain SMS, etc.).
680	func kindLabel(k store.PushKind) string {
681		switch k {
682		case store.PushKindPush:
683			return "推"
684		case store.PushKindBoo:
685			return "噓"
686		case store.PushKindArrow:
687			return "→"
688		}
689		return "?"
690	}

295	// startTest fires a one-shot test notification at the cursored target.
296	// Returns a tea.Cmd that runs the HTTP call in a goroutine (the
297	// dispatcher's http.Client has a 5s timeout) and rendezvous-es the
298	// result back as a notifyTestResultMsg.
299	func (m *notifySettingsModel) startTest() tea.Cmd {
300		idx := m.cursor - notifPrefCount
301		if idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.targets) {
302			return nil
303		}
304		t := m.targets[idx]
305		if m.deps.Notify == nil {
306			// Tests / register-only sessions: no dispatcher wired up. Fail
307			// loud rather than silently no-op.
308			m.err = "notify dispatcher not initialised"
309			return nil
310		}
311		m.flash = fmt.Sprintf("🔔 testing target #%d…", t.ID)
312		m.err = ""
313		// Snapshot the values the goroutine needs so we don't reach back into
314		// the model after returning (model updates are funnelled through
315		// Update by bubbletea's main loop).
316		target := *t
317		user := ""
318		if m.deps.User != nil {
319			user = m.deps.User.UserID
320		}
321		dispatcher := m.deps.Notify
322		return func() tea.Msg {
323			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 8*time.Second)
324			defer cancel()
325			ev := notify.Event{
326				Kind:       notify.KindPush, // bypassed by SendTest; here just for log readability
327				ToUserID:   target.UserID,
328				FromUserID: user,
329				Title:      "[BBS-test] notification target verified",
330				Body: fmt.Sprintf(
331					"User: %s\nTarget: #%d (%s)\nTime: %s\n\nIf you can read this, the webhook URL is wired up correctly.",
332					user, target.ID, target.Label, time.Now().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"),
333				),
334			}
335			return notifyTestResultMsg{
336				targetID: target.ID,
337				err:      dispatcher.SendTest(ctx, &target, ev),
338			}
339		}
340	}
