```text
28	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
29		ctx := context.Background()
30		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
31		if err != nil {
32			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
33		}
34		ti := textinput.New()
35		ti.Placeholder = "搜尋標題 / search title"
36		ti.CharLimit = 64
37		ti.Width = 50
38		m := boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, search: ti}
39		return m.reload(0)
40	}
41	
42	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
43	
44	// reload re-fetches articles using the current m.filter and m.sort and
45	// returns the updated model. anchorID > 0 keeps the cursor on the same
46	// article ID across the reflow; anchorID == 0 leaves the cursor at its
47	// numeric index (clamped to the new length). DB errors after the initial
48	// load are silent so a transient broadcast-driven refresh doesn't blank
49	// the screen — matches the pre-refactor behaviour of the inline reloads.
50	func (m boardViewModel) reload(anchorID int64) boardViewModel {
51		if m.board == nil {
52			return m
53		}
54		ctx := context.Background()
55		arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoardOpts(ctx, m.board.ID,
56			store.ListArticlesOpts{Limit: 100, TitleSearch: m.filter, Sort: m.sort})
57		if err != nil {
58			if m.loadErr == nil {
59				m.loadErr = err
60			}
61			return m
62		}
63		m.articles = arts
64		if anchorID > 0 {
65			m.cursor = findCursorByID(arts, anchorID)
66		}
67		if m.cursor >= len(arts) {
68			m.cursor = max(0, len(arts)-1)
69		}
70		return m
71	}
72	
73	// currentAnchorID returns the cursored article's ID, or 0 if the list is
74	// empty. Used to preserve the highlight across reloads triggered by sort
75	// toggles or pin/mode broadcasts.
76	func (m boardViewModel) currentAnchorID() int64 {
77		if m.cursor < len(m.articles) {
78			return m.articles[m.cursor].ID
79		}
80		return 0
81	}
82	
83	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
84		switch msg := msg.(type) {
85		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
86			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
87			m.search.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
88			return m, nil
89		case ArticleAddedMsg:
90			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
91				// No anchor — preserve cursor index. Matches pre-refactor
92				// behaviour: a freshly added article shifts the cursor to
93				// what was previously one row below it (in newest-first sort).
94				m = m.reload(0)
95			}
96			return m, nil
97		case ArticlePinChangedMsg:
98			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
99				// Pin re-orders the list — re-anchor the cursor by article ID
100				// so the same article stays highlighted across the reflow.
101				m = m.reload(m.currentAnchorID())
102			}
103			return m, nil
104		case ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg:
105			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
106				m = m.reload(m.currentAnchorID())
107			}
108			return m, nil
109		case tea.KeyMsg:
110			// While the search input is focused every printable key types into
111			// it; only enter (confirm), esc (cancel), and ctrl+c (handled at
112			// Root) escape that. Action keys (s/M/p/b/B/Q) and vim navigation
113			// (h/j/k/l/g/G/[/]) all become text characters during search.
114			if m.searchActive {
115				switch msg.String() {
116				case "enter":
117					m.filter = strings.TrimSpace(m.search.Value())
118					m.searchActive = false
119					m.search.Blur()
120					m.cursor = 0
121					m = m.reload(0)
122					return m, nil
123				case "esc":
124					m.search.SetValue("")
125					m.filter = ""
126					m.searchActive = false
127					m.search.Blur()
128					m.cursor = 0
129					m = m.reload(0)
130					return m, nil
131				}
132				var cmd tea.Cmd
133				m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
134				return m, cmd
135			}
136	
137			switch msg.String() {
138			case "/":
139				m.searchActive = true
140				m.search.SetValue(m.filter)
141				m.search.CursorEnd()
142				m.search.Focus()
143				return m, textinput.Blink
144			case "esc":
145				if m.filter != "" {
146					m.filter = ""
147					m.search.SetValue("")
148					m.cursor = 0
149					m = m.reload(0)
150					return m, nil
151				}
152				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
153			case "backspace", "left", "h":
154				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
155			case "Q":
156				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
157			case "s":
158				// Cycle sort. Anchor on the cursored article so the highlight
159				// follows the row across the reorder. NOTE(score-vs-pushcount):
160				// the user-facing label says 推文量 (push amount); under the hood
161				// we sort by recommend_score (signed +1/-1/0 sum). Acceptable
162				// proxy for now; if the product distinction matters later, add
163				// a maintained push_count column.
164				m.sort = (m.sort + 1) % 2
165				m = m.reload(m.currentAnchorID())
166				return m, nil
167			case "up", "k", "[":
168				if m.cursor > 0 {
169					m.cursor--
170				}
171			case "down", "j", "]":
172				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
173					m.cursor++
174				}
175			case "home", "g":
176				m.cursor = 0
177			case "end", "G":
178				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
179					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
180				}
181			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
182				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
183					return m, nil
184				}
185				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
186				return m, func() tea.Msg {
187					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
188				}
189			case "p":
190				if m.board == nil {
191					return m, nil
192				}
193				return m, func() tea.Msg {
194					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
195				}
196			case "b":
197				if m.board == nil || m.board.Banner == "" {
198					return m, nil
199				}
200				return m, func() tea.Msg {
201					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardSplash, BoardID: m.board.ID}
202				}
203			case "B":
204				if m.board == nil || !m.canEditBanner() {
205					return m, nil
206				}
207				return m, func() tea.Msg {
208					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardBannerEdit, BoardID: m.board.ID}
209				}
210			case "M":
211				// Toggle pin on the cursor article. Mod+ only — silent no-op
212				// otherwise so non-mods don't get a confusing error toast for
213				// trying a key the help line doesn't even mention to them.
214				if m.board == nil || !m.canPin() || len(m.articles) == 0 {
215					return m, nil
216				}
217				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
218				pinned := !a.PinnedAt.Valid
219				ctx := context.Background()
220				u := m.deps.User
221				if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetPinned(ctx, a.ID, u.ID, u.Role, pinned); err != nil {
222					return m, nil
223				}
224				m = m.reload(a.ID)
225				if m.deps.Broker != nil {
226					m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticlePinChangedMsg{
227						BoardID: m.board.ID, ArticleID: a.ID, Pinned: pinned,
228					})
229				}
230				return m, nil
231			}
232		}
233		return m, nil
234	}
235	
236	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
237		var b strings.Builder
238		b.WriteString("\n")
239		if m.board != nil {
240			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
241		} else {
242			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
243		}
244		b.WriteString("\n\n")
245		b.WriteString(m.renderBanner())
246	
247		if m.loadErr != nil {
248			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
249			return b.String()
250		}
251	
252		// Search row + sort indicator. Search runs above the article table so the
253		// table's row layout stays predictable across show/hide.
254		switch {
255		case m.searchActive:
256			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("搜尋 / 標題: ") + m.search.View() + "\n")
257			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(Enter 套用 · Esc 取消)") + "\n\n")
258		case m.filter != "":
259			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[搜尋: %s · %d 筆 · / 修改 · Esc 清除]", m.filter, len(m.articles))) + "\n\n")
260		}
261	
262		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
263			hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
264			help := "p post · / search · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
265			if m.filter != "" {
266				hint = "(沒有符合的文章)"
267			}
268			if m.isGuest() {
269				hint = "(no articles yet)"
270				help = "/ search · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
271			}
272			help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
273			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
274			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
275			return b.String()
276		}
277	
278		const (
279			idxW    = 4
280			dateW   = 11
281			scoreW  = 4
282			authorW = 14
283		)
284		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
285		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
286			PadRight("#", idxW),
287			PadRight("Date", dateW),
288			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
289			PadRight("Author", authorW),
290			PadRight("Title", titleW),
291		)
292		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header))
293		if m.sort == store.SortByScoreDesc {
294			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("[排序: 推文量↓]"))
295		}
296		b.WriteString("\n")
297	
298		for i, a := range m.articles {
299			score := ""
300			switch {
301			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
302				score = "爆"
303			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
304				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
305			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
306				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
307			}
308			// Pinned (板規 / 置頂) articles surface a leading "[M] " marker —
309			// the same glyph PTT uses for M-marked posts. Locked / arrows-only
310			// articles stack additional [鎖] / [箭] badges so users see the
311			// constraint before opening the article. Long titles still get
312			// truncated by Truncate.
313			title := a.Title
314			var prefix string
315			if a.PinnedAt.Valid {
316				prefix += "[M]"
317			}
318			switch a.CommentsMode {
319			case store.CommentsModeLocked:
320				prefix += "[鎖]"
321			case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
322				prefix += "[箭]"
323			}
324			if prefix != "" {
325				title = prefix + " " + a.Title
326			}
327			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
328				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
329				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
330				PadRight(score, scoreW),
331				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
332				Truncate(title, titleW),
333			)
334			if i == m.cursor {
335				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
336			} else {
337				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
338			}
339		}
340	
341		help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · / search · s sort · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
342		if m.isGuest() {
343			help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · / search · s sort · ? help · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
344		}
345		help = m.appendBannerHelp(help)
346		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
347		b.WriteString("\n")
348		return b.String()
349	}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/pushes.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// ErrPushNotFound is returned by PushRepo lookups (Delete, future GetByID)
12	// when the row is missing. ErrPermissionDenied is shared with articles.
13	var ErrPushNotFound = errors.New("push not found")
14	
15	// ErrCommentsLocked / ErrCommentsArrowsOnly are returned by PushRepo.Create
16	// when the parent article's comments_mode rejects the requested kind. The
17	// store-side check is the authoritative gate (TUI also gates early for nicer
18	// errors, but multiple sessions can race against a mode change so the store
19	// is the source of truth). See ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.
20	var (
21		ErrCommentsLocked     = errors.New("comments are locked on this article")
22		ErrCommentsArrowsOnly = errors.New("only arrow comments are allowed on this article")
23	)
24	
25	type PushKind string
26	
27	const (
28		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
29		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
30		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
31	)
32	
33	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
34		switch k {
35		case PushKindPush:
36			return "推"
37		case PushKindBoo:
38			return "噓"
39		case PushKindArrow:
40			return "→"
41		}
42		return "?"
43	}
44	
45	type Push struct {
46		ID          int64
47		ArticleID   int64
48		UserID      int64
49		UserUserID  string
50		Kind        PushKind
51		Body        string
52		CreatedAt   time.Time
53	}
54	
55	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
56	
57	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
58	
59	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
60		p := &Push{}
61		var kind string
62		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
63			return nil, err
64		}
65		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
66		return p, nil
67	}
68	
69	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
70		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
71			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
72		)
73		if err != nil {
74			return nil, err
75		}
76		defer rows.Close()
77		var out []*Push
78		for rows.Next() {
79			p, err := scanPush(rows)
80			if err != nil {
81				return nil, err
82			}
83			out = append(out, p)
84		}
85		return out, rows.Err()
86	}
87	
88	// scoreDelta returns the recommend_score contribution of a push of the
89	// given kind. Positive on add; negate on delete. Centralized so Create
90	// and Delete agree on the algebra.
91	func scoreDelta(kind PushKind) (int, error) {
92		switch kind {
93		case PushKindPush:
94			return 1, nil
95		case PushKindBoo:
96			return -1, nil
97		case PushKindArrow:
98			return 0, nil
99		}
100		return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
101	}
102	
103	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the
104	// article (push: +1, boo: -1, arrow: 0). Both writes happen under the
105	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
106	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
107	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
108		delta, err := scoreDelta(kind)
109		if err != nil {
110			return nil, err
111		}
112	
113		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
114		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
115		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, err
118		}
119		defer tx.Rollback()
120	
121		// Gate on the parent article's comments_mode inside the same tx; with
122		// writeMu held, the SELECT sees the latest committed value. This is the
123		// authoritative check — TUI gates early for nicer errors but races
124		// between sessions are resolved here.
125		var modeStr string
126		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
127			`SELECT comments_mode FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
128		).Scan(&modeStr)
129		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
130			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
131		}
132		if err != nil {
133			return nil, err
134		}
135		switch CommentsMode(modeStr) {
136		case CommentsModeLocked:
137			return nil, ErrCommentsLocked
138		case CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
139			if kind != PushKindArrow {
140				return nil, ErrCommentsArrowsOnly
141			}
142		}
143	
144		res, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
145			`INSERT INTO pushes (article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
146			articleID, userID, userUserID, string(kind), body,
147		)
148		if err != nil {
149			return nil, err
150		}
151		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
152		if err != nil {
153			return nil, err
154		}
155		if delta != 0 {
156			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
157				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`,
158				delta, articleID,
159			); err != nil {
160				return nil, err
161			}
162		}
163		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
164			return nil, err
165		}
166	
167		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, id)
168		return scanPush(row)
169	}
170	
171	// Delete hard-deletes a push and reverts its score contribution on the
172	// parent article. Permission: requester must be the push author OR have
173	// a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrPermissionDenied / ErrPushNotFound.
174	// Mirrors Create's writeMu+tx discipline so concurrent deletes can't
175	// double-revert the score.
176	func (r *PushRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, pushID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
177		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
178		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
179		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
180		if err != nil {
181			return err
182		}
183		defer tx.Rollback()
184	
185		var authorID, articleID int64
186		var kindStr string
187		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
188			`SELECT user_id, article_id, kind FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID,
189		).Scan(&authorID, &articleID, &kindStr)
190		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
191			return ErrPushNotFound
192		}
193		if err != nil {
194			return err
195		}
196		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
197			return ErrPermissionDenied
198		}
199		delta, err := scoreDelta(PushKind(kindStr))
200		if err != nil {
201			return err
202		}
203		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID); err != nil {
204			return err
205		}
206		if delta != 0 {
207			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
208				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score - ? WHERE id = ?`,
209				delta, articleID,
210			); err != nil {
211				return err
212			}
213		}
214		return tx.Commit()
215	}
216
```

 `./internal/markdown/markdown.go`

```text
1	// Package markdown is the round-trip serializer/parser for articles.
2	//
3	// An article (with optional pushes) renders to a self-describing
4	// human-readable document with YAML-ish frontmatter:
5	//
6	//	---
7	//	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
8	//	board: Welcome
9	//	author: admin
10	//	created_at: 2026-04-30T12:34:56Z
11	//	updated_at: 2026-04-30T13:00:00Z
12	//	score: 0
13	//	id: 1
14	//	---
15	//
16	//	[arbitrary body — markdown or plain text]
17	//
18	//	<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->
19	//	- 推 [alice] 2026-04-30T12:35:01Z  great post
20	//	- 噓 [bob]   2026-04-30T12:35:14Z  disagree
21	//	- → [carol]  2026-04-30T12:35:20Z  fyi
22	//
23	// The format is intentionally narrow: each frontmatter line is
24	// `key: value`, no nesting, no quoting. Push lines are
25	// `- <glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>` with a TWO-space separator
26	// between timestamp and body.
27	//
28	// The package is a leaf — pure functions over store types, no DB access.
29	package markdown
30	
31	import (
32		"fmt"
33		"strconv"
34		"strings"
35		"time"
36	
37		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
38	)
39	
40	const (
41		frontmatterDelim = "---"
42	
43		// PushesSentinel marks the start of the pushes block. Public so callers
44		// (UI, tests, docs) can warn users not to embed this exact string in
45		// their article body. If a body contains the sentinel, Format will
46		// suffix it with the literal-marker so Parse can restore the original.
47		PushesSentinel = "<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->"
48	
49		// pushesLiteralMarker is appended to a sentinel that legitimately
50		// appears inside the body. Parse strips it back out.
51		pushesLiteralMarker = "<!-- sshbbs:literal -->"
52	)
53	
54	// FormatOpts controls the output of Format.
55	type FormatOpts struct {
56		// IncludePushes appends the pushes section after the body.
57		IncludePushes bool
58		// BoardName is rendered into the frontmatter. Pass the board name
59		// (the package can't look it up — store types don't carry it).
60		BoardName string
61	}
62	
63	// Parsed is the round-trip target. It deliberately does NOT return a
64	// *store.Article because Parse runs without DB context: the caller must
65	// resolve BoardName → BoardID and AuthorName → AuthorID before INSERT.
66	type Parsed struct {
67		Title      string
68		BoardName  string
69		AuthorName string
70		CreatedAt  time.Time
71		UpdatedAt  time.Time
72		ScoreHint  int64
73		IDHint     int64
74		Pinned     bool
75		Body       string
76		Pushes     []ParsedPush
77		// Extra preserves any frontmatter key we don't recognize, so future
78		// schema additions don't drop data on a round-trip through an older
79		// build.
80		Extra map[string]string
81	}
82	
83	// ParsedPush mirrors store.Push but with author as a name (not user_id) and
84	// without a DB-side ID; the caller decides whether/how to materialize it.
85	type ParsedPush struct {
86		Kind      store.PushKind
87		Author    string
88		CreatedAt time.Time
89		Body      string
90	}
91	
92	// Format renders an article (plus optional pushes) to the canonical text
93	// form. Returns an error only on a nil article; otherwise it always
94	// produces a valid, parseable document.
95	func Format(a *store.Article, pushes []*store.Push, opts FormatOpts) (string, error) {
96		if a == nil {
97			return "", fmt.Errorf("markdown.Format: article is nil")
98		}
99		var b strings.Builder
100		b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n")
101		writeFrontmatter(&b, "title", sanitizeFM(a.Title))
102		if opts.BoardName != "" {
103			writeFrontmatter(&b, "board", sanitizeFM(opts.BoardName))
104		}
105		if a.AuthorUserID != "" {
106			writeFrontmatter(&b, "author", sanitizeFM(a.AuthorUserID))
107		}
108		if !a.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
109			writeFrontmatter(&b, "created_at", a.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
110		}
111		if a.UpdatedAt.Valid {
112			writeFrontmatter(&b, "updated_at", a.UpdatedAt.Time.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
113		}
114		writeFrontmatter(&b, "score", strconv.FormatInt(a.RecommendScore, 10))
115		if a.ID > 0 {
116			writeFrontmatter(&b, "id", strconv.FormatInt(a.ID, 10))
117		}
118		if a.PinnedAt.Valid {
119			writeFrontmatter(&b, "pinned", "true")
120		}
121		b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n\n")
122	
123		body := a.Body
124		// Disambiguate any literal sentinel inside the body so Parse won't
125		// mis-cleave at it. Best-effort: if the user already has the
126		// literal-marker after the sentinel, we leave it alone.
127		if strings.Contains(body, PushesSentinel) {
128			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00")
129			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
130			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00", PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
131		}
132		b.WriteString(body)
133		if !strings.HasSuffix(body, "\n") {
134			b.WriteString("\n")
135		}
136	
137		if opts.IncludePushes && len(pushes) > 0 {
138			b.WriteString("\n" + PushesSentinel + "\n")
139			for _, p := range pushes {
140				ts := p.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
141				// Push body is single-line by DB convention; flatten just in case.
142				pb := strings.ReplaceAll(p.Body, "\n", " ")
143				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s [%s] %s  %s\n", p.Kind.Glyph(), p.UserUserID, ts, pb)
144			}
145		}
146	
147		return b.String(), nil
148	}
149	
150	// Parse reads markdown back into a Parsed. Permissive: missing frontmatter
151	// or pushes section degrade to zero values; the whole input becomes Body
152	// when no frontmatter is present.
153	func Parse(md string) (*Parsed, error) {
154		p := &Parsed{Extra: map[string]string{}}
155		rest := md
156	
157		if strings.HasPrefix(rest, frontmatterDelim+"\n") {
158			rest = rest[len(frontmatterDelim)+1:]
159			closeIdx := strings.Index(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n")
160			closeAtEOF := false
161			if closeIdx < 0 && strings.HasSuffix(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim) {
162				closeIdx = len(rest) - len("\n"+frontmatterDelim)
163				closeAtEOF = true
164			}
165			if closeIdx >= 0 {
166				fm := rest[:closeIdx]
167				if closeAtEOF {
168					rest = ""
169				} else {
170					rest = rest[closeIdx+len("\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n"):]
171				}
172				parseFrontmatter(p, fm)
173			}
174		}
175	
176		rest = strings.TrimLeft(rest, "\n")
177		if strings.Contains(rest, PushesSentinel) {
178			// The sentinel may be followed by literal-marker, which means it
179			// belongs to the body. Walk past every literal-marked occurrence.
180			head := rest
181			offset := 0
182			for {
183				i := strings.Index(head, PushesSentinel)
184				if i < 0 {
185					// Fell through without a real sentinel — entire rest is body.
186					p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
187					return p, nil
188				}
189				afterSentinel := i + len(PushesSentinel)
190				if strings.HasPrefix(head[afterSentinel:], pushesLiteralMarker) {
191					// Skip — it's part of the body. Continue searching.
192					next := afterSentinel + len(pushesLiteralMarker)
193					offset += next
194					head = head[next:]
195					continue
196				}
197				// Real sentinel — split.
198				cut := offset + i
199				body := strings.TrimRight(rest[:cut], "\n")
200				p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(body)
201				parsePushes(p, rest[cut+len(PushesSentinel):])
202				return p, nil
203			}
204		}
205		p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
206		return p, nil
207	}
208	
209	func writeFrontmatter(b *strings.Builder, key, val string) {
210		b.WriteString(key)
211		b.WriteString(": ")
212		b.WriteString(val)
213		b.WriteString("\n")
214	}
215	
216	func sanitizeFM(s string) string {
217		// Frontmatter values are single-line. CR/LF would break the parser;
218		// flatten to space.
219		s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\r", " ")
220		s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\n", " ")
221		return s
222	}
223	
224	func parseFrontmatter(p *Parsed, fm string) {
225		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(fm, "\n") {
226			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
227			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
228				continue
229			}
230			idx := strings.IndexByte(line, ':')
231			if idx <= 0 {
232				continue
233			}
234			key := strings.TrimSpace(line[:idx])
235			val := strings.TrimSpace(line[idx+1:])
236			switch key {
237			case "title":
238				p.Title = val
239			case "board":
240				p.BoardName = val
241			case "author":
242				p.AuthorName = val
243			case "created_at":
244				if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
245					p.CreatedAt = t
246				}
247			case "updated_at":
248				if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
249					p.UpdatedAt = t
250				}
251			case "score":
252				if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
253					p.ScoreHint = n
254				}
255			case "id":
256				if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
257					p.IDHint = n
258				}
259			case "pinned":
260				p.Pinned = val == "true" || val == "1"
261			default:
262				p.Extra[key] = val
263			}
264		}
265	}
266	
267	func parsePushes(p *Parsed, block string) {
268		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(block, "\n") {
269			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
270			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
271			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "- ") {
272				continue
273			}
274			if push, ok := parsePushLine(line[2:]); ok {
275				p.Pushes = append(p.Pushes, push)
276			}
277		}
278	}
279	
280	func parsePushLine(s string) (ParsedPush, bool) {
281		// Format: "<glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>"
282		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
283		if s == "" {
284			return ParsedPush{}, false
285		}
286	
287		// Glyph: take everything before the first whitespace.
288		wsIdx := strings.IndexAny(s, " \t")
289		if wsIdx <= 0 {
290			return ParsedPush{}, false
291		}
292		glyph := s[:wsIdx]
293		rest := strings.TrimLeft(s[wsIdx:], " \t")
294	
295		var kind store.PushKind
296		switch glyph {
297		case "推":
298			kind = store.PushKindPush
299		case "噓":
300			kind = store.PushKindBoo
301		case "→":
302			kind = store.PushKindArrow
303		default:
304			return ParsedPush{}, false
305		}
306	
307		if !strings.HasPrefix(rest, "[") {
308			return ParsedPush{}, false
309		}
310		closeBracket := strings.IndexByte(rest, ']')
311		if closeBracket <= 1 {
312			return ParsedPush{}, false
313		}
314		author := rest[1:closeBracket]
315		rest = strings.TrimLeft(rest[closeBracket+1:], " \t")
316	
317		// Timestamp + body separated by 2 spaces. If absent, fall back to one
318		// space (lossy but tolerant of hand-edited files).
319		var tsStr, body string
320		if sepIdx := strings.Index(rest, "  "); sepIdx >= 0 {
321			tsStr = rest[:sepIdx]
322			body = strings.TrimLeft(rest[sepIdx:], " \t")
323		} else if spaceIdx := strings.IndexAny(rest, " \t"); spaceIdx >= 0 {
324			tsStr = rest[:spaceIdx]
325			body = strings.TrimLeft(rest[spaceIdx:], " \t")
326		} else {
327			tsStr = rest
328			body = ""
329		}
330	
331		var ts time.Time
332		if tsStr != "" {
333			if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, tsStr); err == nil {
334				ts = t
335			}
336		}
337		return ParsedPush{Kind: kind, Author: author, CreatedAt: ts, Body: body}, true
338	}
339	
340	func stripLiteralMarkers(s string) string {
341		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker, PushesSentinel)
342	}
343
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"regexp"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode/utf8"
7	
8		"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
9	)
10	
11	// PadRight pads s with spaces on the right so its display width equals w.
12	// If s is wider than w, it's truncated. Critical for table-like rendering
13	// with mixed ASCII + CJK strings — lipgloss's automatic padding miscounts
14	// double-width glyphs in some terminals.
15	func PadRight(s string, w int) string {
16		if w <= 0 {
17			return ""
18		}
19		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
20		if cw == w {
21			return s
22		}
23		if cw > w {
24			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
25		}
26		return s + strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw)
27	}
28	
29	// PadLeft is PadRight's mirror, padding before the string.
30	func PadLeft(s string, w int) string {
31		if w <= 0 {
32			return ""
33		}
34		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
35		if cw == w {
36			return s
37		}
38		if cw > w {
39			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
40		}
41		return strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw) + s
42	}
43	
44	// Truncate cuts s to display-width w, appending "…" if truncation occurred.
45	func Truncate(s string, w int) string {
46		if runewidth.StringWidth(s) <= w {
47			return s
48		}
49		if w <= 1 {
50			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
51		}
52		return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "…")
53	}
54	
55	// Width returns the display width of s.
56	func Width(s string) int { return runewidth.StringWidth(s) }
57	
58	// ansiCSIRe matches a CSI escape sequence: ESC '[' optional-params letter.
59	// SGR (color), cursor moves, and most other terminal control codes used by
60	// banners follow this shape. Other escape kinds (OSC, DCS) are left
61	// untouched — banners almost never carry them.
62	var ansiCSIRe = regexp.MustCompile("\x1b\\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]")
63	
64	// stripCSI returns s with CSI escape sequences removed. Use it for any
65	// width / column calculation that involves banner content (or any other
66	// string that may carry inline color codes). The four helpers above
67	// intentionally do NOT call this — they're the article-list table path
68	// where input is plain text, and stripping every line would just be
69	// overhead.
70	func stripCSI(s string) string {
71		return ansiCSIRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
72	}
73	
74	// bannerVisualWidth returns the display width of s with CSI escapes
75	// excluded from the count.
76	func bannerVisualWidth(s string) int {
77		return runewidth.StringWidth(stripCSI(s))
78	}
79	
80	// bannerTruncate clips s to display-width w, preserving inline CSI runs
81	// (color codes are zero-width). On overflow the result ends with "…"
82	// (which counts toward the budget). If the input contained any CSI, the
83	// result is suffixed with `\x1b[0m` so a forgotten reset doesn't bleed
84	// into subsequent terminal output.
85	func bannerTruncate(s string, w int) string {
86		if w <= 0 {
87			return ""
88		}
89		if bannerVisualWidth(s) <= w {
90			return ensureANSIReset(s)
91		}
92		budget := w - 1 // reserve one cell for "…"
93		var out strings.Builder
94		visible := 0
95		cursor := 0
96		for _, loc := range ansiCSIRe.FindAllStringIndex(s, -1) {
97			if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:loc[0]], &visible, budget) {
98				out.WriteString("…")
99				return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
100			}
101			out.WriteString(s[loc[0]:loc[1]])
102			cursor = loc[1]
103		}
104		if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:], &visible, budget) {
105			out.WriteString("…")
106			return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
107		}
108		out.WriteString("…")
109		return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
110	}
111	
112	func writeBodyUpToBudget(out *strings.Builder, body string, visible *int, budget int) bool {
113		for i := 0; i < len(body); {
114			r, size := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(body[i:])
115			rw := runewidth.RuneWidth(r)
116			if *visible+rw > budget {
117				return false
118			}
119			out.WriteRune(r)
120			*visible += rw
121			i += size
122		}
123		return true
124	}
125	
126	func ensureANSIReset(s string) string {
127		if strings.Contains(s, "\x1b[") && !strings.HasSuffix(s, "\x1b[0m") {
128			return s + "\x1b[0m"
129		}
130		return s
131	}
132
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
11	)
12	
13	// bioEditModel is a single-textarea form screen reached from the user
14	// settings menu. Form-screen convention (CLAUDE.md): no h/l navigation —
15	// those keys must remain available for text editing.
16	type bioEditModel struct {
17		deps    Deps
18		body    textarea.Model
19		width   int
20		height  int
21		err     string
22		success bool
23	}
24	
25	func newBioEditModel(deps Deps) bioEditModel {
26		ta := textarea.New()
27		ta.Placeholder = "你的 bio…（最多 1024 字元，可換行）"
28		ta.CharLimit = 4096 // generous client-side; auth.ValidateBio is the source of truth (rune count)
29		ta.SetWidth(72)
30		ta.SetHeight(10)
31		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Bio != "" {
32			ta.SetValue(deps.User.Bio)
33		}
34		ta.Focus()
35		return bioEditModel{deps: deps, body: ta}
36	}
37	
38	func (m bioEditModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textarea.Blink }
39	
40	func (m bioEditModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
41		switch msg := msg.(type) {
42		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
43			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
44			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-8))
45			m.body.SetHeight(max(8, msg.Height-12))
46			return m, nil
47	
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			if m.success {
50				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
51			}
52			switch msg.String() {
53			case "esc":
54				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
55			case "ctrl+s":
56				return m.submit()
57			}
58		}
59		var cmd tea.Cmd
60		m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
61		return m, cmd
62	}
63	
64	func (m bioEditModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
65		bio := strings.TrimRight(m.body.Value(), "\n")
66		if err := auth.ValidateBio(bio); err != nil {
67			m.err = err.Error()
68			return m, nil
69		}
70		if m.deps.User == nil {
71			m.err = "internal error: no user"
72			return m, nil
73		}
74		ctx := context.Background()
75		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().SetBio(ctx, m.deps.User.ID, bio); err != nil {
76			m.err = err.Error()
77			return m, nil
78		}
79		// Refresh User in place so the settings screen header and any future
80		// reads see the canonical bio without a re-login.
81		if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
82			*m.deps.User = *fresh
83		}
84		m.success = true
85		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
86	}
87	
88	func (m bioEditModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 修改 Bio Edit bio "))
92		b.WriteString("\n\n")
93	
94		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio (free-form, ≤ 1024 chars; newlines OK):") + "\n")
95		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
96	
97		if m.err != "" {
98			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
99		}
100		if m.success {
101			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ saved"))
102		}
103		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel"))
104		b.WriteString("\n")
105		return b.String()
106	}
107
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5	
6		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
7	)
8	
9	// notifyWB fires a KindWB event for a delivered water balloon. Skipped when
10	// the dispatcher is nil (tests / register-only sessions). Self-WB is
11	// expected to be filtered at the call site (see CLAUDE.md: self-WB is a
12	// memo and intentionally bypasses Broker.Send).
13	func notifyWB(deps Deps, toUserID int64, fromUserID, body string) {
14		if deps.Notify == nil {
15			return
16		}
17		deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
18			Kind:       notify.KindWB,
19			ToUserID:   toUserID,
20			FromUserID: fromUserID,
21			Title:      fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s 丟了水球給你", fromUserID),
22			Body:       body,
23		})
24	}
25	
26	// notifyMail fires a KindMail event for delivered mail. Self-mail is
27	// expected to be filtered at the call site.
28	func notifyMail(deps Deps, toUserID int64, fromUserID, subject, body string) {
29		if deps.Notify == nil {
30			return
31		}
32		// Cap the body excerpt — mail body can be 4 KB and most webhook
33		// receivers truncate aggressively anyway.
34		excerpt := body
35		if len(excerpt) > 280 {
36			excerpt = excerpt[:280] + "…"
37		}
38		deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
39			Kind:       notify.KindMail,
40			ToUserID:   toUserID,
41			FromUserID: fromUserID,
42			Title:      fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s 寄信給你: %s", fromUserID, subject),
43			Body:       excerpt,
44		})
45	}
46
```

## i18n Scaffolding Report

### 1. **Users Table Schema & Struct** (internal/store/users.go:10–25, 0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql)

**User struct fields:**
- ID, UserID, PasswordHash, Nickname, RealName, Email
- NumLogins, NumPosts, LastLoginAt, LastHost, CreatedAt
- Role, MustChangePassword, **Bio** (string)

**Migration pattern:** The most recent is `0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`. Bio was added via `ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';` at that migration's top level. The pattern is: one-off `ALTER TABLE` + `CREATE TABLE` for related entities (here: `user_notif_prefs` + `user_notif_targets`).

**Next migration would be:** `0010_add_user_locale.sql` (integers increment: 0001, 0002, ..., 0009 in use; next is **0010**).

**Column addition pattern:** For a `locale` TEXT column, use:
```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW';
```
Then update `userColumns` const (users.go:34–35) and `scanUser()` (line 40–46) to include the new field.

---

### 2. **Notification Prefs Pattern** (internal/store/notify.go, internal/tui/screen_notify_settings.go)

**Storage model:**
- **NotifyPrefs struct** (notify.go:15–21): Five boolean fields (`OnPush`, `OnWB`, `OnMail`, `OnReply`, `OnlyWhenOffline`).
- **Lazy-loaded defaults:** `DefaultNotifyPrefs()` (line 26–28) mirrors column DEFAULTs; **absence from DB = all defaults**.
- **Upsert pattern:** `GetPrefs()` (line 64–84) returns defaults if row absent; `SetPrefs()` (line 87–103) **upserts via `ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE`**.

**TUI load & update:**
- Screen loads prefs in constructor `newNotifySettingsModel()` (screen_notify_settings.go:72–97):
  ```go
  p, err := deps.Store.Notify().GetPrefs(ctx, deps.User.ID)
  m.prefs = p
  ```
- Toggle tracked with `prefsDirty` flag (line 46); saved via `Ctrl+S` → `savePrefs()` (line 229–246):
  ```go
  m.deps.Store.Notify().SetPrefs(context.Background(), m.deps.User.ID, m.prefs)
  ```
- **No User struct refresh** — prefs live in separate table, don't mutate `User` object.

---

### 3. **User Settings Sub-Menu Architecture** (internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go:1–105)

**Sub-screen registry:** Static menuItem list in `newUserSettingsModel()` (lines 21–28). Currently four entries: password change, bio edit, notification settings, back.

**Navigation:** Each item has a `to: Screen` constant (ScreenPasswordChange, ScreenBioEdit, ScreenNotifySettings). When user presses Enter, emit `NavigateMsg{To: it.to}` (lines 50–51).

**Pattern for adding a toggle:** The settings sub-menu itself is purely **navigational**; it doesn't host toggles. Real toggles live in dedicated sub-screens (see bio-edit, notify-settings). **For a locale toggle, add a new ScreenLocaleSettings screen** or integrate inline (see below).

**Inline toggle pattern does NOT exist here.** Every editable setting gets its own Screen. However, for a simple 2-option toggle (zh-TW ↔ en), you could:
- Add a new `menuItem` in screen_user_settings.go pointing to a minimal `ScreenLocaleSettings`
- Or reuse notify-settings pattern: add `Locale` field to User struct, create `screen_locale_settings.go` with a single toggle similar to `flipPref()` in notify-settings (line 214–227).

---

### 4. **User Object in Deps: Load & Refresh** (internal/tui/root.go:17–24, screen_bio_edit.go:64–86)

**Deps struct** (root.go:17–24):
```go
type Deps struct {
    Store              *store.Store
    Broker             *chat.Broker
    Notify             *notify.Manager
    User               *store.User     // pointer; nil iff IsRegister
    IsRegister         bool
    MustChangePassword bool
}
```

**Refresh pattern** (screen_bio_edit.go:79–83):
```go
if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
    *m.deps.User = *fresh  // in-place mutation of the Deps.User pointer
}
```

**Impact:** This mutates the User object **in place** via pointer dereference. All screens sharing `deps` see the updated User immediately (no re-login needed). **For locale, follow this exact pattern** in your locale-settings save handler.

---

### 5. **Migration Version Numbers Currently in Use**

```

0002_seed_boards.sql
0003_add_mail.sql
0004_add_user_roles.sql
0005_articles_updated_at.sql

0007_articles_pinned_at.sql
0008_articles_comments_mode.sql
0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql (bio + user_notif_prefs + user_notif_targets)
```

**Next:** Use `0010_add_user_locale.sql` for the locale column.

```text
17	// articleExportModel renders an article (with optional pushes) as
18	// markdown for the user to copy. Three exits:
19	//   - mouse-select in the terminal scrollback (always works; just View)
20	//   - `c` → emit OSC 52 to push into the local terminal clipboard
21	//   - `3` → write to data/exports/<userid>/<id>-<unix>.md (registered users)
22	//
23	// Read-only screen — no permission gates beyond "guest can't write to disk".
24	type articleExportModel struct {
25		deps      Deps
26		articleID int64
27		article   *store.Article
28		pushes    []*store.Push
29		board     *store.Board
30	
31		includePushes bool
32		width, height int
33		scroll        int
34		rendered      string
35		loadErr       error
36	
37		// transient feedback line shown under the help footer.
38		statusLine string
39	}
40	
41	func newArticleExportModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleExportModel {
42		ctx := context.Background()
43		m := articleExportModel{deps: deps, articleID: articleID}
44		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
45		if err != nil {
46			m.loadErr = err
47			return m
48		}
49		pushes, _ := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
50		board, _ := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, a.BoardID)
51		m.article = a
52		m.pushes = pushes
53		m.board = board
54		m.rerender()
55		return m
56	}
57	
58	func (m *articleExportModel) rerender() {
59		if m.article == nil {
60			return
61		}
62		boardName := ""
63		if m.board != nil {
64			boardName = m.board.Name
65		}
66		out, err := markdown.Format(m.article, m.pushes, markdown.FormatOpts{
67			IncludePushes: m.includePushes,
68			BoardName:     boardName,
69		})
70		if err != nil {
71			m.rendered = "(format error: " + err.Error() + ")"
72			return
73		}
74		m.rendered = out
75	}
76	
77	func (m articleExportModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
78	
79	func (m articleExportModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
80		switch msg := msg.(type) {
81		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
82			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
83			return m, nil
84	
85		case tea.KeyMsg:
86			if m.loadErr != nil {
87				return m, m.backCmd()
88			}
89			switch msg.String() {
90			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
91				return m, m.backCmd()
92			case "1":
93				m.includePushes = false
94				m.rerender()
95				m.scroll = 0
96				m.statusLine = ""
97				return m, nil
98			case "2":
99				m.includePushes = true
100				m.rerender()
```

```text
16	// runImport handles `sshbbs import --file FILE --board NAME [--author USER]`.
17	// It opens the SQLite store, parses the markdown file, looks up the target
18	// board and author, and inserts a new article. Pushes in the markdown file
19	// are deliberately ignored — round-tripping pushes requires verifying each

25		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("import", flag.ContinueOnError)
26		fs.SetOutput(os.Stderr)
27		fs.Usage = func() {
28			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Usage: sshbbs import --file PATH --board NAME [--author USER] [--db PATH]")
29			fs.PrintDefaults()
30		}
31		file := fs.String("file", "", "path to the markdown file (use '-' for stdin)")
32		board := fs.String("board", "", "target board name (overrides frontmatter `board:`)")
33		author := fs.String("author", "admin", "author user_id to attribute the import to")
34		dbPath := fs.String("db", "data/bbs.db", "SQLite database path")
35	
36		if err := fs.Parse(args); err != nil {
37			if errors.Is(err, flag.ErrHelp) {
38				return 0
39			}
40			return 2
41		}
42	
43		if *file == "" {
44			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "error: --file is required")
45			fs.Usage()
46			return 2
47		}
48	
49		body, err := readFile(*file)
50		if err != nil {
51			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "read %s: %v\n", *file, err)
52			return 1
53		}
54		parsed, err := markdown.Parse(string(body))
55		if err != nil {
56			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "parse markdown: %v\n", err)
57			return 1
58		}
59		if parsed.Title == "" {
60			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "error: markdown has no `title:` frontmatter")
61			return 1
62		}
63		targetBoard := *board
64		if targetBoard == "" {
65			targetBoard = parsed.BoardName
66		}
67		if targetBoard == "" {
68			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "error: target board not given (use --board or `board:` frontmatter)")
69			return 1

71	
72		st, err := store.Open(*dbPath)
73		if err != nil {
74			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "open store %s: %v\n", *dbPath, err)
75			return 1
76		}
77		defer st.Close()
78	
79		ctx := context.Background()
80		user, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, *author)
```

## i18n Glyph Rendering & Round-Trip Analysis

### 1. **Push Glyph Rendering Call Sites**

All push kind glyphs (推/噓/→) are rendered from two central functions:

**TUI display:**
- `/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:665-675` — `renderPushKind(k)` function returns styled glyphs for UI rendering:
  - Line 668: `StylePushKind.Render("推")` for push
  - Line 670: `StyleBooKind.Render("噓")` for boo
  - Line 672: `StyleArrowKind.Render("→")` for arrow
- Used at line 606 to render each push in the article view table

**Notification titles (webhook):**
- `/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:677-690` — `kindLabel(k)` function returns plain text glyphs for webhooks/notifications:
  - Line 683: returns `"推"` for push
  - Line 685: returns `"噓"` for boo
  - Line 687: returns `"→"` for arrow
- Called at line 544 in notification dispatch: `fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章", u.UserID, kindLabel(m.pushKind))`

**Store layer:**
- `/internal/store/pushes.go:33-43` — `PushKind.Glyph()` method (mirrors renderPushKind/kindLabel):
  - Line 36: `"推"` for PushKindPush
  - Line 38: `"噓"` for PushKindBoo
  - Line 40: `"→"` for PushKindArrow
- Used by markdown export at line 143

**Markdown export (disk output):**
- `/internal/markdown/markdown.go:137-144` — push serialization in Format():
  - Line 143: `fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s [%s] %s  %s\n", p.Kind.Glyph(), ...)` — outputs the glyph to disk

**Inline help text:**
- `/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:639` — help line shows "+ 推 · - 噓 · = →"
- Line 492: error message "comment required for 推/噓"

---

### 2. **Markdown Export/Parse Round-Trip Analysis**

**CRITICAL: This IS persisted and round-tripped.**

The export flow:
1. User presses `y` → navigates to `ScreenArticleExport` 
2. `screen_article_export.go:66` calls `markdown.Format(m.article, m.pushes, FormatOpts{IncludePushes: true, ...})`
3. Export renders pushes at line 143: `"- %s [%s] %s  %s\n"` where first `%s` is `p.Kind.Glyph()` (推/噓/→)
4. User can save to `data/exports/<userid>/<id>-<unix>.md`

The import flow (round-trip):
1. CLI: `sshbbs import --file FILE.md --board NAME`
2. `/cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go:54` calls `markdown.Parse(string(body))`
3. `/internal/markdown/markdown.go:267-277` — `parsePushes()` parses the push block
4. `/internal/markdown/markdown.go:280-338` — `parsePushLine()` **EXPLICITLY PARSES THE GLYPH**:
   - Line 297: `case "推":` → `kind = store.PushKindPush`
   - Line 299: `case "噓":` → `kind = store.PushKindBoo`
   - Line 301: `case "→":` → `kind = store.PushKindArrow`
   - Line 303: `default: return ParsedPush{}, false` (unknown glyph fails parse)

**CONSEQUENCE: Changing the glyph in English mode WILL break the import round-trip.** If you export as "- 👍 [alice] ..." and later try to import that file, `parsePushLine()` won't recognize "👍" and will silently drop the push (line 303 returns `false`).

Note: `cmd_import.go:19-21` explicitly states: "Pushes in the markdown file are deliberately ignored — round-tripping pushes requires verifying each push's author exists in the users table, which is left for a future iteration." But the parsing infrastructure exists and *will parse* the glyphs if you call Parse directly.

---

### 3. **Column Width Validation**

**Good news: Width parity confirmed.**

`/internal/tui/runewidth.go` provides:
- Line 19-26: `PadRight(s, w)` uses `runewidth.StringWidth()` 
- Line 55-56: `Width(s)` public wrapper around `runewidth.StringWidth()`

Width measurements (using `go-runewidth`):
- CJK glyphs (推=2, 噓=2, 爆=2, 鎖=2, 箭=2)
- Emoji (👍=2, 👎=2, 💥=2)
- ASCII arrow (→=1) ← NOTE: renders as 1-cell, NOT 2!

**Problem discovered:** → is 1 cell wide, not 2. If you map → to 👍, you *gain* 1 cell. The score column and title truncation at line 284-290 uses computed widths, so a 1-cell vs 2-cell glyph WILL shift the table layout. You need to either:
1. Map 推→👍 but keep 噓→👎 and introduce a fixed-width alias for arrow (e.g., " →" with leading space = 2 cells)
2. Or accept that en-mode uses emojis and recalculate titleW in the scoring switch

No test suite exists for width assertions. You should add one to `*_test.go` that validates both CJK and emoji render correctly in the score column.

---

### 4. **"爆" (Explosion) Rendering**

`/internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:299-307`:
```
case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
  score = "爆"
```

**Threshold: RecommendScore ≥ 100.** Map to 💥 or "HOT" in English mode.
- Width of "爆" = 2 cells
- Width of "💥" = 2 cells  
- Width of "HOT" = 3 cells (width overflow!)

If you use "HOT", the score column expands. The config uses a fixed `scoreW = 4` (line 281), so "HOT" fits (3 chars) but "爆" or "💥" are both width=2. Recommendation: use 💥 for 1:1 width parity, or pad "HOT" to 2 cells (e.g., "HT" or use a 2-char marker).

---

### 5. **Lock/Arrow Badges**

`/internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:318-323`:
- Line 320: `prefix += "[鎖]"` for CommentsModeLocked (width of 鎖=2, total badge="[鎖]"=4 cells)
- Line 322: `prefix += "[箭]"` for CommentsModeArrowsOnly (width of 箭=2, total badge="[箭]"=4 cells)

Also rendered in article view:
- `screen_article_view.go:574` — `"[箭] 僅開放箭頭"` in the header
- `screen_article_view.go:576` — `"[鎖] 已關閉留言"` in the header

English equivalents need to maintain ≤4 cell width for title truncation not to break:
- `[🔒]` = 5 cells (bracket + emoji + bracket) — OVERFLOW
- `[L]` = 3 cells — OK
- `[→]` = 3 cells — OK (reusing arrow)

---

### 6. **Notification Titles for Webhooks**

`/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:537-546`:
```go
if m.deps.Notify != nil && m.article.AuthorID != u.ID {
  m.deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
    Title: fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章", u.UserID, kindLabel(m.pushKind)),
    ...
  })
}
```

**Issue: Notification titles are sent to user-configured external webhooks.** These titles contain the push verb rendered in Chinese (推/噓/→). 

**Decision needed:** Should these respect recipient locale? Currently:
- The dispatcher sends the same Chinese text to all webhooks
- Webhook consumers (Discord, Slack, Telegram, SMS) render it as-is
- Non-Chinese speakers see "推" / "噓" / "→" in their notifications

Two approaches:
1. **Locale-oblivious:** Keep Chinese in webhook titles (current state) — most users on a PTT-style BBS are Chinese speakers
2. **Locale-aware:** Require the store/article to track recipient's locale, then call `kindLabel()` at dispatch time with a target locale
   - This requires propagating locale through `notify.Event` or looking it up from `ToUserID`

For an MVP English mode, **webhook titles should probably respect the recipient's configured UI locale**, but this requires refactoring the notify dispatcher to accept a target locale and adding a translation layer for kindLabel(). Currently, `kindLabel()` is hardcoded to Chinese glyphs.

**Other notify call sites:**
- `/internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:13-24` — `notifyWB()` for water balloons, hardcoded Chinese: `"[BBS] %s 丟了水球給你"`
- `/internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:28-44` — `notifyMail()` for mail, hardcoded Chinese: `"[BBS] %s 寄信給你: %s"`

All three need localization for proper English mode support.

---

### Summary Table

| Item | File:Line | Status | Impact |
|------|-----------|--------|--------|
| Push glyph rendering (TUI) | screen_article_view.go:668,670,672 | Centralized in `renderPushKind()` | High — 1 function, easy to swap |
| Push glyph parsing (markdown) | markdown.go:297-303 | Hard-coded glyphs in switch | **BREAKING** — export/import round-trip depends on exact glyph |
| Notification glyphs | screen_article_view.go:544, notify_helpers.go:17,38 | Hardcoded Chinese | Medium — webhooks expose locale mismatch |
| Width helpers | runewidth.go:19,34,56 | Uses `go-runewidth.StringWidth()` | Low — emojis measure correctly |
| Explosion threshold | screen_board_view.go:301 | >= 100 recommend_score | Low — just the condition |
| Lock/arrow badges | screen_board_view.go:320,322 | Hardcoded glyphs in prefix | Medium — title truncation depends on prefix width |

**Recommendation:** Create an `i18n.PushKind` function that returns a localized glyph/label, then update `renderPushKind()`, `kindLabel()`, `PushKind.Glyph()`, and the markdown parser to use it. For round-trip safety, **either persist the kind enum (not the glyph) to markdown export, or accept that localized exports cannot be re-imported.**

# Bilingual support (zh-TW + en) for sshbbs — implementation plan

Default locale stays `zh-TW`; user opts into `en` via a new User Settings entry. In `en`, push glyphs render as 👍/👎 (with 爆 → 💥) to keep column widths stable; everything else is direct translation. **Markdown export/import stays canonical zh-TW** — `PushKind.Glyph()` is unchanged so round-trip via `cmd_import` is byte-stable.

## 1. `internal/i18n` package design

Create `internal/i18n/i18n.go` (+ `i18n_test.go`, + per-locale tables `zh_tw.go`, `en.go`).

**API surface**

```go
package i18n

type Locale string

const (
    LocaleZHTW Locale = "zh-TW"
    LocaleEN   Locale = "en"
    Default           = LocaleZHTW
)

// Valid reports whether s is a recognised locale value. Used by the
// settings screen and by the User-loader fallback.
func Valid(s string) bool

// Normalize coerces a stored value to a known locale, falling back to
// Default on empty or unrecognised input. Centralizes the "old DB row /
// nil User" fallback so call sites don't have to.
func Normalize(s string) Locale

// T looks up key in loc's table. Missing keys fall back to zh-TW; missing
// in both returns the key itself wrapped in «» so it stands out in the UI
// (so we can grep for unlocalised keys post-deploy without crashing).
func T(loc Locale, key string) string

// Tf is fmt.Sprintf over T. Format directives live in the locale string
// so word order can flow naturally per-language ("%s pushed your post" vs
// "%s 推了你的文章").
func Tf(loc Locale, key string, args ...any) string
```

**Storage choice: Go map literals per locale, not embedded JSON.**

Justification:
- Compile-time safety. A typo in a key name fails at the call site (we'll use `const KeyMainMenuTitle = "screen.main_menu.title"` shared keys; see §7), not at runtime when the user happens to navigate to that screen.
- No new dependency, no new I/O path, no need to teach `embed` or wire up a loader. The repo's pattern (CLAUDE.md §"data deliberately diverges") favours pure-Go primitives over framework adoption.
- Two locales × ~150 strings is small enough that maps are readable. JSON would only win at ~5 locales or if non-engineers were translating.
- Existing `internal/markdown` package shows the precedent: leaf package, pure functions, no DB or I/O.

Layout:
```
internal/i18n/
    i18n.go        // Locale type, Valid/Normalize/T/Tf, the table registry
    keys.go        // const Key* = "screen.main_menu.title" string identifiers
    zh_tw.go       // var zhTW = map[string]string{...}
    en.go          // var en   = map[string]string{...}
    glyphs.go      // PushGlyph(loc, kind) etc. — see §3
    i18n_test.go   // key-completeness, glyph width, Normalize fallback
```

Format-arg handling: lean entirely on `fmt.Sprintf`. The few strings that interpolate (`"%s 推了你的文章"`, `"確定刪除推文 #%d?"`) keep their `%s`/`%d` directives in both translations. Don't introduce ICU MessageFormat — over-engineered for two locales without plural-form differences worth speaking of.

## 2. Locale storage + plumbing

**Migration `0010_add_user_locale.sql`:**

```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW';
```

That's it. Per CLAUDE.md, migrations live under `internal/store/migrations/` and are picked up via the `go:embed migrations/*.sql` glob in `internal/store/migrate.go`. No schema_migrations bookkeeping change needed — `migrate.apply()` parses the `0010` prefix.

**`internal/store/users.go` changes:**

1. Add `Locale string` to the `User` struct (place after `Bio`).
2. Append `, locale` to the `userColumns` const string (literal SQL).
3. Append `&u.Locale` to the `scanUser` Scan call list.
4. Add a repo method:
   ```go
   func (r *UserRepo) SetLocale(ctx context.Context, id int64, locale string) error
   ```
   Mirrors `SetBio`/`SetPassword`: takes `r.s.writeMu.Lock()`, runs `UPDATE users SET locale = ? WHERE id = ?`, returns the err. Caller (the new locale-settings screen) is responsible for validation via `i18n.Valid` before invoking.
5. `InsertSystemAccount` — leave alone. The DEFAULT 'zh-TW' covers admin/guest seeds.

**Where `Deps.User.Locale` is read in TUI screens:**

Helper `func (m someModel) loc() i18n.Locale { return i18n.Normalize(localeOf(m.deps)) }` defined once per model file (or as a free function `localeOf(deps Deps) i18n.Locale` in a new `internal/tui/i18n.go` shim). Implementation:

```go
// localeOf returns the User.Locale or i18n.Default on a register/login
// screen where deps.User is nil. Centralises the nil-guard so individual
// screens don't repeat it.
func localeOf(d Deps) i18n.Locale {
    if d.User == nil {
        return i18n.Default
    }
    return i18n.Normalize(d.User.Locale)
}
```

**Fallback when `User` is nil:** the register screen and the SSH password-callback path can hit T() before any User row exists. `localeOf` returns `i18n.Default` (zh-TW) which is exactly the behaviour we want. The register form's `?` placeholder shows in zh-TW; once the user submits and we have a `*store.User`, subsequent screens see the persisted locale (defaults to zh-TW for new accounts).

**In-place refresh after locale change:** mirror the bio-edit pattern (`screen_bio_edit.go:79-83`):
```go
if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
    *m.deps.User = *fresh
}
```
Because `Deps.User` is a pointer (`*store.User`), the in-place overwrite is visible to every screen that holds the same `Deps` value — so the next navigate already renders in the new locale without a re-login.

## 3. Push / score / badge glyph mapping

Live in `internal/i18n/glyphs.go`. These are tied to UI display, NOT to canonical storage — the markdown serializer keeps using `PushKind.Glyph()`.

| Concept | zh-TW | en | Both widths | Where used |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Push (+1) | `推` | `👍` | 2 | `renderPushKind`, `kindLabel`, `screen_article_view` push input prompt |
| Boo (-1) | `噓` | `👎` | 2 | same |
| Arrow (0) | `→` | `→` | 1 | same — keep universal arrow |
| Score "exploded" (≥100) | `爆` | `💥` | 2 | `screen_board_view.go:301-307` |
| Score "X" (negative) | `X9` | `X9` | n+1 | same — leave; X is universal |
| Locked badge | `[鎖]` | `[L]` | 4 → 3 | `screen_board_view.go:320`, `screen_article_view.go:576` |
| Arrows-only badge | `[箭]` | `[A]` | 4 → 3 | `screen_board_view.go:322`, `screen_article_view.go:574` |
| Pinned badge | `[M]` | `[M]` | 3 | `screen_board_view.go:316` — universal, no swap |

The `[L]`/`[A]` badge is a **prefix** on the title cell, not a column-aligned cell, so the 1-column shrink is acceptable (CJK text after the badge floats; English doesn't care). Verified by reading the assembly at `screen_board_view.go:324-326` — `prefix + " " + a.Title` is then run through `Truncate(title, titleW)`, which is runewidth-aware.

**Helper signatures (in `internal/i18n/glyphs.go`):**

```go
import "github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"

// PushGlyph returns the locale-appropriate glyph for a push kind. Unstyled.
// Caller is responsible for lipgloss styling so the styles package doesn't
// need to import store.
func PushGlyph(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string

// PushVerb returns the locale-appropriate short verb for use inside a
// templated sentence (e.g. webhook titles: "alice 推了你的文章" /
// "alice 👍 your post"). Distinct from PushGlyph only because the en
// translation may want a verb like "upvoted" instead of the emoji.
// Recommendation: also use 👍/👎 here for compact webhook embeds —
// matches the in-TUI rendering, and avoids the verb-tense headache.
func PushVerb(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string

// ScoreExploded returns the glyph for an article whose recommend_score
// has hit the "爆" threshold (≥100 in screen_board_view).
func ScoreExploded(loc Locale) string  // "爆" / "💥"

// CommentsModeBadge returns the bracketed mode marker shown on board
// list rows and the article header. Empty for CommentsModeOpen.
func CommentsModeBadge(loc Locale, m store.CommentsMode) string  // "[鎖]" / "[L]" etc.
```

The two TUI sibling functions in `screen_article_view.go` (`renderPushKind` keeping its lipgloss styles, `kindLabel` plain) become thin wrappers:

```go
func renderPushKind(loc i18n.Locale, k store.PushKind) string {
    g := i18n.PushGlyph(loc, k)
    switch k {
    case store.PushKindPush: return StylePushKind.Render(g)
    case store.PushKindBoo:  return StyleBooKind.Render(g)
    case store.PushKindArrow: return StyleArrowKind.Render(g)
    }
    return "?"
}
func kindLabel(loc i18n.Locale, k store.PushKind) string {
    return i18n.PushVerb(loc, k)
}
```

Every call site already has `m.deps.User` (or wants `m.loc()`), so threading the extra arg is mechanical.

**`PushKind.Glyph()` in `internal/store/pushes.go:33-43` stays unchanged.** That method is the canonical serializer used by `markdown.Format` line 143, and `parsePushLine` (markdown.go:297-303) only knows how to read `推`/`噓`/`→`. Touching it would break round-trip with existing exported files and with any hand-edited markdown the user re-imports. This is the most important invariant in the whole change.

## 4. The new locale-settings screen

**Recommendation: dedicated `screen_locale_settings.go` (3rd entry in user-settings sub-menu, before "通知設定").**

Rationale:
- The notify-settings screen already proves the pattern (`prefsDirty` + `Ctrl+S` to commit), but locale is a single-radio-style choice with two options. A dedicated screen lets us render a 2-row chooser with `[•] zh-TW (繁體中文) / [ ] English` and explicit Save/Cancel semantics — clearer than wedging another toggle into notify-settings (which is "events + targets", a different concern).
- Mirrors `screen_bio_edit`: small focused form, navigated to from `screen_user_settings`, returns to it on save/cancel. Different from bio-edit in that it's not a textinput form — so it stays a non-form screen (no `isFormScreen` entry); h/l navigation can stay bound for left=back/right=select.
- Keeps the user-settings menu structure stable: 4 → 5 entries, numeric shortcuts `1-5` (extend the case in `screen_user_settings.go:52`).

**`screen_user_settings.go` updates:**
```go
items := []menuItem{
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.password"), hint: ..., to: ScreenPasswordChange},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.bio"),      hint: ..., to: ScreenBioEdit},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.locale"),   hint: ..., to: ScreenLocaleSettings},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.notify"),   hint: ..., to: ScreenNotifySettings},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "common.back"),                   hint: ..., to: ScreenMainMenu},
}
```
Update the case `"1", "2", "3", "4"` → `"1", "2", "3", "4", "5"`.

**`messages.go`:** add `ScreenLocaleSettings` after `ScreenNotifySettings` (append at the bottom — `Screen` is `iota`, append-only is the safe pattern).

**`root.go:navigate` switch:** add
```go
case ScreenLocaleSettings:
    sub = newLocaleSettingsModel(m.deps)
```

**`help.go`:** add a `screenHelp[ScreenLocaleSettings]` entry; do **not** add to `isFormScreen` (it's a chooser, not a textinput form, so `?` should still pop the help overlay).

**Model sketch:**

```go
type localeSettingsModel struct {
    deps    Deps
    cursor  int            // 0 = zh-TW, 1 = en
    options []i18n.Locale  // {LocaleZHTW, LocaleEN}
    dirty   bool
    err     string
    flash   string
}
```

Keys: `j/k`/arrows move; Enter/Space selects (sets cursor + dirty); `Ctrl+S` persists via `Users().SetLocale` then refreshes `*m.deps.User` and toasts; Esc/`h`/left returns to user-settings (warn if dirty? — match notify-settings, which doesn't warn; same pattern here, just discard).

## 5. Webhook notification locale strategy

**Pick recipient locale.**

Rationale:
- A push notification is an event on the recipient's phone; the body and title should read in the recipient's preferred language. If alice (en) pushes bob's article and bob has zh-TW, bob should get `[BBS] alice 推了你的文章`, not `[BBS] alice 👍 your post`.
- Consistent with how email/SMS UX works generally — sender's choice of UI doesn't dictate the recipient's notification text.
- Cheap: the dispatcher already does a `users` lookup-by-id implicitly inside the deliver path? No — it loads `user_notif_prefs` by user_id. So we need one extra `Users().GetByID(ctx, ev.ToUserID)` in the dispatcher's `deliver` to grab `Locale`.

**Where the lookup happens:**

The cleanest seam is at the call site in TUI helpers (`notify_helpers.go` + the inline push-notify in `screen_article_view.go:539-547`), because:
- Title/body are already pre-rendered there as plain strings; the dispatcher just POSTs them. (Confirmed in `internal/notify/dispatcher.go:241-266` — `m.post` writes `ev.Title`/`ev.Body` to the HTTP body via `buildRequest`, no transformation.)
- Threading locale into the dispatcher would force the dispatcher to know about i18n keys (cross-package coupling: `internal/notify` would have to import `internal/i18n` AND `internal/store` for the User lookup).
- Doing it at the call site is one extra DB read per notification, which is fine — notifications are already async and rate-limited by webhook upstreams.

So `notify_helpers.go` becomes:

```go
func notifyWB(deps Deps, toUserID int64, fromUserID, body string) {
    if deps.Notify == nil {
        return
    }
    loc := i18n.Default
    if deps.Store != nil {
        if u, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), toUserID); err == nil {
            loc = i18n.Normalize(u.Locale)
        }
    }
    deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
        Kind:       notify.KindWB,
        ToUserID:   toUserID,
        FromUserID: fromUserID,
        Title:      i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.wb.title", fromUserID),
        Body:       body,
    })
}
```

Same shape for `notifyMail` and the inline `Notify.Dispatch` in `screen_article_view.go`. The push notification specifically needs the verb interpolation:

```go
Title: i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.push.title", u.UserID, i18n.PushVerb(loc, m.pushKind)),
```
with strings:
```
zh-TW: "[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章"
en   : "[BBS] %s %s your post"
```

If `Store.Users().GetByID` fails (deleted user race), we fall back to `i18n.Default` and the notification still goes out — graceful degradation, no panic.

## 6. Markdown export untouched — invariant statement

**`internal/store/pushes.go` `PushKind.Glyph()` is canonical, locale-independent, and stays returning `推`/`噓`/`→`.**

Touched-by audit:
- `internal/markdown/markdown.go:143` (Format writer) → unchanged.
- `internal/markdown/markdown.go:297-303` (parsePushLine reader) → unchanged.
- `cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go:54` (CLI import) → unchanged; it consumes whatever Format produced and the parser only knows zh glyphs.

The localised TUI helpers (`renderPushKind`, `kindLabel`, score "爆", `[鎖]`/`[箭]`) all live in `internal/tui/` and `internal/i18n/glyphs.go`, never reach into the store layer's serialization path. Add a comment to `PushKind.Glyph()` reinforcing this:

```go
// Glyph returns the canonical CJK glyph for the kind. This is used by the
// markdown serializer (round-trip with `sshbbs export` / `sshbbs import`)
// and MUST stay locale-independent — the parser at
// internal/markdown/markdown.go:297-303 only recognises 推/噓/→.
// For locale-aware UI rendering, use internal/i18n.PushGlyph instead.
func (k PushKind) Glyph() string { ... }
```

A markdown round-trip test should be added (or the existing one extended) to assert that exporting a push from an `en`-locale user still produces `推` in the file (see §9).

## 7. String key naming convention

Flat dotted scheme, grouped by screen:

```
common.back
common.save
common.cancel

screen.main_menu.title              // " 主選單 Main Menu " / " Main Menu "

screen.main_menu.admin              // "管理 Admin" / "Admin"

screen.locale_settings.title

screen.article_view.arrows_label

screen.notify_settings.title

screen.notify_settings.flash_updated       // "✓ 已更新 target #%d"
```
