screen.board_list.search_placeholder
screen.board_view.title
screen.board_view.sort_byscore_label  // "[排序: 推文量↓]" / "[sort: by score↓]"
screen.board_view.help_line / .help_line_guest

screen.online.title
screen.mail_inbox.title
screen.mail_thread.title
screen.password_change.*
screen.bio_edit.*
screen.register.*
screen.admin_users.*
screen.article_export.*
screen.board_splash.help            // "press any key" — universal but localizable
screen.board_banner_edit.*

error.permission_denied              // "權限不足" / "Permission denied"
error.guest_readonly                 // "guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)" / "guest accounts are read-only"
error.admin_only                     // "管理員專用 (admin only)" / "admin only"
error.comments_locked                // "本文已鎖定留言" / "Comments are locked on this article"
error.comments_arrows_only           // "本文僅開放箭頭"
error.password_wrong                 // "目前密碼錯誤" / "Current password is wrong"
error.no_user                        // "internal error: no user"  (probably leave English-only)

notify.push.title                    // "[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章" / "[BBS] %s %s your post"
notify.wb.title                      // "[BBS] %s 丟了水球給你"   / "[BBS] %s sent you a water balloon"
notify.mail.title                    // "[BBS] %s 寄信給你: %s"   / "[BBS] %s mailed you: %s"
notify.test.title                    // "[BBS-test] notification target verified"  (leave English — operator-facing)

help.global.help                     // "show this help (any key dismisses)"
help.global.wb_inbox                 // "open 水球 inbox" / "open water balloon inbox"
help.global.disconnect

help.main_menu.move
help.main_menu.select
help.main_menu.jump
help.main_menu.quit
... (one entry per HelpEntry row in screenHelp)
```

Conventions:
- All-lowercase, dot-separated.
- Top namespace mirrors a stable concept: `common.`, `screen.<screen_name>.`, `error.`, `notify.`, `help.<screen_name>.`.
- The screen segment matches the file basename without `screen_` prefix and without `.go` (so `screen_main_menu.go` → `screen.main_menu.*`). One refactor-friendly mapping rule.
- Format directives (`%s`, `%d`, `%v`) appear in both translations; both must have the same number of args (asserted in §9 tests).
- Keys defined as `const Key... = "..."` in `internal/i18n/keys.go` so call sites are typo-safe (`i18n.T(loc, i18n.KeyMainMenuTitle)`).

## 8. Migration order / phasing — recommend 3 PRs

**PR 1 — foundation, no UI text changes (zh-only still rendered):**
- Add migration `0010_add_user_locale.sql`.
- Extend `User` struct + `userColumns` + `scanUser` + add `SetLocale` repo method.
- Add `internal/i18n` package with `Locale`, `Valid`, `Normalize`, `T`, `Tf`, `keys.go`, and stub locale tables (only `zh_tw.go` populated; `en.go` is empty so `T(en, key)` falls back to zh and the UI still looks identical to today).
- Add `internal/i18n/glyphs.go` with PushGlyph/PushVerb/ScoreExploded/CommentsModeBadge — both locales filled (this is the smallest, lowest-risk locale data).
- Add `localeOf(deps)` helper in `internal/tui/i18n.go`.
- Add `ScreenLocaleSettings` const + `screen_locale_settings.go` + Root.navigate case + user-settings menu entry. The screen reads/writes the column; nothing else changes visually.
- Tests: locale persistence round-trip; settings screen toggle; `Normalize` fallbacks; PushGlyph table.

End state: a user can flip their locale in settings; nothing else changes yet. Safe to merge.

**PR 2 — batch-migrate strings, screen-by-screen (en table populated):**
- Populate `en.go` with all keys from `keys.go`.
- Convert each screen's hard-coded strings to `i18n.T(m.loc(), Key...)` calls. Recommended order (least to most state):
  1. `screen_main_menu.go` (small, high visibility, easy to verify)
  2. `screen_user_settings.go` (already touched in PR 1)
  3. `screen_locale_settings.go` (the new screen — populate its strings)
  4. `screen_board_list.go`, `screen_board_view.go` (no push glyphs yet — leave score/badge calls as-is for now, swap in PR 3)
  5. `screen_article_view.go` (excluding `renderPushKind` — that's PR 3)
  6. `screen_article_edit.go`, `screen_article_export.go`
  7. `screen_post_compose.go`, `screen_register.go`, `screen_password_change.go`, `screen_bio_edit.go`
  8. `screen_wb.go`, `screen_mail.go`, `screen_online.go`, `screen_admin_users.go`
  9. `screen_notify_settings.go`, `screen_board_banner_edit.go`, `screen_board_splash.go`
  10. `help.go` keymap entries (`screenHelp` table values + `helpGlobals`).
- Also localize webhook notification titles in `notify_helpers.go` + `screen_article_view.go:544` per §5.
- Update the few existing tests that grep for CJK strings to either parameterize over locale or pin to `LocaleZHTW` explicitly (most TUI tests don't construct a User with a locale, so `Normalize("")` returns Default = zh-TW and they keep passing — see §9).

End state: en mode is functional but still uses zh push glyphs.

**PR 3 — push glyph swap with width tests:**
- Wire `renderPushKind`/`kindLabel`/the `[鎖]`/`[箭]` badges/`爆` score in `screen_board_view.go:301` and `screen_article_view.go:573-577,665-690` through `i18n.PushGlyph` / `i18n.CommentsModeBadge` / `i18n.ScoreExploded`.
- Add the runewidth-parity test (§9).
- Update `help.go` Article-view help entry from `"+ / - / =", "推 / 噓 / → (non-guest)"` to use locale glyphs.

This phasing isolates the highest-risk visual change (emoji widths in real terminals) into a small reviewable PR with a dedicated test.

## 9. Testing strategy

**`internal/i18n/i18n_test.go`** — three tests, all keyed off the public API:

1. **Key completeness**: iterate `zhTW`, assert every key exists in `en` (and vice-versa). Fail with the missing key list. Catches "translator forgot to add key X" at PR time.
2. **Format-arg parity**: for every key, parse out `%s`/`%d`/`%v`/`%q` directives in both locales' values; assert the count and types match. Catches "translator dropped the `%s`" — the Tf call would otherwise emit the `%!(MISSING)` token at runtime.
3. **Normalize fallback**: table-driven over `("", "fr", "zh", "zh-TW", "en")` → expect `Default, Default, Default, LocaleZHTW, LocaleEN`.

**`internal/i18n/glyphs_test.go`** — runewidth parity:

```go
func TestPushGlyph_WidthParity(t *testing.T) {
    for _, k := range []store.PushKind{store.PushKindPush, store.PushKindBoo, store.PushKindArrow} {
        zh := i18n.PushGlyph(i18n.LocaleZHTW, k)
        en := i18n.PushGlyph(i18n.LocaleEN, k)
        if runewidth.StringWidth(zh) != runewidth.StringWidth(en) {
            t.Errorf("kind %v: zh=%q (w=%d) vs en=%q (w=%d) — must match for column alignment",
                k, zh, runewidth.StringWidth(zh), en,
                runewidth.StringWidth(zh), runewidth.StringWidth(en))
        }
    }
    // Same for ScoreExploded.
}
```

This catches the case where someone "improves" a glyph to a 1-cell ASCII variant and breaks the article-list column math. Run under the same `runewidth` import the TUI uses (`github.com/mattn/go-runewidth`).

**TUI tests:**
- **Keybinding-parity tests** (`screen_*_test.go`) don't reference label text, so they're unaffected. Spot-checked `screen_user_settings_test.go` patterns; they assert NavigateMsg payloads, not strings.
- **`help_test.go`** asserts certain header strings are present in the rendered help overlay; update its assertions to either (a) use the i18n key directly (`i18n.T(i18n.Default, key)`) and assert that's a substring, or (b) do `setLocale(t, i18n.LocaleZHTW)` per test and assert the literal — option (a) is more refactor-resistant.
- **Render tests that grep for "[M]", "[鎖]", "[箭]"** in `screen_board_view_test.go:339,350-351,383` and `screen_article_view_test.go:889-890`: parameterize the test over locale, keep the existing zh assertion when locale is zh-TW, add an `[L]`/`[A]` assertion when locale is en. Construct the test User with `Locale: "en"` to drive the new path.
- **Locale-settings screen** new test: toggle, save, assert `*deps.User` updated in place AND DB row updated.
- **Webhook recipient-locale** new test: insert a recipient User with `Locale: "en"`, send a push as a zh-locale user, capture the Notify.Dispatch event via a recording dispatcher (similar to `chat.fakeSender`), assert `Title` is in English.

**Markdown round-trip regression** (in `internal/markdown/markdown_test.go` — extend existing test):
- Construct a Push, hand it to `Format` with the producing user's locale set to `en`, assert the output literally contains `推` (since `PushKind.Glyph()` is locale-independent).
- Then `Parse` the output and assert the parsed push kind round-trips. If this fails, the §6 invariant has been broken.

CI command stays `make test-race`. No new flaky surface.

**Automated (run before each PR merges):**

make test-race                    # full suite, race detector — all green
go test -race ./internal/i18n/... # focused
go test -race ./internal/tui/... -run TestLocaleSettings
go test -race ./internal/markdown/... -run TestRoundtripPushGlyphIsLocaleIndependent
```

**Manual end-to-end (after PR 3 merges):**
```bash
make hostkey                      # if not already done
make db-reset                     # fresh DB so migration 0010 runs cleanly
make run                          # boots :2222
```

In a second terminal:
```bash
ssh new@localhost -p 2222         # register a fresh user "alice", any password OK during register
# After registering, you land on the main menu in zh-TW (default).
# Verify: header "主選單 Main Menu", item "看板列表 Boards", help line in zh.

# Navigate: 個人設定 → 語言 Language
# Move cursor to English, Enter to select, Ctrl+S to save.
# Expect toast "✓ Locale saved" and the screen header to flip to English on next render.

# Esc back to main menu — now in English.
# Verify: "Main Menu" header, "Boards" item, "Online" item, English help line.

# Open a board, open an article.
# Verify: 推 column shows 👍 (in green), 噓 shows 👎 (in red), → unchanged.
# Score column: post 100 pushes (or doctor recommend_score in DB), verify shows 💥 not 爆.
# Pin an article (mod): [M] still shows.
# Lock comments: prefix shows [L] not [鎊]; article header says "[L] Comments locked".

# Push the article: + key. Verify the push input prompt's leading glyph is 👍.
```

In a third terminal:
```bash
ssh bob@localhost -p 2222         # register bob, leave at zh-TW default
# Have bob open an article alice authored, push it (+).
# Set up a webhook target on alice's account first (use https://requestbin.com).
# Verify alice's webhook receives "[BBS] bob 👍 your post" because alice's
# locale is en. Conversely have alice push bob's article — bob's webhook
# receives "[BBS] alice 推了你的文章" in zh.
```

**Markdown round-trip (proves §6 invariant):**
```bash
# As alice (en mode), find an article ID with pushes, then:
./sshbbs export --article 5 --include-pushes > /tmp/a.md
grep -E '^- (推|噓|→) \[' /tmp/a.md           # MUST find canonical zh glyphs
! grep -E '^- (👍|👎) \[' /tmp/a.md            # MUST NOT find emoji
./sshbbs import --file /tmp/a.md --board Welcome    # round-trip back in
# Verify the new imported article has the same push count and kinds in the DB.
```

If either grep behaves differently, PR 3 broke §6 — revert and audit `renderPushKind` callers for accidental writes back into `markdown.Format`.

**SQLite migration verification:**
```bash
sqlite3 data/bbs.db ".schema users" | grep -i locale
# Expect:  locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW'
sqlite3 data/bbs.db "SELECT user_id, locale FROM users;"
# Expect every existing row to show 'zh-TW' — DEFAULT applied to backfilled rows.
```

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## What this is
6	
7	A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
8	`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
9	SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
10	is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
11	keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
12	post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
13	they remain usable as text-edit keys.
14	
15	For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
16	`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.
17	
18	## Common commands
19	
20	```bash
21	make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
22	make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
23	make watch              # auto-rebuild on .go/.sql changes; SIGINT-then-restart via `air`
24	make build              # produces ./sshbbs
25	make test               # plain go test
26	make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
27	make cover              # per-package coverage
28	make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*
29	
30	# focused testing
31	go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
32	go test -race ./internal/chat/...
33	
34	# manual two-user smoke
35	ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
36	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
37	```
38	
39	If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
40	Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.
41	
42	## Architecture — the non-obvious parts
43	
44	### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
45	`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
46	`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
47	`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
48	handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
49	other SSH username, `auth.VerifyLogin` runs bcrypt against the DB and
50	stashes the `user_id` (int64) in the context for `makeProgramHandler`
51	to load.
52	
53	This means `auth.Register` rejects the literal username `new` — that
54	constant is referenced in two places (auth + middleware) and they must
55	not drift. `auth.go` defines it once.
56	
57	### Wish uses `MiddlewareWithProgramHandler`, not `Middleware`
58	We pick the *ProgramHandler* variant deliberately
59	(`internal/server/server.go`) because we need the `*tea.Program` pointer
60	to register the session with `chat.Broker` and to `program.Send(tea.Quit())`
61	during graceful shutdown. The simpler `Middleware(handler)` form constructs
62	the program internally and hides the pointer — you can't broadcast
63	into it. Don't switch back.
64	
65	### The chat broker takes a `Sender` interface, not `*tea.Program`
66	`chat.Sender` is `interface { Send(tea.Msg) }`. `*tea.Program` satisfies
67	it naturally. The interface exists purely so unit tests can substitute
68	a recording fake (`fakeSender` in `broker_test.go`) without spinning up
69	a real bubbletea program. If you find yourself wanting to mock the
70	broker, you probably want to add a method here, not refactor.
71	
72	### Live broadcasts are "send to everyone, filter on receive"
73	For 推文 (push) updates, `screen_article_view.updatePushInput` calls
74	`broker.SendToAll(senderUID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, ...})`. Every
75	connected session receives the message; each article-view model
76	ignores it unless `msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID`. This is simpler than
77	tracking who's viewing what and good enough for our scale. When the
78	filter matches, we **re-fetch from DB** rather than trust the broker
79	payload — that way timestamps and `recommend_score` are always canonical.
80	
81	Water balloons (`broker.Send(toUID, ...)`) target a single user; the
82	multi-session "雙開" case fans out to every live `Session` of that user.
83	
84	### Per-user webhook notifications go through `internal/notify`
85	`notify.Manager` runs a tiny worker pool that POSTs `{title, body}` JSON
86	to user-configured webhook URLs. Call-sites (push / WB / mail / Re:)
87	invoke `Dispatch` (non-blocking, drops on full buffer). The BBS
88	deliberately does NOT parse `apprise://` URLs — operators run
89	`caronc/apprise-api` as a docker sidecar and the BBS just POSTs to
90	`http://apprise:8000/notify/<key>`. Architecture rationale +
91	deployment recipe: `docs/notifications.md`.
92	
93	### `Store.writeMu` is process-level, not row-level
94	`internal/store/store.go` exposes a single `sync.Mutex` that's grabbed
95	inside every multi-statement repo method (e.g. `PushRepo.Create` runs
96	`INSERT INTO pushes` and `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score` in one
97	transaction under the mutex). This keeps the cached score atomic from
98	the application's perspective and avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` flapping with
99	the pure-Go driver. Don't use `sql.Tx` ad-hoc inside repo methods
100	without also taking `writeMu`. The `pushes` test
101	(`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`, 50 concurrent inserts) is the
102	canary — if it ever fails under `-race`, the mutex discipline broke.
103	
104	### Migrations live under the store package, not the repo root
105	`internal/store/migrations/*.sql` is embedded with `go:embed
106	migrations/*.sql` from `internal/store/migrate.go`. `go:embed` can't
107	ascend with `..`, so the SQL files live next to the Go code that loads
108	them. Filename format `NNNN_name.sql`; `migrate.apply()` parses the
109	prefix as the version and tracks applied versions in the
110	`schema_migrations` table.
111	
112	### Data deliberately diverges from pttbbs in three places
113	1. **No recno-based article addressing** — `articles.id` (auto-increment)
114	   is opaque and never re-used. Bookmarks stay valid forever.
115	2. **bcrypt cost-12, not `crypt(3)` DES** — `auth.bcryptCost`.
116	3. **No binary shared memory / `userinfo_t` arrays** — presence is a
117	   `sync.RWMutex`-guarded `map[int64][]*Session` in `chat.Broker`.
118	
119	The full mapping (userec_t / boardheader_t / fileheader_t / `.DIR` /
120	推文 / 水球 / userinfo_t) is in `docs/ptt_trace_code/01..06_*.md`.
121	
122	## Testing layers (see `docs/testing.md` for the full doc)
123	
124	- **Layer 1**: plain `go test` for `auth` / `store` / `chat`. Most coverage lives here.
125	- **Layer 2**: direct `tea.Model.Update()` tests in `internal/tui/`. Use
126	  `package tui` (internal), not `tui_test`, to reach unexported screen
127	  constructors. Keybinding-parity tests are table-driven over
128	  `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` etc. — when adding new keys,
129	  extend the table.
130	- **No `teatest` dependency.** It's experimental (`x/exp`); we test
131	  `Update()` directly with `tea.KeyMsg{...}` synthetic input.
132	- **Always run `-race`.** Several tests (`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`,
133	  `TestBroker_Concurrency`) exist specifically as race-detector bait.
134	- **Any test needing a DB** uses `storetest.New(t)` which gives a
135	  fresh `t.TempDir()`-backed SQLite. Never share stores across tests.
136	
137	## Conventions worth preserving
138	
139	- **UTF-8 throughout, no Big5.** Use `internal/tui/runewidth.go` helpers
140	  (`PadRight`, `PadLeft`, `Truncate`, `Width`) for any list rendering
141	  that mixes ASCII and CJK — `lipgloss`'s automatic width is unreliable
142	  for double-width glyphs.
143	- **Form screens skip h/l navigation** so the keys remain available for
144	  text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`,
145	  `screen_wb` (compose half), `screen_bio_edit`, `screen_password_change`.
146	  The canonical list lives in `tui.isFormScreen` (`internal/tui/help.go`)
147	  — adding a new form screen means appending it there too so `?` passes
148	  through to textinput as a literal character.
149	- **NavigateMsg is the only legitimate way to swap screens.** Sub-models
150	  emit it from `Update`; `Root.navigate` is the single switch statement
151	  that constructs the new sub. When you add a new screen, add the case
152	  there and a `Screen*` constant in `messages.go`.
153	- **Window-resize forwarding**: `Root.Update` always forwards
154	  `tea.WindowSizeMsg` to the active sub before its own logic. When
155	  switching to a new sub, `Root.navigate` resends the last known size
156	  so the freshly-mounted screen lays out correctly on first render.
157	
158	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
159	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
160	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
161	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
162	
163	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
164	
165	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
166	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
167	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
168	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
169	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
170	
171	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
172	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
173	drift is caught immediately.
174	
175	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
176	
177	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
178	
179	   ```
180	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
181	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
182	   ```
183	
184	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
185	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
186	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
187	
188	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
189	
190	   ```
191	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
192	   ```
193	
194	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
195	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
196	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
197	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
198	
199	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
200	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
201	
202	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
203	
204	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
205	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
206	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
207	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
208	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
209	
210	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
211	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
212	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
213	
214	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
215	
216	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
217	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
218	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
219	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
220	   revisit adjacent decisions).
221	
222	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
223	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
224	
225	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
226	
227	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
228	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
229	capturing:
230	
231	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
232	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
233	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
234	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
235	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
236	
237	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
238	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
239	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
240	when-to-add rules.
241	
242	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
243	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
244	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
245	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
246	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
247	invariant.
248	
249	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
250	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.
251	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance --> (end)
252
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/users.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type User struct {
11		ID                 int64
12		UserID             string
13		PasswordHash       string
14		Nickname           string
15		RealName           string
16		Email              string
17		NumLogins          int64
18		NumPosts           int64
19		LastLoginAt        sql.NullTime
20		LastHost           sql.NullString
21		CreatedAt          time.Time
22		Role               Role
23		MustChangePassword bool
24		Bio                string
25	}
26	
27	type UserRepo struct{ s *Store }
28	
29	var (
30		ErrUserNotFound = errors.New("user not found")
31		ErrInvalidRole  = errors.New("invalid role")
32	)
33	
34	const userColumns = `id, user_id, password_hash, nickname, realname, email,
35		num_logins, num_posts, last_login_at, last_host, created_at, role, must_change_password, bio`
36	
37	func scanUser(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*User, error) {
38		u := &User{}
39		var mustChange int64
40		err := row.Scan(
41			&u.ID, &u.UserID, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Nickname, &u.RealName, &u.Email,
42			&u.NumLogins, &u.NumPosts, &u.LastLoginAt, &u.LastHost, &u.CreatedAt,
43			&u.Role, &mustChange, &u.Bio,
44		)
45		u.MustChangePassword = mustChange != 0
46		return u, err
47	}
48	
49	func (r *UserRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash, nickname, email string) (*User, error) {
50		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
51		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
52		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
53			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, nickname, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
54			userID, passwordHash, nickname, email,
55		)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, err
58		}
59		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
60		if err != nil {
61			return nil, err
62		}
63		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
64	}
65	
66	func (r *UserRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*User, error) {
67		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id)
68		u, err := scanUser(row)
69		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
70			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
71		}
72		return u, err
73	}
74	
75	func (r *UserRepo) GetByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (*User, error) {
76		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE user_id = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, userID)
77		u, err := scanUser(row)
78		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
79			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
80		}
81		return u, err
82	}
83	
84	func (r *UserRepo) NoteLogin(ctx context.Context, id int64, host string) error {
85		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
86		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
87		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
88			`UPDATE users SET last_login_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, last_host = ?, num_logins = num_logins + 1 WHERE id = ?`,
89			host, id,
90		)
91		return err
92	}
93	
94	func (r *UserRepo) IncrementPosts(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
95		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
96		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
97		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
98			`UPDATE users SET num_posts = num_posts + 1 WHERE id = ?`, id,
99		)
100		return err
101	}
102	
103	// InsertSystemAccount creates a privileged user (admin or guest) with the
104	// given role and must_change_password flag, bypassing the regular Create
105	// path which always defaults role='user'. Used only by the bootstrap seed.
106	func (r *UserRepo) InsertSystemAccount(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash string, role Role, mustChangePassword bool) (*User, error) {
107		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
108		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
109		mc := int64(0)
110		if mustChangePassword {
111			mc = 1
112		}
113		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
114			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, role, must_change_password) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
115			userID, passwordHash, string(role), mc,
116		)
117		if err != nil {
118			return nil, err
119		}
120		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
121		if err != nil {
122			return nil, err
123		}
124		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
125	}
126	
127	// SetPassword updates the password hash and clears must_change_password
128	// in one statement. The TUI password-change screen calls this after
129	// validating the current password and the new-password rules.
130	func (r *UserRepo) SetPassword(ctx context.Context, id int64, passwordHash string) error {
131		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
132		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
133		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
134			`UPDATE users SET password_hash = ?, must_change_password = 0 WHERE id = ?`,
135			passwordHash, id,
136		)
137		return err
138	}
139	
140	// SetBio updates the user's profile bio. Callers (the bio-edit screen) are
141	// responsible for length validation via auth.ValidateBio.
142	func (r *UserRepo) SetBio(ctx context.Context, id int64, bio string) error {
143		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
144		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
145		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
146			`UPDATE users SET bio = ? WHERE id = ?`, bio, id,
147		)
148		return err
149	}
150	
151	// SetRole updates the role of the given user. Callers (admin user-management
152	// screen) are responsible for enforcing higher-level invariants like the
153	// last-admin guard via CountByRole.
154	func (r *UserRepo) SetRole(ctx context.Context, id int64, role Role) error {
155		if !role.Valid() {
156			return ErrInvalidRole
157		}
158		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
159		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
160		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
161			`UPDATE users SET role = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(role), id,
162		)
163		return err
164	}
165	
166	// ListAll returns users ordered by id, paginated. limit<=0 defaults to 20.
167	func (r *UserRepo) ListAll(ctx context.Context, limit, offset int) ([]*User, error) {
168		if limit <= 0 {
169			limit = 20
170		}
171		if offset < 0 {
172			offset = 0
173		}
174		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
175			`SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`,
176			limit, offset,
177		)
178		if err != nil {
179			return nil, err
180		}
181		defer rows.Close()
182		var out []*User
183		for rows.Next() {
184			u, err := scanUser(rows)
185			if err != nil {
186				return nil, err
187			}
188			out = append(out, u)
189		}
190		return out, rows.Err()
191	}
192	
193	// CountByRole returns the number of users currently assigned a given role.
194	// Used by the admin screen's last-admin guard.
195	func (r *UserRepo) CountByRole(ctx context.Context, role Role) (int, error) {
196		var n int
197		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
198			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE role = ?`, string(role),
199		).Scan(&n)
200		return n, err
201	}
202

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/root.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
16	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
17	type Deps struct {
18		Store              *store.Store
19		Broker             *chat.Broker    // nil during register
20		Notify             *notify.Manager // may be nil in tests; call sites must guard
21		User               *store.User     // nil iff IsRegister
22		IsRegister         bool
23		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
24	}
25	
26	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
27	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
28	// and the toast footer.
29	type Root struct {
30		deps   Deps
31		sub    tea.Model
32		width  int
33		height int
34	
35		toast      string
36		toastUntil time.Time
37	
38		// activeScreen tracks which Screen the current sub corresponds to, so
39		// the help overlay can render a context-aware keymap. Set in NewRoot
40		// for the bootstrap sub and in navigate for every subsequent swap.
41		activeScreen Screen
42		// helpVisible is true while the help overlay should render in place of
43		// the sub view. Toggled on '?' and dismissed by any key.
44		helpVisible bool
45	}
46	
47	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
48	
49	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
50	//
51	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
52	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
53	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
54	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
55		r := Root{deps: deps}
56		switch {
57		case deps.IsRegister:
58			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
59			// No Screen const for register; help is suppressed via deps.IsRegister.
60		case deps.MustChangePassword:
61			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
62			r.activeScreen = ScreenPasswordChange
63		default:
64			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
65			r.activeScreen = ScreenMainMenu
66		}
67		return r
68	}
69	
70	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
71		return m.sub.Init()
72	}
73	
74	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
75		switch msg := msg.(type) {
76		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
77			m.width = msg.Width
78			m.height = msg.Height
79			var cmd tea.Cmd
80			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
81			return m, cmd
82	
83		case tea.KeyMsg:
84			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
85			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
86				return m, tea.Quit
87			}
88			// Help overlay: any key dismisses, swallow the keypress so the
89			// sub doesn't act on it. ctrl+c above is the deliberate exception.
90			if m.helpVisible {
91				m.helpVisible = false
92				return m, nil
93			}
94			// '?' opens the help overlay on screens that aren't typing-forms.
95			if msg.String() == "?" && helpAvailable(m.deps, m.activeScreen) {
96				m.helpVisible = true
97				return m, nil
98			}
99			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
100			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
101				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
102			}
103	
104		case NavigateMsg:
105			return m.navigate(msg)
106	
107		case ErrorMsg:
108			if msg.Err != nil {
109				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
110			}
111			return m, nil
112	
113		case WBIncomingMsg:
114			// Suppress the toast when the active screen is a wbThreadModel
115			// pointed at this exact counterparty — the thread itself appends
116			// the message, so the toast would be redundant noise.
117			showToast := true
118			if m.activeScreen == ScreenWBThread {
119				if tm, ok := m.sub.(wbThreadModel); ok && tm.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
120					showToast = false
121				}
122			}
123			if showToast {
124				m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
125			}
126			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect. (The
127			// thread also marks it read — both paths are idempotent.)
128			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
129				go func(id int64) {
130					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
131				}(msg.ID)
132			}
133			// fall through so the active screen can also react
134		}
135	
136		var cmd tea.Cmd
137		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
138		return m, cmd
139	}
140	
141	func (m Root) View() string {
142		if m.helpVisible {
143			// Replace the sub view entirely so the keymap reads cleanly. Toast
144			// suppressed too — would distract from a help screen.
145			return renderHelp(m.activeScreen)
146		}
147		v := m.sub.View()
148		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
149			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
150		}
151		return v
152	}
153	
154	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
155		m.toast = s
156		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
157	}
158	
159	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
160	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
161	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
162		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
163			return nil
164		}
165		switch to {
166		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
167			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
171	
172	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
173	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
174	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
175		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
176			return nil
177		}
178		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
179			return nil
180		}
181		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
182	}
183	
184	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
185		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
186			return m, cmd
187		}
188		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
189			return m, cmd
190		}
191		var sub tea.Model
192		switch n.To {
193		case ScreenMainMenu:
194			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
195		case ScreenBoardList:
196			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
197		case ScreenBoardView:
198			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
199		case ScreenArticleView:
200			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
201		case ScreenPostCompose:
202			// n.ArticleID, when non-zero, identifies the parent article — this
203			// path is the "Re: reply" target. screen_article_view's R key sets
204			// it; new-post entry points (e.g., board view's `p` key) leave it 0.
205			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID, n.ArticleID)
206		case ScreenWBInbox:
207			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
208		case ScreenWBCompose:
209			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
210		case ScreenWBThread:
211			sub = newWBThreadModel(m.deps, n.CounterpartyUserID)
212		case ScreenOnline:
213			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
214		case ScreenMailInbox:
215			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
216		case ScreenMailThread:
217			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
218		case ScreenMailCompose:
219			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
220		case ScreenPasswordChange:
221			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
222		case ScreenAdminUsers:
223			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
224		case ScreenArticleEdit:
225			sub = newArticleEditModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
226		case ScreenArticleExport:
227			sub = newArticleExportModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
228		case ScreenBoardSplash:
229			sub = newBoardSplashModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
230		case ScreenBoardBannerEdit:
231			sub = newBoardBannerEditModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
232		case ScreenUserSettings:
233			sub = newUserSettingsModel(m.deps)
234		case ScreenBioEdit:
235			sub = newBioEditModel(m.deps)
236		case ScreenNotifySettings:
237			sub = newNotifySettingsModel(m.deps)
238		default:
239			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
240			return m, nil
241		}
242		m.sub = sub
243		m.activeScreen = n.To
244		cmd := sub.Init()
245		if m.width > 0 {
246			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
247			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
248			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
249			if cmd2 != nil {
250				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
251			}
252		}
253		return m, cmd
254	}
255

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/messages.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14		ScreenWBThread
15		ScreenOnline
16		ScreenMailInbox
17		ScreenMailThread
18		ScreenMailCompose
19		ScreenPasswordChange
20		ScreenAdminUsers
21		ScreenArticleEdit
22		ScreenArticleExport
23		ScreenBoardSplash
24		ScreenBoardBannerEdit
25		ScreenUserSettings
26		ScreenBioEdit
27		ScreenNotifySettings
28	)
29	
30	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
31	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
32	type NavigateMsg struct {
33		To           Screen
34		BoardID      int64
35		ArticleID    int64
36		Recipient          string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
37		MailID             int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
38		MailThreadID       int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
39		CounterpartyUserID int64  // for ScreenWBThread: which 1-to-1 thread to open
40	}
41	
42	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
43	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
44	
45	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
46	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
47	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
48		ID         int64
49		FromUserID string
50		Body       string
51	}
52	
53	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
54	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
55	// re-render.
56	type PushAddedMsg struct {
57		ArticleID  int64
58		UserUserID string
59		Kind       string
60		Body       string
61	}
62	
63	// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
64	// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
65	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
66	// counter from DB.
67	type MailIncomingMsg struct {
68		ID         int64
69		ThreadID   int64
70		FromUserID string
71		Subject    string
72	}
73	
74	// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
75	// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
76	// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
77	type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
78		BoardID      int64
79		ArticleID    int64
80		AuthorUserID string
81		Title        string
82	}
83	
84	// ArticleUpdatedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when an article's
85	// title or body is edited. Article-view models filter by ArticleID and
86	// re-fetch from DB so they don't render stale text.
87	type ArticleUpdatedMsg struct {
88		ArticleID int64
89	}
90	
91	// ArticlePinChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod pins or unpins an article.
92	// Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article list so the
93	// pinned-first ordering and the [M] marker stay current across all live
94	// sessions viewing the same board. ArticleID + Pinned are advisory; the
95	// re-fetch is the source of truth.
96	type ArticlePinChangedMsg struct {
97		BoardID   int64
98		ArticleID int64
99		Pinned    bool
100	}
101	
102	// ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod changes an article's
103	// comments_mode (open / arrows_only / locked). Article-view models filter
104	// by ArticleID; board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch their list
105	// so the [鎖]/[箭] badges stay current. Mode is the new CommentsMode string;
106	// receivers should re-fetch from DB rather than trust the payload.
107	type ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg struct {
108		BoardID   int64
109		ArticleID int64
110		Mode      string // store.CommentsMode value, kept as string for tui-package isolation
111	}
112
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	)
9	
10	// userSettingsModel is the "個人設定" sub-menu reached from the main menu.
11	// Three entries: change password, edit bio, edit notification settings.
12	// Layout and key handling mirror mainMenuModel deliberately so muscle memory
13	// transfers across BBS-internal menus.
14	type userSettingsModel struct {
15		deps   Deps
16		items  []menuItem
17		cursor int
18	}
19	
20	func newUserSettingsModel(deps Deps) userSettingsModel {
21		items := []menuItem{
22			{label: "修改密碼 Change password", hint: "current → new → confirm", to: ScreenPasswordChange},
23			{label: "修改 Bio Edit bio", hint: "free-form profile blurb", to: ScreenBioEdit},
24			{label: "通知設定 Notification settings", hint: "webhook targets + per-event toggles", to: ScreenNotifySettings},
25			{label: "返回 Back", hint: "main menu", to: ScreenMainMenu},
26		}
27		return userSettingsModel{deps: deps, items: items}
28	}
29	
30	func (m userSettingsModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
31	
32	func (m userSettingsModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
33		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
34			switch k.String() {
35			case "esc", "q", "left", "h", "backspace":
36				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
37			case "Q":
38				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
39			case "up", "k":
40				if m.cursor > 0 {
41					m.cursor--
42				}
43				return m, nil
44			case "down", "j":
45				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
46					m.cursor++
47				}
48				return m, nil
49			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
50				it := m.items[m.cursor]
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
52			case "1", "2", "3", "4":
53				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
54				if idx < len(m.items) {
55					it := m.items[idx]
56					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
57				}
58			}
59		}
60		return m, nil
61	}
62	
63	func (m userSettingsModel) View() string {
64		u := m.deps.User
65		var b strings.Builder
66		b.WriteString("\n")
67		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 個人設定 User Settings "))
68		b.WriteString("\n\n")
69	
70		if u != nil {
71			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("帳號 %s · 角色 %s", u.UserID, u.Role)))
72			b.WriteString("\n")
73			bio := strings.TrimSpace(u.Bio)
74			if bio == "" {
75				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(尚未填寫 bio)"))
76			} else {
77				// Show only the first line of bio in the header so the menu
78				// itself stays compact; the bio-edit screen owns the full view.
79				firstLine := bio
80				if i := strings.IndexByte(firstLine, '\n'); i >= 0 {
81					firstLine = firstLine[:i]
82				}
83				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio: ") + Truncate(firstLine, 60))
84			}
85			b.WriteString("\n\n")
86		}
87	
88		for i, it := range m.items {
89			marker := "  "
90			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
91			if i == m.cursor {
92				marker = "▸ "
93				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-40s ", i+1, it.label))
94			}
95			b.WriteString(marker + row)
96			if it.hint != "" {
97				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
98			}
99			b.WriteString("\n")
100		}
101	
102		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-4 jump · Esc/←/h back"))
103		b.WriteString("\n")
104		return b.String()
105	}
106

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/screen_notify_settings.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
14	)
15	
16	// notifyTestResultMsg is delivered by the goroutine that ran SendTest.
17	// The screen renders msg.err in the error/flash row so the user sees
18	// the HTTP outcome inline.
19	type notifyTestResultMsg struct {
20		targetID int64
21		err      error
22	}
23	
24	// notifySettingsModel is the unified screen for the per-event toggles
25	// (user_notif_prefs) and webhook target list (user_notif_targets).
26	//
27	// Layout:
28	//
29	//   - Top: 5 boolean toggles (Push / WB / Mail / Reply / OnlyWhenOffline)
30	//     navigated with j/k, flipped with Space. Persisted via Ctrl+S.
31	//   - Bottom: target list, then "+ Add new target" entry. Targets toggle
32	//     enabled with `t`, edit with `e`, delete with `d`. Adds and edits
33	//     pop an inline two-field form (label + URL).
34	//
35	// State machine: mode == modeList during normal browsing; switches to
36	// modeEdit while the inline form is up. Mirrors the inline-textinput-
37	// inside-list pattern used by screen_wb.go.
38	type notifySettingsModel struct {
39		deps Deps
40	
41		prefs   store.NotifyPrefs
42		targets []*store.NotifyTarget
43		loadErr error
44	
45		cursor      int  // 0..4 = toggles, 5..5+N-1 = targets, 5+N = "+ add"
46		prefsDirty  bool // true if Ctrl+S would persist a change
47	
48		mode editMode
49	
50		editLabel textinput.Model
51		editURL   textinput.Model
52		editFocus int   // 0 = label, 1 = url
53		editingID int64 // 0 for new target, otherwise target.ID
54	
55		width  int
56		height int
57		err    string
58		flash  string // transient success message
59	}
60	
61	type editMode int
62	
63	const (
64		modeList editMode = iota
65		modeEdit
66	)
67	
68	const (
69		notifPrefCount = 5 // Push, WB, Mail, Reply, OnlyWhenOffline
70	)
71	
72	func newNotifySettingsModel(deps Deps) notifySettingsModel {
73		m := notifySettingsModel{deps: deps}
74		m.editLabel = textinput.New()
75		m.editLabel.Placeholder = "label (任意便於辨識的名稱)"
76		m.editLabel.CharLimit = 64
77		m.editLabel.Width = 60
78		m.editURL = textinput.New()
79		m.editURL.Placeholder = "Discord webhook URL / https://ntfy.sh/<topic> / 任何 HTTP(S) 端點"
80		m.editURL.CharLimit = 512
81		m.editURL.Width = 60
82	
83		if deps.User != nil && deps.Store != nil {
84			ctx := context.Background()
85			p, err := deps.Store.Notify().GetPrefs(ctx, deps.User.ID)
86			if err != nil {
87				m.loadErr = err
88			}
89			m.prefs = p
90			ts, err := deps.Store.Notify().ListTargets(ctx, deps.User.ID)
91			if err != nil && m.loadErr == nil {
92				m.loadErr = err
93			}
94			m.targets = ts
95		}
96		return m
97	}
98	
99	func (m notifySettingsModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
100	
101	// rowCount returns the total navigable row count: 5 prefs + N targets + 1 add.
102	func (m notifySettingsModel) rowCount() int {
103		return notifPrefCount + len(m.targets) + 1
104	}
105	
106	// addRowIndex returns the cursor index of the "+ add target" row.
107	func (m notifySettingsModel) addRowIndex() int {
108		return notifPrefCount + len(m.targets)
109	}
110	
111	func (m notifySettingsModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
112		switch msg := msg.(type) {
113		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
114			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
115			m.editLabel.Width = max(20, msg.Width-16)
116			m.editURL.Width = max(20, msg.Width-16)
117			return m, nil
118	
119		case tea.KeyMsg:
120			if m.mode == modeEdit {
121				return m.updateEdit(msg)
122			}
123			return m.updateList(msg)
124	
125		case notifyTestResultMsg:
126			if msg.err != nil {
127				m.err = fmt.Sprintf("target #%d test failed: %v", msg.targetID, msg.err)
128				m.flash = ""
129			} else {
130				m.flash = fmt.Sprintf("✓ target #%d test delivered — check your receiver", msg.targetID)
131				m.err = ""
132			}
133			return m, nil
134		}
135		return m, nil
136	}
137	
138	func (m notifySettingsModel) updateList(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
139		switch msg.String() {
140		case "esc", "left", "h", "backspace":
141			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
142		case "Q":
143			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
144		case "up", "k":
145			if m.cursor > 0 {
146				m.cursor--
147			}
148			m.err = ""
149			return m, nil
150		case "down", "j":
151			if m.cursor < m.rowCount()-1 {
152				m.cursor++
153			}
154			m.err = ""
155			return m, nil
156		case " ":
157			if m.cursor < notifPrefCount {
158				m.flipPref(m.cursor)
159				m.prefsDirty = true
160				m.err = ""
161			} else if m.cursor == m.addRowIndex() {
162				m.startAdd()
163			} else {
164				// Space on a target → toggle enabled (alias of `t`).
165				m.toggleTarget()
166			}
167			return m, nil
168		case "ctrl+s":
169			return m.savePrefs()
170		case "a":
171			m.startAdd()
172			return m, nil
173		case "t":
174			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
175				m.toggleTarget()
176			}
177			return m, nil
178		case "e":
179			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
180				m.startEdit()
181			}
182			return m, nil
183		case "d":
184			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
185				m.deleteTarget()
186			}
187			return m, nil
188		case "T":
189			// Send a synthetic test notification to the cursored target.
190			// Bypasses prefs (so users can test before flipping any toggles)
191			// and bypasses the dispatch queue (so we get a synchronous
192			// pass/fail to surface in the flash row).
193			if m.cursor >= notifPrefCount && m.cursor < m.addRowIndex() {
194				return m, m.startTest()
195			}
196			return m, nil
197		case "enter", "right", "l":
198			if m.cursor == m.addRowIndex() {
199				m.startAdd()
200				return m, nil
201			}
202			// Enter on a toggle = flip; on a target = edit.
203			if m.cursor < notifPrefCount {
204				m.flipPref(m.cursor)
205				m.prefsDirty = true
206				return m, nil
207			}
208			m.startEdit()
209			return m, nil
210		}
211		return m, nil
212	}
213	
214	func (m *notifySettingsModel) flipPref(idx int) {
215		switch idx {
216		case 0:
217			m.prefs.OnPush = !m.prefs.OnPush
218		case 1:
219			m.prefs.OnWB = !m.prefs.OnWB
220		case 2:
221			m.prefs.OnMail = !m.prefs.OnMail
222		case 3:
223			m.prefs.OnReply = !m.prefs.OnReply
224		case 4:
225			m.prefs.OnlyWhenOffline = !m.prefs.OnlyWhenOffline
226		}
227	}
228	
229	func (m notifySettingsModel) savePrefs() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
230		if m.deps.User == nil {
231			m.err = "internal error: no user"
232			return m, nil
233		}
234		if !m.prefsDirty {
235			m.flash = "(no changes)"
236			return m, nil
237		}
238		if err := m.deps.Store.Notify().SetPrefs(context.Background(), m.deps.User.ID, m.prefs); err != nil {
239			m.err = err.Error()
240			return m, nil
241		}
242		m.prefsDirty = false
243		m.flash = "✓ 已儲存事件偏好"
244		m.err = ""
245		return m, nil
246	}
247	
248	func (m *notifySettingsModel) startAdd() {
249		m.mode = modeEdit
250		m.editingID = 0
251		m.editLabel.SetValue("")
252		m.editURL.SetValue("")
253		m.editFocus = 0
254		m.editLabel.Focus()
255		m.editURL.Blur()
256		m.err = ""
257	}
258	
259	func (m *notifySettingsModel) startEdit() {
260		idx := m.cursor - notifPrefCount
261		if idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.targets) {
262			return
263		}
264		t := m.targets[idx]
265		m.mode = modeEdit
266		m.editingID = t.ID
267		m.editLabel.SetValue(t.Label)
268		m.editURL.SetValue(t.URL)
269		m.editFocus = 0
270		m.editLabel.Focus()
271		m.editURL.Blur()
272		m.err = ""
273	}
274	
275	func (m *notifySettingsModel) toggleTarget() {
276		idx := m.cursor - notifPrefCount
277		if idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.targets) {
278			return
279		}
280		t := m.targets[idx]
281		want := !t.Enabled
282		if err := m.deps.Store.Notify().SetTargetEnabled(context.Background(), t.ID, m.deps.User.ID, want); err != nil {
283			m.err = err.Error()
284			return
285		}
286		t.Enabled = want
287		if want {
288			m.flash = "✓ 已啟用 target #" + fmt.Sprint(t.ID)
289		} else {
290			m.flash = "✓ 已停用 target #" + fmt.Sprint(t.ID)
291		}
292		m.err = ""
293	}
294	
295	// startTest fires a one-shot test notification at the cursored target.
296	// Returns a tea.Cmd that runs the HTTP call in a goroutine (the
297	// dispatcher's http.Client has a 5s timeout) and rendezvous-es the
298	// result back as a notifyTestResultMsg.
299	func (m *notifySettingsModel) startTest() tea.Cmd {
300		idx := m.cursor - notifPrefCount
301		if idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.targets) {
302			return nil
303		}
304		t := m.targets[idx]
305		if m.deps.Notify == nil {
306			// Tests / register-only sessions: no dispatcher wired up. Fail
307			// loud rather than silently no-op.
308			m.err = "notify dispatcher not initialised"
309			return nil
310		}
311		m.flash = fmt.Sprintf("🔔 testing target #%d…", t.ID)
312		m.err = ""
313		// Snapshot the values the goroutine needs so we don't reach back into
314		// the model after returning (model updates are funnelled through
315		// Update by bubbletea's main loop).
316		target := *t
317		user := ""
318		if m.deps.User != nil {
319			user = m.deps.User.UserID
320		}
321		dispatcher := m.deps.Notify
322		return func() tea.Msg {
323			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 8*time.Second)
324			defer cancel()
325			ev := notify.Event{
326				Kind:       notify.KindPush, // bypassed by SendTest; here just for log readability
327				ToUserID:   target.UserID,
328				FromUserID: user,
329				Title:      "[BBS-test] notification target verified",
330				Body: fmt.Sprintf(
331					"User: %s\nTarget: #%d (%s)\nTime: %s\n\nIf you can read this, the webhook URL is wired up correctly.",
332					user, target.ID, target.Label, time.Now().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"),
333				),
334			}
335			return notifyTestResultMsg{
336				targetID: target.ID,
337				err:      dispatcher.SendTest(ctx, &target, ev),
338			}
339		}
340	}
341	
342	func (m *notifySettingsModel) deleteTarget() {
343		idx := m.cursor - notifPrefCount
344		if idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.targets) {
345			return
346		}
347		t := m.targets[idx]
348		if err := m.deps.Store.Notify().DeleteTarget(context.Background(), t.ID, m.deps.User.ID); err != nil {
349			m.err = err.Error()
350			return
351		}
352		m.targets = append(m.targets[:idx], m.targets[idx+1:]...)
353		if m.cursor >= m.rowCount() {
354			m.cursor = m.rowCount() - 1
355		}
356		m.flash = "✓ 已刪除 target"
357		m.err = ""
358	}
359	
360	func (m notifySettingsModel) updateEdit(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
361		switch msg.String() {
362		case "esc":
363			m.mode = modeList
364			m.err = ""
365			return m, nil
366		case "tab", "down":
367			m.cycleEditFocus()
368			return m, nil
369		case "shift+tab", "up":
370			m.cycleEditFocus()
371			return m, nil
372		case "enter", "ctrl+s":
373			return m.submitEdit()
374		}
375		var cmd tea.Cmd
376		if m.editFocus == 0 {
377			m.editLabel, cmd = m.editLabel.Update(msg)
378		} else {
379			m.editURL, cmd = m.editURL.Update(msg)
380		}
381		return m, cmd
382	}
383	
384	func (m *notifySettingsModel) cycleEditFocus() {
385		if m.editFocus == 0 {
386			m.editFocus = 1
387			m.editLabel.Blur()
388			m.editURL.Focus()
389		} else {
390			m.editFocus = 0
391			m.editURL.Blur()
392			m.editLabel.Focus()
393		}
394	}
395	
396	func (m notifySettingsModel) submitEdit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
397		if m.deps.User == nil {
398			m.err = "internal error: no user"
399			return m, nil
400		}
401		label := strings.TrimSpace(m.editLabel.Value())
402		url := strings.TrimSpace(m.editURL.Value())
403		if err := store.ValidateNotifyURL(url); err != nil {
404			m.err = err.Error()
405			return m, nil
406		}
407		ctx := context.Background()
408		if m.editingID == 0 {
409			// Insert new.
410			id, err := m.deps.Store.Notify().AddTarget(ctx, m.deps.User.ID, label, url)
411			if err != nil {
412				m.err = err.Error()
413				return m, nil
414			}
415			// Re-list so created_at reflects the canonical row order.
416			ts, _ := m.deps.Store.Notify().ListTargets(ctx, m.deps.User.ID)
417			m.targets = ts
418			m.flash = fmt.Sprintf("✓ 已新增 target #%d", id)
419		} else {
420			// Update existing — preserve enabled state.
421			var enabled = true
422			for _, t := range m.targets {
423				if t.ID == m.editingID {
424					enabled = t.Enabled
425					break
426				}
427			}
428			if err := m.deps.Store.Notify().UpdateTarget(ctx, m.editingID, m.deps.User.ID, label, url, enabled); err != nil {
429				m.err = err.Error()
430				return m, nil
431			}
432			ts, _ := m.deps.Store.Notify().ListTargets(ctx, m.deps.User.ID)
433			m.targets = ts
434			m.flash = fmt.Sprintf("✓ 已更新 target #%d", m.editingID)
435		}
436		m.mode = modeList
437		m.editingID = 0
438		m.err = ""
439		return m, nil
440	}
441	
442	func (m notifySettingsModel) View() string {
443		var b strings.Builder
444		b.WriteString("\n")
445		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 通知設定 Notification settings "))
446		b.WriteString("\n\n")
447	
448		if m.loadErr != nil {
449			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
450		}
451	
452		// Section 1: prefs toggles.
453		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("事件 Events") + "\n")
454		prefsRows := []struct {
455			label string
456			on    bool
457			note  string
458		}{
459			{"推/噓 我的文章 (push)", m.prefs.OnPush, "someone pushed/booed my article"},
460			{"收到水球 (wb)", m.prefs.OnWB, "私訊 DM"},
461			{"收到站內信 (mail)", m.prefs.OnMail, "persistent threaded mail"},
462			{"有人回文 Re: (reply)", m.prefs.OnReply, "someone posted a Re: reply on my article"},
463			{"僅在離線時通知 only-when-offline", m.prefs.OnlyWhenOffline, "skip dispatch when I have a live session"},
464		}
465		for i, r := range prefsRows {
466			marker := "  "
467			check := "[ ]"
468			if r.on {
469				check = "[x]"
470			}
471			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %d. %s", check, i+1, r.label)
472			if i == m.cursor && m.mode == modeList {
473				marker = "▸ "
474				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %d. %-44s", check, i+1, r.label))
475			}
476			b.WriteString(marker + row + "  " + StyleDim.Render(r.note) + "\n")
477		}
478	
479		if m.prefsDirty {
480			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(prefs unsaved — Ctrl+S to persist)") + "\n")
481		}
482	
483		// Section 2: target list.
484		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render("通知目標 Webhook targets") + "\n")
485		if len(m.targets) == 0 {
486			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("  (尚未設定 target — 按 a 新增)") + "\n")
487		} else {
488			for i, t := range m.targets {
489				rowIdx := notifPrefCount + i
490				marker := "  "
491				check := "[×]"
492				if t.Enabled {
493					check = "[✓]"
494				}
495				label := t.Label
496				if label == "" {
497					label = "(no label)"
498				}
499				row := fmt.Sprintf("  %s #%d %s  %s", check, t.ID, PadRight(Truncate(label, 24), 24), Truncate(t.URL, 60))
500				if rowIdx == m.cursor && m.mode == modeList {
501					marker = "▸ "
502					row = StyleHighlight.Render(row)
503				}
504				b.WriteString(marker + row + "\n")
505			}
506		}
507	
508		// Add row.
509		addRow := "  + 新增 target Add new target"
510		if m.addRowIndex() == m.cursor && m.mode == modeList {
511			b.WriteString("▸ " + StyleHighlight.Render(addRow) + "\n")
512		} else {
513			b.WriteString("  " + addRow + "\n")
514		}
515	
516		// Inline edit form.
517		if m.mode == modeEdit {
518			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHeader.Render(" Webhook target ") + "\n\n")
519			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Label:") + "\n  " + m.editLabel.View() + "\n\n")
520			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("URL (http:// or https://):") + "\n  " + m.editURL.View() + "\n")
521			if m.err != "" {
522				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
523			}
524			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab cycle field · Enter/Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel"))
525			b.WriteString("\n")
526			return b.String()
527		}
528	
529		if m.err != "" {
530			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
531		}
532		if m.flash != "" {
533			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleSuccess.Render(m.flash))
534		}
535		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("j/k move · Space toggle/select · Ctrl+S save prefs · a 新增 · e 編輯 · t 啟停 · T 測試 · d 刪除 · Esc back"))
536		b.WriteString("\n")
537		return b.String()
538	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/pushes.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// ErrPushNotFound is returned by PushRepo lookups (Delete, future GetByID)
12	// when the row is missing. ErrPermissionDenied is shared with articles.
13	var ErrPushNotFound = errors.New("push not found")
14	
15	// ErrCommentsLocked / ErrCommentsArrowsOnly are returned by PushRepo.Create
16	// when the parent article's comments_mode rejects the requested kind. The
17	// store-side check is the authoritative gate (TUI also gates early for nicer
18	// errors, but multiple sessions can race against a mode change so the store
19	// is the source of truth). See ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.
20	var (
21		ErrCommentsLocked     = errors.New("comments are locked on this article")
22		ErrCommentsArrowsOnly = errors.New("only arrow comments are allowed on this article")
23	)
24	
25	type PushKind string
26	
27	const (
28		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
29		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
30		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
31	)
32	
33	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
34		switch k {
35		case PushKindPush:
36			return "推"
37		case PushKindBoo:
38			return "噓"
39		case PushKindArrow:
40			return "→"
41		}
42		return "?"
43	}
44	
45	type Push struct {
46		ID          int64
47		ArticleID   int64
48		UserID      int64
49		UserUserID  string
50		Kind        PushKind
51		Body        string
52		CreatedAt   time.Time
53	}
54	
55	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
56	
57	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
58	
59	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
60		p := &Push{}
61		var kind string
62		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
63			return nil, err
64		}
65		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
66		return p, nil
67	}
68	
69	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
70		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
71			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
72		)
73		if err != nil {
74			return nil, err
75		}
76		defer rows.Close()
77		var out []*Push
78		for rows.Next() {
79			p, err := scanPush(rows)
80			if err != nil {
81				return nil, err
82			}
83			out = append(out, p)
84		}
85		return out, rows.Err()
86	}
87	
88	// scoreDelta returns the recommend_score contribution of a push of the
89	// given kind. Positive on add; negate on delete. Centralized so Create
90	// and Delete agree on the algebra.
91	func scoreDelta(kind PushKind) (int, error) {
92		switch kind {
93		case PushKindPush:
94			return 1, nil
95		case PushKindBoo:
96			return -1, nil
97		case PushKindArrow:
98			return 0, nil
99		}
100		return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
101	}
102	
103	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the
104	// article (push: +1, boo: -1, arrow: 0). Both writes happen under the
105	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
106	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
107	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
108		delta, err := scoreDelta(kind)
109		if err != nil {
110			return nil, err
111		}
112	
113		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
114		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
115		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, err
118		}
119		defer tx.Rollback()
120	
121		// Gate on the parent article's comments_mode inside the same tx; with
122		// writeMu held, the SELECT sees the latest committed value. This is the
123		// authoritative check — TUI gates early for nicer errors but races
124		// between sessions are resolved here.
125		var modeStr string
126		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
127			`SELECT comments_mode FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
128		).Scan(&modeStr)
129		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
130			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
131		}
132		if err != nil {
133			return nil, err
134		}
135		switch CommentsMode(modeStr) {
136		case CommentsModeLocked:
137			return nil, ErrCommentsLocked
138		case CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
139			if kind != PushKindArrow {
140				return nil, ErrCommentsArrowsOnly
141			}
142		}
143	
144		res, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
145			`INSERT INTO pushes (article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
146			articleID, userID, userUserID, string(kind), body,
147		)
148		if err != nil {
149			return nil, err
150		}
151		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
152		if err != nil {
153			return nil, err
154		}
155		if delta != 0 {
156			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
157				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`,
158				delta, articleID,
159			); err != nil {
160				return nil, err
161			}
162		}
163		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
164			return nil, err
165		}
166	
167		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, id)
168		return scanPush(row)
169	}
170	
171	// Delete hard-deletes a push and reverts its score contribution on the
172	// parent article. Permission: requester must be the push author OR have
173	// a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrPermissionDenied / ErrPushNotFound.
174	// Mirrors Create's writeMu+tx discipline so concurrent deletes can't
175	// double-revert the score.
176	func (r *PushRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, pushID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
177		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
178		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
179		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
180		if err != nil {
181			return err
182		}
183		defer tx.Rollback()
184	
185		var authorID, articleID int64
186		var kindStr string
187		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
188			`SELECT user_id, article_id, kind FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID,
189		).Scan(&authorID, &articleID, &kindStr)
190		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
191			return ErrPushNotFound
192		}
193		if err != nil {
194			return err
195		}
196		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
197			return ErrPermissionDenied
198		}
199		delta, err := scoreDelta(PushKind(kindStr))
200		if err != nil {
201			return err
202		}
203		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID); err != nil {
204			return err
205		}
206		if delta != 0 {
207			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
208				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score - ? WHERE id = ?`,
209				delta, articleID,
210			); err != nil {
211				return err
212			}
213		}
214		return tx.Commit()
215	}
216
