 `./internal/markdown/markdown.go`

```text
1	// Package markdown is the round-trip serializer/parser for articles.
2	//
3	// An article (with optional pushes) renders to a self-describing
4	// human-readable document with YAML-ish frontmatter:
5	//
6	//	---
7	//	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
8	//	board: Welcome
9	//	author: admin
10	//	created_at: 2026-04-30T12:34:56Z
11	//	updated_at: 2026-04-30T13:00:00Z
12	//	score: 0
13	//	id: 1
14	//	---
15	//
16	//	[arbitrary body — markdown or plain text]
17	//
18	//	<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->
19	//	- 推 [alice] 2026-04-30T12:35:01Z  great post
20	//	- 噓 [bob]   2026-04-30T12:35:14Z  disagree
21	//	- → [carol]  2026-04-30T12:35:20Z  fyi
22	//
23	// The format is intentionally narrow: each frontmatter line is
24	// `key: value`, no nesting, no quoting. Push lines are
25	// `- <glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>` with a TWO-space separator
26	// between timestamp and body.
27	//
28	// The package is a leaf — pure functions over store types, no DB access.
29	package markdown
30	
31	import (
32		"fmt"
33		"strconv"
34		"strings"
35		"time"
36	
37		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
38	)
39	
40	const (
41		frontmatterDelim = "---"
42	
43		// PushesSentinel marks the start of the pushes block. Public so callers
44		// (UI, tests, docs) can warn users not to embed this exact string in
45		// their article body. If a body contains the sentinel, Format will
46		// suffix it with the literal-marker so Parse can restore the original.
47		PushesSentinel = "<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->"
48	
49		// pushesLiteralMarker is appended to a sentinel that legitimately
50		// appears inside the body. Parse strips it back out.
51		pushesLiteralMarker = "<!-- sshbbs:literal -->"
52	)
53	
54	// FormatOpts controls the output of Format.
55	type FormatOpts struct {
56		// IncludePushes appends the pushes section after the body.
57		IncludePushes bool
58		// BoardName is rendered into the frontmatter. Pass the board name
59		// (the package can't look it up — store types don't carry it).
60		BoardName string
61	}
62	
63	// Parsed is the round-trip target. It deliberately does NOT return a
64	// *store.Article because Parse runs without DB context: the caller must
65	// resolve BoardName → BoardID and AuthorName → AuthorID before INSERT.
66	type Parsed struct {
67		Title      string
68		BoardName  string
69		AuthorName string
70		CreatedAt  time.Time
71		UpdatedAt  time.Time
72		ScoreHint  int64
73		IDHint     int64
74		Pinned     bool
75		Body       string
76		Pushes     []ParsedPush
77		// Extra preserves any frontmatter key we don't recognize, so future
78		// schema additions don't drop data on a round-trip through an older
79		// build.
80		Extra map[string]string
81	}
82	
83	// ParsedPush mirrors store.Push but with author as a name (not user_id) and
84	// without a DB-side ID; the caller decides whether/how to materialize it.
85	type ParsedPush struct {
86		Kind      store.PushKind
87		Author    string
88		CreatedAt time.Time
89		Body      string
90	}
91	
92	// Format renders an article (plus optional pushes) to the canonical text
93	// form. Returns an error only on a nil article; otherwise it always
94	// produces a valid, parseable document.
95	func Format(a *store.Article, pushes []*store.Push, opts FormatOpts) (string, error) {
96		if a == nil {
97			return "", fmt.Errorf("markdown.Format: article is nil")
98		}
99		var b strings.Builder
100		b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n")
101		writeFrontmatter(&b, "title", sanitizeFM(a.Title))
102		if opts.BoardName != "" {
103			writeFrontmatter(&b, "board", sanitizeFM(opts.BoardName))
104		}
105		if a.AuthorUserID != "" {
106			writeFrontmatter(&b, "author", sanitizeFM(a.AuthorUserID))
107		}
108		if !a.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
109			writeFrontmatter(&b, "created_at", a.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
110		}
111		if a.UpdatedAt.Valid {
112			writeFrontmatter(&b, "updated_at", a.UpdatedAt.Time.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
113		}
114		writeFrontmatter(&b, "score", strconv.FormatInt(a.RecommendScore, 10))
115		if a.ID > 0 {
116			writeFrontmatter(&b, "id", strconv.FormatInt(a.ID, 10))
117		}
118		if a.PinnedAt.Valid {
119			writeFrontmatter(&b, "pinned", "true")
120		}
121		b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n\n")
122	
123		body := a.Body
124		// Disambiguate any literal sentinel inside the body so Parse won't
125		// mis-cleave at it. Best-effort: if the user already has the
126		// literal-marker after the sentinel, we leave it alone.
127		if strings.Contains(body, PushesSentinel) {
128			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00")
129			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
130			body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00", PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
131		}
132		b.WriteString(body)
133		if !strings.HasSuffix(body, "\n") {
134			b.WriteString("\n")
135		}
136	
137		if opts.IncludePushes && len(pushes) > 0 {
138			b.WriteString("\n" + PushesSentinel + "\n")
139			for _, p := range pushes {
140				ts := p.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
141				// Push body is single-line by DB convention; flatten just in case.
142				pb := strings.ReplaceAll(p.Body, "\n", " ")
143				fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s [%s] %s  %s\n", p.Kind.Glyph(), p.UserUserID, ts, pb)
144			}
145		}
146	
147		return b.String(), nil
148	}
149	
150	// Parse reads markdown back into a Parsed. Permissive: missing frontmatter
151	// or pushes section degrade to zero values; the whole input becomes Body
152	// when no frontmatter is present.
153	func Parse(md string) (*Parsed, error) {
154		p := &Parsed{Extra: map[string]string{}}
155		rest := md
156	
157		if strings.HasPrefix(rest, frontmatterDelim+"\n") {
158			rest = rest[len(frontmatterDelim)+1:]
159			closeIdx := strings.Index(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n")
160			closeAtEOF := false
161			if closeIdx < 0 && strings.HasSuffix(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim) {
162				closeIdx = len(rest) - len("\n"+frontmatterDelim)
163				closeAtEOF = true
164			}
165			if closeIdx >= 0 {
166				fm := rest[:closeIdx]
167				if closeAtEOF {
168					rest = ""
169				} else {
170					rest = rest[closeIdx+len("\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n"):]
171				}
172				parseFrontmatter(p, fm)
173			}
174		}
175	
176		rest = strings.TrimLeft(rest, "\n")
177		if strings.Contains(rest, PushesSentinel) {
178			// The sentinel may be followed by literal-marker, which means it
179			// belongs to the body. Walk past every literal-marked occurrence.
180			head := rest
181			offset := 0
182			for {
183				i := strings.Index(head, PushesSentinel)
184				if i < 0 {
185					// Fell through without a real sentinel — entire rest is body.
186					p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
187					return p, nil
188				}
189				afterSentinel := i + len(PushesSentinel)
190				if strings.HasPrefix(head[afterSentinel:], pushesLiteralMarker) {
191					// Skip — it's part of the body. Continue searching.
192					next := afterSentinel + len(pushesLiteralMarker)
193					offset += next
194					head = head[next:]
195					continue
196				}
197				// Real sentinel — split.
198				cut := offset + i
199				body := strings.TrimRight(rest[:cut], "\n")
200				p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(body)
201				parsePushes(p, rest[cut+len(PushesSentinel):])
202				return p, nil
203			}
204		}
205		p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
206		return p, nil
207	}
208	
209	func writeFrontmatter(b *strings.Builder, key, val string) {
210		b.WriteString(key)
211		b.WriteString(": ")
212		b.WriteString(val)
213		b.WriteString("\n")
214	}
215	
216	func sanitizeFM(s string) string {
217		// Frontmatter values are single-line. CR/LF would break the parser;
218		// flatten to space.
219		s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\r", " ")
220		s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\n", " ")
221		return s
222	}
223	
224	func parseFrontmatter(p *Parsed, fm string) {
225		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(fm, "\n") {
226			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
227			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
228				continue
229			}
230			idx := strings.IndexByte(line, ':')
231			if idx <= 0 {
232				continue
233			}
234			key := strings.TrimSpace(line[:idx])
235			val := strings.TrimSpace(line[idx+1:])
236			switch key {
237			case "title":
238				p.Title = val
239			case "board":
240				p.BoardName = val
241			case "author":
242				p.AuthorName = val
243			case "created_at":
244				if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
245					p.CreatedAt = t
246				}
247			case "updated_at":
248				if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
249					p.UpdatedAt = t
250				}
251			case "score":
252				if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
253					p.ScoreHint = n
254				}
255			case "id":
256				if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
257					p.IDHint = n
258				}
259			case "pinned":
260				p.Pinned = val == "true" || val == "1"
261			default:
262				p.Extra[key] = val
263			}
264		}
265	}
266	
267	func parsePushes(p *Parsed, block string) {
268		for line := range strings.SplitSeq(block, "\n") {
269			line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
270			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
271			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "- ") {
272				continue
273			}
274			if push, ok := parsePushLine(line[2:]); ok {
275				p.Pushes = append(p.Pushes, push)
276			}
277		}
278	}
279	
280	func parsePushLine(s string) (ParsedPush, bool) {
281		// Format: "<glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>"
282		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
283		if s == "" {
284			return ParsedPush{}, false
285		}
286	
287		// Glyph: take everything before the first whitespace.
288		wsIdx := strings.IndexAny(s, " \t")
289		if wsIdx <= 0 {
290			return ParsedPush{}, false
291		}
292		glyph := s[:wsIdx]
293		rest := strings.TrimLeft(s[wsIdx:], " \t")
294	
295		var kind store.PushKind
296		switch glyph {
297		case "推":
298			kind = store.PushKindPush
299		case "噓":
300			kind = store.PushKindBoo
301		case "→":
302			kind = store.PushKindArrow
303		default:
304			return ParsedPush{}, false
305		}
306	
307		if !strings.HasPrefix(rest, "[") {
308			return ParsedPush{}, false
309		}
310		closeBracket := strings.IndexByte(rest, ']')
311		if closeBracket <= 1 {
312			return ParsedPush{}, false
313		}
314		author := rest[1:closeBracket]
315		rest = strings.TrimLeft(rest[closeBracket+1:], " \t")
316	
317		// Timestamp + body separated by 2 spaces. If absent, fall back to one
318		// space (lossy but tolerant of hand-edited files).
319		var tsStr, body string
320		if sepIdx := strings.Index(rest, "  "); sepIdx >= 0 {
321			tsStr = rest[:sepIdx]
322			body = strings.TrimLeft(rest[sepIdx:], " \t")
323		} else if spaceIdx := strings.IndexAny(rest, " \t"); spaceIdx >= 0 {
324			tsStr = rest[:spaceIdx]
325			body = strings.TrimLeft(rest[spaceIdx:], " \t")
326		} else {
327			tsStr = rest
328			body = ""
329		}
330	
331		var ts time.Time
332		if tsStr != "" {
333			if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, tsStr); err == nil {
334				ts = t
335			}
336		}
337		return ParsedPush{Kind: kind, Author: author, CreatedAt: ts, Body: body}, true
338	}
339	
340	func stripLiteralMarkers(s string) string {
341		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker, PushesSentinel)
342	}
343
```

555	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
556		if m.loadErr != nil {
557			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
558		}
559		if m.article == nil {
560			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
561		}
562		a := m.article
563	
564		var b strings.Builder
565		b.WriteString("\n")
566		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
567		b.WriteString("\n\n")
568	
569		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
570		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
571		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
572		switch a.CommentsMode {
573		case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
574			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("留言:  ") + StyleError.Render("[箭] 僅開放箭頭") + "\n")
575		case store.CommentsModeLocked:
576			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("留言:  ") + StyleError.Render("[鎖] 已關閉留言") + "\n")
577		}
578		b.WriteString("\n")
579	
580		body := m.rendered
581		if body == "" {
582			body = a.Body
583		}
584		bodyLines := strings.Split(body, "\n")
585		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
586		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
587		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
588		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
589			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
590		}
591		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
592			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
593			b.WriteString("\n")
594		}
595	
596		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
597			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
598			for i, p := range m.pushes {
599				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
600				gutter := "  "
601				if i == m.pushCursor {
602					gutter = "▸ "
603				}
604				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
605					gutter,
606					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
607					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
608					p.Body,
609					StyleDim.Render(ts),
610				)
611				if i == m.pushCursor {
612					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
613				}
614				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
615			}
616		}
617	
618		if m.pushing {
619			b.WriteString("\n  ")
620			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
621			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
622			b.WriteString("\n")
623			if m.err != "" {
624				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
625			}
626			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
627		} else if m.pendingDelete {
628			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
629			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
630				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
631			}
632			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
633		} else if m.pickingCommentsMode {
634			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHeader.Render(" 留言模式 "))
635			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("1 開放  2 僅箭頭  3 鎖文  Esc 取消"))
636		} else {
637			help := "j/k scroll · y 匯出"
638			if m.canPush() {
639				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = → · r 回文"
640			}
641			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
642				help += " · p/P 選推文"
643			}
644			if m.canEditArticle() {
645				help += " · E 編輯"
646			}
647			if m.canSetCommentsMode() {
648				help += " · M 留言模式"
649			}
650			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
651				help += " · D 刪除推文"
652			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
653				help += " · D 刪除文章"
654			}
655			help += " · ? help · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
656			if m.err != "" {
657				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
658			}
659			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
660		}
661		b.WriteString("\n")
662		return b.String()
663	}
664	
665	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
666		switch k {
667		case store.PushKindPush:
668			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
669		case store.PushKindBoo:
670			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
671		case store.PushKindArrow:
672			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
673		}
674		return "?"
675	}
676	
677	// kindLabel renders a short verb for push notifications. Mirrors the
678	// renderPushKind glyphs (推 / 噓 / →) but as plain text so it survives
679	// non-color webhooks (Discord embeds, plain SMS, etc.).
680	func kindLabel(k store.PushKind) string {
681		switch k {
682		case store.PushKindPush:
683			return "推"
684		case store.PushKindBoo:
685			return "噓"
686		case store.PushKindArrow:
687			return "→"
688		}
689		return "?"
690	}

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5	
6		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
7	)
8	
9	// notifyWB fires a KindWB event for a delivered water balloon. Skipped when
10	// the dispatcher is nil (tests / register-only sessions). Self-WB is
11	// expected to be filtered at the call site (see CLAUDE.md: self-WB is a
12	// memo and intentionally bypasses Broker.Send).
13	func notifyWB(deps Deps, toUserID int64, fromUserID, body string) {
14		if deps.Notify == nil {
15			return
16		}
17		deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
18			Kind:       notify.KindWB,
19			ToUserID:   toUserID,
20			FromUserID: fromUserID,
21			Title:      fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s 丟了水球給你", fromUserID),
22			Body:       body,
23		})
24	}
25	
26	// notifyMail fires a KindMail event for delivered mail. Self-mail is
27	// expected to be filtered at the call site.
28	func notifyMail(deps Deps, toUserID int64, fromUserID, subject, body string) {
29		if deps.Notify == nil {
30			return
31		}
32		// Cap the body excerpt — mail body can be 4 KB and most webhook
33		// receivers truncate aggressively anyway.
34		excerpt := body
35		if len(excerpt) > 280 {
36			excerpt = excerpt[:280] + "…"
37		}
38		deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
39			Kind:       notify.KindMail,
40			ToUserID:   toUserID,
41			FromUserID: fromUserID,
42			Title:      fmt.Sprintf("[BBS] %s 寄信給你: %s", fromUserID, subject),
43			Body:       excerpt,
44		})
45	}
46

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
11	)
12	
13	// bioEditModel is a single-textarea form screen reached from the user
14	// settings menu. Form-screen convention (CLAUDE.md): no h/l navigation —
15	// those keys must remain available for text editing.
16	type bioEditModel struct {
17		deps    Deps
18		body    textarea.Model
19		width   int
20		height  int
21		err     string
22		success bool
23	}
24	
25	func newBioEditModel(deps Deps) bioEditModel {
26		ta := textarea.New()
27		ta.Placeholder = "你的 bio…（最多 1024 字元，可換行）"
28		ta.CharLimit = 4096 // generous client-side; auth.ValidateBio is the source of truth (rune count)
29		ta.SetWidth(72)
30		ta.SetHeight(10)
31		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Bio != "" {
32			ta.SetValue(deps.User.Bio)
33		}
34		ta.Focus()
35		return bioEditModel{deps: deps, body: ta}
36	}
37	
38	func (m bioEditModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textarea.Blink }
39	
40	func (m bioEditModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
41		switch msg := msg.(type) {
42		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
43			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
44			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-8))
45			m.body.SetHeight(max(8, msg.Height-12))
46			return m, nil
47	
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			if m.success {
50				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
51			}
52			switch msg.String() {
53			case "esc":
54				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
55			case "ctrl+s":
56				return m.submit()
57			}
58		}
59		var cmd tea.Cmd
60		m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
61		return m, cmd
62	}
63	
64	func (m bioEditModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
65		bio := strings.TrimRight(m.body.Value(), "\n")
66		if err := auth.ValidateBio(bio); err != nil {
67			m.err = err.Error()
68			return m, nil
69		}
70		if m.deps.User == nil {
71			m.err = "internal error: no user"
72			return m, nil
73		}
74		ctx := context.Background()
75		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().SetBio(ctx, m.deps.User.ID, bio); err != nil {
76			m.err = err.Error()
77			return m, nil
78		}
79		// Refresh User in place so the settings screen header and any future
80		// reads see the canonical bio without a re-login.
81		if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
82			*m.deps.User = *fresh
83		}
84		m.success = true
85		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
86	}
87	
88	func (m bioEditModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 修改 Bio Edit bio "))
92		b.WriteString("\n\n")
93	
94		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio (free-form, ≤ 1024 chars; newlines OK):") + "\n")
95		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
96	
97		if m.err != "" {
98			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
99		}
100		if m.success {
101			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ saved"))
102		}
103		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel"))
104		b.WriteString("\n")
105		return b.String()
106	}
107
```
</details></tool-use>

201	// helpGlobals are the cross-screen keys shown on every help page so users
202	// don't need to discover them per screen.
203	var helpGlobals = []HelpEntry{
204		{"?", "show this help (any key dismisses)"},
205		{"Ctrl+U", "open 水球 inbox (logged-in only)"},
206		{"Ctrl+C", "disconnect"},
207	}
208	
209	// isFormScreen reports whether a screen is a text-entry form whose
210	// textinput / textarea must receive '?' as a literal character. The help
211	// overlay is suppressed on these screens; the bottom help-line each form
212	// already renders is the source of truth there.
213	func isFormScreen(s Screen) bool {
214		switch s {
215		case ScreenPostCompose,
216			ScreenWBCompose,
217			ScreenMailCompose,
218			ScreenArticleEdit,
219			ScreenBoardBannerEdit,
220			ScreenPasswordChange,
221			ScreenBioEdit:
222			return true
223		}
224		return false
225	}
226	
227	// helpAvailable reports whether the help overlay should respond to '?' on
228	// this screen. False for form screens and during register/MustChangePassword
229	// (those flows start before any Screen const is meaningful).
230	func helpAvailable(deps Deps, s Screen) bool {
231		if deps.IsRegister || deps.MustChangePassword {
232			return false
233		}
234		return !isFormScreen(s)
235	}
236	
237	// helpBox is the lipgloss frame around the help body. Width is set per-call
238	// so the box hugs the rendered content rather than spanning the terminal.
239	var helpBox = lipgloss.NewStyle().
240		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
241		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("63")).
242		Padding(1, 2)
243	
244	// renderHelp builds the full-screen help overlay for the given screen.
245	// Includes per-screen entries (or a fallback message) plus the globals.
246	func renderHelp(s Screen) string {
247		var b strings.Builder
248		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 鍵盤捷徑說明 Help ") + "\n\n")
249	
250		sections, ok := screenHelp[s]
251		if !ok {
252			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no per-screen keymap registered for this screen)") + "\n\n")
253		} else {
254			for i, sec := range sections {
255				if i > 0 {
256					b.WriteString("\n")
257				}
258				if sec.Heading != "" {
259					b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  "+sec.Heading) + "\n")
260				}
261				for _, e := range sec.Entries {
262					b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
263				}
264			}
265			b.WriteString("\n")
266		}
267	
268		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  Global") + "\n")
269		for _, e := range helpGlobals {
270			b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
271		}
272	
273		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to dismiss"))
274	
275		return helpBox.Render(b.String())
276	}
277	
278	// formatHelpRow pads keys to a stable column width so the descriptions
279	// align — uses the runewidth-aware PadRight because keys often contain
280	// arrows / CJK glyphs.
281	func formatHelpRow(e HelpEntry) string {
282		const keysW = 22
283		return fmt.Sprintf("    %s  %s", PadRight(e.Keys, keysW), e.Desc)
284	}

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
10	)
11	
12	type menuItem struct {
13		label string
14		hint  string
15		to    Screen
16	}
17	
18	type mainMenuModel struct {
19		deps   Deps
20		items  []menuItem
21		cursor int
22	}
23	
24	func newMainMenuModel(deps Deps) mainMenuModel {
25		items := []menuItem{
26			{label: "看板列表 Boards", hint: "browse and read articles", to: ScreenBoardList},
27			{label: "水球 Water Balloons", hint: "private messages with online users", to: ScreenWBInbox},
28			{label: "線上使用者 Online", hint: "see who's logged in", to: ScreenOnline},
29			{label: "信箱 Mail", hint: "persistent threaded mail", to: ScreenMailInbox},
30			{label: "個人設定 User settings", hint: "password / bio / notifications", to: ScreenUserSettings},
31		}
32		// Admin gets an extra entry; positioned before the Quit row so the
33		// numeric shortcut for Quit always lands on the LAST item regardless
34		// of role (the "5 quits" shortcut becomes "6 quits" for admin).
35		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
36			items = append(items, menuItem{
37				label: "管理 Admin",
38				hint:  "manage user roles",
39				to:    ScreenAdminUsers,
40			})
41		}
42		items = append(items, menuItem{label: "離線 Quit", hint: "disconnect", to: -1})
43		return mainMenuModel{deps: deps, items: items}
44	}
45	
46	func (m mainMenuModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
47	
48	func (m mainMenuModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
49		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
50			switch k.String() {
51			case "q", "esc":
52				return m, tea.Quit
53			case "up", "k":
54				if m.cursor > 0 {
55					m.cursor--
56				}
57				return m, nil
58			case "down", "j":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62				return m, nil
63			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
64				it := m.items[m.cursor]
65				if it.to == -1 {
66					return m, tea.Quit
67				}
68				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
69			case "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7":
70				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
71				if idx < len(m.items) {
72					it := m.items[idx]
73					if it.to == -1 {
74						return m, tea.Quit
75					}
76					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
77				}
78			}
79		}
80		return m, nil
81	}
82	
83	func (m mainMenuModel) View() string {
84		u := m.deps.User
85		nick := u.Nickname
86		if nick == "" {
87			nick = u.UserID
88		}
89	
90		var b strings.Builder
91		b.WriteString("\n")
92		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" SSH-BBS · %s (%s) ", nick, u.UserID)))
93		b.WriteString("\n\n")
94	
95		if u.LastLoginAt.Valid {
96			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("上次登入 %s · 累計登入 %d 次 · 發文 %d 篇",
97				u.LastLoginAt.Time.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
98				u.NumLogins,
99				u.NumPosts,
100			)))
101			b.WriteString("\n\n")
102		}
103	
104		b.WriteString("  ")
105		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 主選單 Main Menu "))
106		b.WriteString("\n\n")
107	
108		for i, it := range m.items {
109			marker := "  "
110			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
111			if i == m.cursor {
112				marker = "▸ "
113				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-32s ", i+1, it.label))
114			}
115			b.WriteString(marker + row)
116			if it.hint != "" {
117				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
118			}
119			b.WriteString("\n")
120		}
121	
122		b.WriteString("\n  ")
123		b.WriteString(StyleHelp.Render(fmt.Sprintf("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-%d jump · ? help · q quit", len(m.items))))
124		b.WriteString("\n")
125		return b.String()
126	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/migrations/0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`

```text
1	-- User settings (bio) plus a per-user notification system.
2	-- bio is a free-form profile blurb. Empty default keeps existing rows valid.
3	-- user_notif_prefs holds the four event toggles (push / wb / mail / reply)
4	-- plus only_when_offline. Row is upserted lazily; absence ≡ all-default.
5	-- user_notif_targets stores per-user webhook URLs that the dispatcher
6	-- POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to. Compatible with caronc/apprise-api's
7	-- /notify/<key> endpoint and any other simple webhook (Discord, ntfy.sh,
8	-- Slack, custom). The BBS deliberately does NOT parse apprise:// URLs —
9	-- that is delegated to whatever webhook target the user points us at.
10	
11	ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';
12	
13	CREATE TABLE user_notif_prefs (
14	    user_id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
15	    on_push           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 推/噓/→ on my article
16	    on_wb             INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 水球
17	    on_mail           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 站內信

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"regexp"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode/utf8"
7	
8		"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
9	)
10	
11	// PadRight pads s with spaces on the right so its display width equals w.
12	// If s is wider than w, it's truncated. Critical for table-like rendering
13	// with mixed ASCII + CJK strings — lipgloss's automatic padding miscounts
14	// double-width glyphs in some terminals.
15	func PadRight(s string, w int) string {
16		if w <= 0 {
17			return ""
18		}
19		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
20		if cw == w {
21			return s
22		}
23		if cw > w {
24			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
25		}
26		return s + strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw)
27	}
28	
29	// PadLeft is PadRight's mirror, padding before the string.
30	func PadLeft(s string, w int) string {
31		if w <= 0 {
32			return ""
33		}
34		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
35		if cw == w {
36			return s
37		}
38		if cw > w {
39			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
40		}
41		return strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw) + s
42	}
43	
44	// Truncate cuts s to display-width w, appending "…" if truncation occurred.
45	func Truncate(s string, w int) string {
46		if runewidth.StringWidth(s) <= w {
47			return s
48		}
49		if w <= 1 {
50			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
51		}
52		return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "…")
53	}
54	
55	// Width returns the display width of s.
56	func Width(s string) int { return runewidth.StringWidth(s) }
57	
58	// ansiCSIRe matches a CSI escape sequence: ESC '[' optional-params letter.
59	// SGR (color), cursor moves, and most other terminal control codes used by
60	// banners follow this shape. Other escape kinds (OSC, DCS) are left
61	// untouched — banners almost never carry them.
62	var ansiCSIRe = regexp.MustCompile("\x1b\\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]")
63	
64	// stripCSI returns s with CSI escape sequences removed. Use it for any
65	// width / column calculation that involves banner content (or any other
66	// string that may carry inline color codes). The four helpers above
67	// intentionally do NOT call this — they're the article-list table path
68	// where input is plain text, and stripping every line would just be
69	// overhead.
70	func stripCSI(s string) string {
71		return ansiCSIRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
72	}
73	
74	// bannerVisualWidth returns the display width of s with CSI escapes
75	// excluded from the count.
76	func bannerVisualWidth(s string) int {
77		return runewidth.StringWidth(stripCSI(s))
78	}
79	
80	// bannerTruncate clips s to display-width w, preserving inline CSI runs
81	// (color codes are zero-width). On overflow the result ends with "…"
82	// (which counts toward the budget). If the input contained any CSI, the
83	// result is suffixed with `\x1b[0m` so a forgotten reset doesn't bleed
84	// into subsequent terminal output.
85	func bannerTruncate(s string, w int) string {
86		if w <= 0 {
87			return ""
88		}
89		if bannerVisualWidth(s) <= w {
90			return ensureANSIReset(s)
91		}
92		budget := w - 1 // reserve one cell for "…"
93		var out strings.Builder
94		visible := 0
95		cursor := 0
96		for _, loc := range ansiCSIRe.FindAllStringIndex(s, -1) {
97			if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:loc[0]], &visible, budget) {
98				out.WriteString("…")
99				return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
100			}
101			out.WriteString(s[loc[0]:loc[1]])
102			cursor = loc[1]
103		}
104		if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:], &visible, budget) {
105			out.WriteString("…")
106			return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
107		}
108		out.WriteString("…")
109		return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
110	}
111	
112	func writeBodyUpToBudget(out *strings.Builder, body string, visible *int, budget int) bool {
113		for i := 0; i < len(body); {
114			r, size := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(body[i:])
115			rw := runewidth.RuneWidth(r)
116			if *visible+rw > budget {
117				return false
118			}
119			out.WriteRune(r)
120			*visible += rw
121			i += size
122		}
123		return true
124	}
125	
126	func ensureANSIReset(s string) string {
127		if strings.Contains(s, "\x1b[") && !strings.HasSuffix(s, "\x1b[0m") {
128			return s + "\x1b[0m"
129		}
130		return s
131	}
132
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
160	// SendTest synchronously delivers one notification to a single target,
161	// bypassing both user prefs and the dispatch queue. Powers the "T"
162	// affordance on the notify-settings screen so users can verify a freshly
163	// added webhook URL without triggering a real event.
164	//
165	// Returns nil on 2xx/3xx, an error on transport failure or 4xx/5xx
166	// (with a short body excerpt to surface receiver-side complaints like
167	// Discord's "embed exceeds maximum size of 6000"). The error message
168	// has the webhook URL's last path segment (the secret) redacted so a
169	// user pasting it back doesn't accidentally publish their token.
170	func (m *Manager) SendTest(ctx context.Context, target *store.NotifyTarget, ev Event) error {
171		t0 := time.Now()
172		req, err := buildRequest(ctx, target, ev)
173		if err != nil {
174			return fmt.Errorf("build request: %w", err)
175		}
176		resp, err := m.client.Do(req)
177		if err != nil {
178			// http.Client wraps the error with the full URL via *url.Error;
179			// strip the secret before bubbling up.
180			log.Warn("notify: test post failed",
181				"target", target.ID, "url", RedactURL(target.URL),
182				"err", scrubURL(err.Error(), target.URL))
183			return scrubURLErr(err, target.URL)
184		}
185		defer resp.Body.Close()
186		if resp.StatusCode >= 400 {
187			excerpt, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 256))
188			log.Warn("notify: test target rejected",
189				"target", target.ID, "url", RedactURL(target.URL),
190				"status", resp.StatusCode)
191			return fmt.Errorf("HTTP %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(excerpt)))
192		}
193		log.Info("notify: test delivered",
194			"target", target.ID, "url", RedactURL(target.URL),
195			"status", resp.StatusCode, "duration", time.Since(t0))
196		return nil
197	}
198	
199	// scrubURL replaces every occurrence of rawURL inside a string with its
200	// redacted form. Used to clean error messages that net/http builds with
201	// the full URL embedded (e.g. `Post "https://...": context deadline ...`).
202	func scrubURL(s, rawURL string) string {
203		if rawURL == "" {
204			return s
205		}
206		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, rawURL, RedactURL(rawURL))
207	}
208	
209	// scrubURLErr is the error variant: re-wraps the error with its rendered
210	// message scrubbed. Loses the original error type (no errors.Is matching),
211	// which is fine for the user-facing flash row.
212	func scrubURLErr(err error, rawURL string) error {
213		if err == nil {
214			return nil
215		}
216		return fmt.Errorf("%s", scrubURL(err.Error(), rawURL))
217	}
218	
219	func (m *Manager) deliver(ctx context.Context, ev Event) {
220		prefs, err := m.store.Notify().GetPrefs(ctx, ev.ToUserID)
221		if err != nil {
222			log.Warn("notify: load prefs", "to", ev.ToUserID, "err", err)
223			return
224		}
225		if !prefsAllow(prefs, ev.Kind) {
226			return
227		}
228		if prefs.OnlyWhenOffline && m.online != nil && m.online.IsOnline(ev.ToUserID) {
229			return
230		}
231		targets, err := m.store.Notify().ListEnabledTargets(ctx, ev.ToUserID)
232		if err != nil {
233			log.Warn("notify: load targets", "to", ev.ToUserID, "err", err)
234			return
235		}
236		for _, t := range targets {
237			m.post(ctx, t, ev)
238		}
239	}
240	
241	func (m *Manager) post(ctx context.Context, t *store.NotifyTarget, ev Event) {
242		t0 := time.Now()
243		req, err := buildRequest(ctx, t, ev)
244		if err != nil {
245			log.Warn("notify: build request", "target", t.ID, "err", err)
246			return
247		}
248		resp, err := m.client.Do(req)
249		if err != nil {
250			log.Warn("notify: post failed",
251				"target", t.ID, "url", RedactURL(t.URL), "kind", ev.Kind,
252				"err", scrubURL(err.Error(), t.URL))
253			return
254		}
255		defer resp.Body.Close()
256		if resp.StatusCode >= 400 {
257			log.Warn("notify: target rejected",
258				"target", t.ID, "url", RedactURL(t.URL),
259				"status", resp.StatusCode, "kind", ev.Kind)
260			return
261		}
262		log.Info("notify: delivered",
263			"target", t.ID, "url", RedactURL(t.URL), "kind", ev.Kind,
264			"to", ev.ToUserID, "from", ev.FromUserID,
265			"status", resp.StatusCode, "duration", time.Since(t0))
266	}
267	
268	func prefsAllow(p store.NotifyPrefs, k Kind) bool {
269		switch k {
270		case KindPush:
271			return p.OnPush
272		case KindWB:
273			return p.OnWB
274		case KindMail:
275			return p.OnMail
276		case KindReply:
277			return p.OnReply
278		}
279		return false
```
</details></tool-use>

# Bilingual support (zh-TW + en) for sshbbs — implementation plan

Default locale stays `zh-TW`; user opts into `en` via a new User Settings entry. In `en`, push glyphs render as 👍/👎 (with 爆 → 💥) to keep column widths stable; everything else is direct translation. **Markdown export/import stays canonical zh-TW** — `PushKind.Glyph()` is unchanged so round-trip via `cmd_import` is byte-stable.

## 1. `internal/i18n` package design

Create `internal/i18n/i18n.go` (+ `i18n_test.go`, + per-locale tables `zh_tw.go`, `en.go`).

**API surface**

```go
package i18n

type Locale string

const (
    LocaleZHTW Locale = "zh-TW"
    LocaleEN   Locale = "en"
    Default           = LocaleZHTW
)

// Valid reports whether s is a recognised locale value. Used by the
// settings screen and by the User-loader fallback.
func Valid(s string) bool

// Normalize coerces a stored value to a known locale, falling back to
// Default on empty or unrecognised input. Centralizes the "old DB row /
// nil User" fallback so call sites don't have to.
func Normalize(s string) Locale

// T looks up key in loc's table. Missing keys fall back to zh-TW; missing
// in both returns the key itself wrapped in «» so it stands out in the UI
// (so we can grep for unlocalised keys post-deploy without crashing).
func T(loc Locale, key string) string

// Tf is fmt.Sprintf over T. Format directives live in the locale string
// so word order can flow naturally per-language ("%s pushed your post" vs
// "%s 推了你的文章").
func Tf(loc Locale, key string, args ...any) string
```

**Storage choice: Go map literals per locale, not embedded JSON.**

Justification:
- Compile-time safety. A typo in a key name fails at the call site (we'll use `const KeyMainMenuTitle = "screen.main_menu.title"` shared keys; see §7), not at runtime when the user happens to navigate to that screen.
- No new dependency, no new I/O path, no need to teach `embed` or wire up a loader. The repo's pattern (CLAUDE.md §"data deliberately diverges") favours pure-Go primitives over framework adoption.
- Two locales × ~150 strings is small enough that maps are readable. JSON would only win at ~5 locales or if non-engineers were translating.
- Existing `internal/markdown` package shows the precedent: leaf package, pure functions, no DB or I/O.

Layout:
```
internal/i18n/
    i18n.go        // Locale type, Valid/Normalize/T/Tf, the table registry
    keys.go        // const Key* = "screen.main_menu.title" string identifiers
    zh_tw.go       // var zhTW = map[string]string{...}
    en.go          // var en   = map[string]string{...}
    glyphs.go      // PushGlyph(loc, kind) etc. — see §3
    i18n_test.go   // key-completeness, glyph width, Normalize fallback
```

Format-arg handling: lean entirely on `fmt.Sprintf`. The few strings that interpolate (`"%s 推了你的文章"`, `"確定刪除推文 #%d?"`) keep their `%s`/`%d` directives in both translations. Don't introduce ICU MessageFormat — over-engineered for two locales without plural-form differences worth speaking of.

## 2. Locale storage + plumbing

**Migration `0010_add_user_locale.sql`:**

```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW';
```

That's it. Per CLAUDE.md, migrations live under `internal/store/migrations/` and are picked up via the `go:embed migrations/*.sql` glob in `internal/store/migrate.go`. No schema_migrations bookkeeping change needed — `migrate.apply()` parses the `0010` prefix.

**`internal/store/users.go` changes:**

1. Add `Locale string` to the `User` struct (place after `Bio`).
2. Append `, locale` to the `userColumns` const string (literal SQL).
3. Append `&u.Locale` to the `scanUser` Scan call list.
4. Add a repo method:
   ```go
   func (r *UserRepo) SetLocale(ctx context.Context, id int64, locale string) error
   ```
   Mirrors `SetBio`/`SetPassword`: takes `r.s.writeMu.Lock()`, runs `UPDATE users SET locale = ? WHERE id = ?`, returns the err. Caller (the new locale-settings screen) is responsible for validation via `i18n.Valid` before invoking.
5. `InsertSystemAccount` — leave alone. The DEFAULT 'zh-TW' covers admin/guest seeds.

**Where `Deps.User.Locale` is read in TUI screens:**

Helper `func (m someModel) loc() i18n.Locale { return i18n.Normalize(localeOf(m.deps)) }` defined once per model file (or as a free function `localeOf(deps Deps) i18n.Locale` in a new `internal/tui/i18n.go` shim). Implementation:

```go
// localeOf returns the User.Locale or i18n.Default on a register/login
// screen where deps.User is nil. Centralises the nil-guard so individual
// screens don't repeat it.
func localeOf(d Deps) i18n.Locale {
    if d.User == nil {
        return i18n.Default
    }
    return i18n.Normalize(d.User.Locale)
}
```

**Fallback when `User` is nil:** the register screen and the SSH password-callback path can hit T() before any User row exists. `localeOf` returns `i18n.Default` (zh-TW) which is exactly the behaviour we want. The register form's `?` placeholder shows in zh-TW; once the user submits and we have a `*store.User`, subsequent screens see the persisted locale (defaults to zh-TW for new accounts).

**In-place refresh after locale change:** mirror the bio-edit pattern (`screen_bio_edit.go:79-83`):
```go
if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
    *m.deps.User = *fresh
}
```
Because `Deps.User` is a pointer (`*store.User`), the in-place overwrite is visible to every screen that holds the same `Deps` value — so the next navigate already renders in the new locale without a re-login.

## 3. Push / score / badge glyph mapping

Live in `internal/i18n/glyphs.go`. These are tied to UI display, NOT to canonical storage — the markdown serializer keeps using `PushKind.Glyph()`.

| Concept | zh-TW | en | Both widths | Where used |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Push (+1) | `推` | `👍` | 2 | `renderPushKind`, `kindLabel`, `screen_article_view` push input prompt |
| Boo (-1) | `噓` | `👎` | 2 | same |
| Arrow (0) | `→` | `→` | 1 | same — keep universal arrow |
| Score "exploded" (≥100) | `爆` | `💥` | 2 | `screen_board_view.go:301-307` |
| Score "X" (negative) | `X9` | `X9` | n+1 | same — leave; X is universal |
| Locked badge | `[鎖]` | `[L]` | 4 → 3 | `screen_board_view.go:320`, `screen_article_view.go:576` |
| Arrows-only badge | `[箭]` | `[A]` | 4 → 3 | `screen_board_view.go:322`, `screen_article_view.go:574` |
| Pinned badge | `[M]` | `[M]` | 3 | `screen_board_view.go:316` — universal, no swap |

The `[L]`/`[A]` badge is a **prefix** on the title cell, not a column-aligned cell, so the 1-column shrink is acceptable (CJK text after the badge floats; English doesn't care). Verified by reading the assembly at `screen_board_view.go:324-326` — `prefix + " " + a.Title` is then run through `Truncate(title, titleW)`, which is runewidth-aware.

**Helper signatures (in `internal/i18n/glyphs.go`):**

```go
import "github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"

// PushGlyph returns the locale-appropriate glyph for a push kind. Unstyled.
// Caller is responsible for lipgloss styling so the styles package doesn't
// need to import store.
func PushGlyph(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string

// PushVerb returns the locale-appropriate short verb for use inside a
// templated sentence (e.g. webhook titles: "alice 推了你的文章" /
// "alice 👍 your post"). Distinct from PushGlyph only because the en
// translation may want a verb like "upvoted" instead of the emoji.
// Recommendation: also use 👍/👎 here for compact webhook embeds —
// matches the in-TUI rendering, and avoids the verb-tense headache.
func PushVerb(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string

// ScoreExploded returns the glyph for an article whose recommend_score
// has hit the "爆" threshold (≥100 in screen_board_view).
func ScoreExploded(loc Locale) string  // "爆" / "💥"

// CommentsModeBadge returns the bracketed mode marker shown on board
// list rows and the article header. Empty for CommentsModeOpen.
func CommentsModeBadge(loc Locale, m store.CommentsMode) string  // "[鎖]" / "[L]" etc.
```

The two TUI sibling functions in `screen_article_view.go` (`renderPushKind` keeping its lipgloss styles, `kindLabel` plain) become thin wrappers:

```go
func renderPushKind(loc i18n.Locale, k store.PushKind) string {
    g := i18n.PushGlyph(loc, k)
    switch k {
    case store.PushKindPush: return StylePushKind.Render(g)
    case store.PushKindBoo:  return StyleBooKind.Render(g)
    case store.PushKindArrow: return StyleArrowKind.Render(g)
    }
    return "?"
}
func kindLabel(loc i18n.Locale, k store.PushKind) string {
    return i18n.PushVerb(loc, k)
}
```

Every call site already has `m.deps.User` (or wants `m.loc()`), so threading the extra arg is mechanical.

**`PushKind.Glyph()` in `internal/store/pushes.go:33-43` stays unchanged.** That method is the canonical serializer used by `markdown.Format` line 143, and `parsePushLine` (markdown.go:297-303) only knows how to read `推`/`噓`/`→`. Touching it would break round-trip with existing exported files and with any hand-edited markdown the user re-imports. This is the most important invariant in the whole change.

## 4. The new locale-settings screen

**Recommendation: dedicated `screen_locale_settings.go` (3rd entry in user-settings sub-menu, before "通知設定").**

Rationale:
- The notify-settings screen already proves the pattern (`prefsDirty` + `Ctrl+S` to commit), but locale is a single-radio-style choice with two options. A dedicated screen lets us render a 2-row chooser with `[•] zh-TW (繁體中文) / [ ] English` and explicit Save/Cancel semantics — clearer than wedging another toggle into notify-settings (which is "events + targets", a different concern).
- Mirrors `screen_bio_edit`: small focused form, navigated to from `screen_user_settings`, returns to it on save/cancel. Different from bio-edit in that it's not a textinput form — so it stays a non-form screen (no `isFormScreen` entry); h/l navigation can stay bound for left=back/right=select.
- Keeps the user-settings menu structure stable: 4 → 5 entries, numeric shortcuts `1-5` (extend the case in `screen_user_settings.go:52`).

**`screen_user_settings.go` updates:**
```go
items := []menuItem{
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.password"), hint: ..., to: ScreenPasswordChange},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.bio"),      hint: ..., to: ScreenBioEdit},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.locale"),   hint: ..., to: ScreenLocaleSettings},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "screen.user_settings.notify"),   hint: ..., to: ScreenNotifySettings},
    {label: i18n.T(loc, "common.back"),                   hint: ..., to: ScreenMainMenu},
}
```
Update the case `"1", "2", "3", "4"` → `"1", "2", "3", "4", "5"`.

**`messages.go`:** add `ScreenLocaleSettings` after `ScreenNotifySettings` (append at the bottom — `Screen` is `iota`, append-only is the safe pattern).

**`root.go:navigate` switch:** add
```go
case ScreenLocaleSettings:
    sub = newLocaleSettingsModel(m.deps)
```

**`help.go`:** add a `screenHelp[ScreenLocaleSettings]` entry; do **not** add to `isFormScreen` (it's a chooser, not a textinput form, so `?` should still pop the help overlay).

**Model sketch:**

```go
type localeSettingsModel struct {
    deps    Deps
    cursor  int            // 0 = zh-TW, 1 = en
    options []i18n.Locale  // {LocaleZHTW, LocaleEN}
    dirty   bool
    err     string
    flash   string
}
```

Keys: `j/k`/arrows move; Enter/Space selects (sets cursor + dirty); `Ctrl+S` persists via `Users().SetLocale` then refreshes `*m.deps.User` and toasts; Esc/`h`/left returns to user-settings (warn if dirty? — match notify-settings, which doesn't warn; same pattern here, just discard).

## 5. Webhook notification locale strategy

**Pick recipient locale.**

Rationale:
- A push notification is an event on the recipient's phone; the body and title should read in the recipient's preferred language. If alice (en) pushes bob's article and bob has zh-TW, bob should get `[BBS] alice 推了你的文章`, not `[BBS] alice 👍 your post`.
- Consistent with how email/SMS UX works generally — sender's choice of UI doesn't dictate the recipient's notification text.
- Cheap: the dispatcher already does a `users` lookup-by-id implicitly inside the deliver path? No — it loads `user_notif_prefs` by user_id. So we need one extra `Users().GetByID(ctx, ev.ToUserID)` in the dispatcher's `deliver` to grab `Locale`.

**Where the lookup happens:**

The cleanest seam is at the call site in TUI helpers (`notify_helpers.go` + the inline push-notify in `screen_article_view.go:539-547`), because:
- Title/body are already pre-rendered there as plain strings; the dispatcher just POSTs them. (Confirmed in `internal/notify/dispatcher.go:241-266` — `m.post` writes `ev.Title`/`ev.Body` to the HTTP body via `buildRequest`, no transformation.)
- Threading locale into the dispatcher would force the dispatcher to know about i18n keys (cross-package coupling: `internal/notify` would have to import `internal/i18n` AND `internal/store` for the User lookup).
- Doing it at the call site is one extra DB read per notification, which is fine — notifications are already async and rate-limited by webhook upstreams.

So `notify_helpers.go` becomes:

```go
func notifyWB(deps Deps, toUserID int64, fromUserID, body string) {
    if deps.Notify == nil {
        return
    }
    loc := i18n.Default
    if deps.Store != nil {
        if u, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), toUserID); err == nil {
            loc = i18n.Normalize(u.Locale)
        }
    }
    deps.Notify.Dispatch(notify.Event{
        Kind:       notify.KindWB,
        ToUserID:   toUserID,
        FromUserID: fromUserID,
        Title:      i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.wb.title", fromUserID),
        Body:       body,
    })
}
```

Same shape for `notifyMail` and the inline `Notify.Dispatch` in `screen_article_view.go`. The push notification specifically needs the verb interpolation:

```go
Title: i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.push.title", u.UserID, i18n.PushVerb(loc, m.pushKind)),
```
with strings:
```
zh-TW: "[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章"
en   : "[BBS] %s %s your post"
```

If `Store.Users().GetByID` fails (deleted user race), we fall back to `i18n.Default` and the notification still goes out — graceful degradation, no panic.

## 6. Markdown export untouched — invariant statement

**`internal/store/pushes.go` `PushKind.Glyph()` is canonical, locale-independent, and stays returning `推`/`噓`/`→`.**

Touched-by audit:
- `internal/markdown/markdown.go:143` (Format writer) → unchanged.
- `internal/markdown/markdown.go:297-303` (parsePushLine reader) → unchanged.
- `cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go:54` (CLI import) → unchanged; it consumes whatever Format produced and the parser only knows zh glyphs.

The localised TUI helpers (`renderPushKind`, `kindLabel`, score "爆", `[鎖]`/`[箭]`) all live in `internal/tui/` and `internal/i18n/glyphs.go`, never reach into the store layer's serialization path. Add a comment to `PushKind.Glyph()` reinforcing this:

```go
// Glyph returns the canonical CJK glyph for the kind. This is used by the
// markdown serializer (round-trip with `sshbbs export` / `sshbbs import`)
// and MUST stay locale-independent — the parser at
// internal/markdown/markdown.go:297-303 only recognises 推/噓/→.
// For locale-aware UI rendering, use internal/i18n.PushGlyph instead.
func (k PushKind) Glyph() string { ... }
```

A markdown round-trip test should be added (or the existing one extended) to assert that exporting a push from an `en`-locale user still produces `推` in the file (see §9).

## 7. String key naming convention

Flat dotted scheme, grouped by screen:

```
common.back
common.save
common.cancel
common.confirm_delete

screen.main_menu.title              // " 主選單 Main Menu " / " Main Menu "
screen.main_menu.boards             // "看板列表 Boards" / "Boards"
screen.main_menu.wb                 // "水球 Water Balloons" / "Water Balloons"
screen.main_menu.online             // "線上使用者 Online" / "Online users"
screen.main_menu.mail               // "信箱 Mail" / "Mail"
screen.main_menu.user_settings      // "個人設定 User settings" / "User settings"
screen.main_menu.admin              // "管理 Admin" / "Admin"
screen.main_menu.quit               // "離線 Quit" / "Quit"
screen.main_menu.help_line          // formatted help line, %d for item count

screen.user_settings.title
screen.user_settings.password
screen.user_settings.bio
screen.user_settings.locale         // NEW — "語言 Language" / "Language"
screen.user_settings.notify
screen.user_settings.bio_empty      // "(尚未填寫 bio)" / "(no bio yet)"

screen.locale_settings.title
screen.locale_settings.option_zh
screen.locale_settings.option_en
screen.locale_settings.saved        // "✓ 已儲存語言偏好" / "✓ Locale saved"

screen.article_view.header          // " 文章 #%d · %s "
screen.article_view.confirm_delete_article  // "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)" / "Delete this article? (y/N)"
screen.article_view.confirm_delete_push     // "確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)"
screen.article_view.locked_label    // "[鎖] 已關閉留言" — composed from CommentsModeBadge + " " + locked text
screen.article_view.arrows_label
screen.article_view.help_line_*     // see §10 — split into role-conditional pieces

screen.notify_settings.title
screen.notify_settings.events_header
screen.notify_settings.targets_header
screen.notify_settings.no_targets
screen.notify_settings.add_target
screen.notify_settings.prefs_unsaved
screen.notify_settings.flash_saved
screen.notify_settings.flash_added         // "✓ 已新增 target #%d"
screen.notify_settings.flash_updated       // "✓ 已更新 target #%d"
screen.notify_settings.flash_deleted       // "✓ 已刪除 target"
screen.notify_settings.flash_enabled       // "✓ 已啟用 target #%d"
screen.notify_settings.flash_disabled      // "✓ 已停用 target #%d"
screen.notify_settings.test_running        // "🔔 testing target #%d…"
screen.notify_settings.test_succeeded      // "✓ target #%d test delivered…"
screen.notify_settings.test_failed         // "target #%d test failed: %v"
screen.notify_settings.help_line

screen.board_list.title             // "看板列表 Boards"
screen.board_list.search_placeholder
screen.board_view.title
screen.board_view.sort_byscore_label  // "[排序: 推文量↓]" / "[sort: by score↓]"
screen.board_view.help_line / .help_line_guest

screen.online.title
screen.mail_inbox.title
screen.mail_thread.title
screen.password_change.*
screen.bio_edit.*
screen.register.*
screen.admin_users.*
screen.article_export.*
screen.board_splash.help            // "press any key" — universal but localizable
screen.board_banner_edit.*

error.permission_denied              // "權限不足" / "Permission denied"
error.guest_readonly                 // "guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)" / "guest accounts are read-only"
error.admin_only                     // "管理員專用 (admin only)" / "admin only"
error.comments_locked                // "本文已鎖定留言" / "Comments are locked on this article"
error.comments_arrows_only           // "本文僅開放箭頭"
error.password_wrong                 // "目前密碼錯誤" / "Current password is wrong"
error.no_user                        // "internal error: no user"  (probably leave English-only)

notify.push.title                    // "[BBS] %s %s 了你的文章" / "[BBS] %s %s your post"
notify.wb.title                      // "[BBS] %s 丟了水球給你"   / "[BBS] %s sent you a water balloon"
notify.mail.title                    // "[BBS] %s 寄信給你: %s"   / "[BBS] %s mailed you: %s"
notify.test.title                    // "[BBS-test] notification target verified"  (leave English — operator-facing)

help.global.help                     // "show this help (any key dismisses)"
help.global.wb_inbox                 // "open 水球 inbox" / "open water balloon inbox"
help.global.disconnect

help.main_menu.move
help.main_menu.select
help.main_menu.jump
help.main_menu.quit
... (one entry per HelpEntry row in screenHelp)
```

Conventions:
- All-lowercase, dot-separated.
- Top namespace mirrors a stable concept: `common.`, `screen.<screen_name>.`, `error.`, `notify.`, `help.<screen_name>.`.
- The screen segment matches the file basename without `screen_` prefix and without `.go` (so `screen_main_menu.go` → `screen.main_menu.*`). One refactor-friendly mapping rule.
- Format directives (`%s`, `%d`, `%v`) appear in both translations; both must have the same number of args (asserted in §9 tests).
- Keys defined as `const Key... = "..."` in `internal/i18n/keys.go` so call sites are typo-safe (`i18n.T(loc, i18n.KeyMainMenuTitle)`).

## 8. Migration order / phasing — recommend 3 PRs

**PR 1 — foundation, no UI text changes (zh-only still rendered):**
- Add migration `0010_add_user_locale.sql`.
- Extend `User` struct + `userColumns` + `scanUser` + add `SetLocale` repo method.
- Add `internal/i18n` package with `Locale`, `Valid`, `Normalize`, `T`, `Tf`, `keys.go`, and stub locale tables (only `zh_tw.go` populated; `en.go` is empty so `T(en, key)` falls back to zh and the UI still looks identical to today).
- Add `internal/i18n/glyphs.go` with PushGlyph/PushVerb/ScoreExploded/CommentsModeBadge — both locales filled (this is the smallest, lowest-risk locale data).
- Add `localeOf(deps)` helper in `internal/tui/i18n.go`.
- Add `ScreenLocaleSettings` const + `screen_locale_settings.go` + Root.navigate case + user-settings menu entry. The screen reads/writes the column; nothing else changes visually.
- Tests: locale persistence round-trip; settings screen toggle; `Normalize` fallbacks; PushGlyph table.

End state: a user can flip their locale in settings; nothing else changes yet. Safe to merge.

**PR 2 — batch-migrate strings, screen-by-screen (en table populated):**
- Populate `en.go` with all keys from `keys.go`.
- Convert each screen's hard-coded strings to `i18n.T(m.loc(), Key...)` calls. Recommended order (least to most state):
  1. `screen_main_menu.go` (small, high visibility, easy to verify)
  2. `screen_user_settings.go` (already touched in PR 1)
  3. `screen_locale_settings.go` (the new screen — populate its strings)
  4. `screen_board_list.go`, `screen_board_view.go` (no push glyphs yet — leave score/badge calls as-is for now, swap in PR 3)
  5. `screen_article_view.go` (excluding `renderPushKind` — that's PR 3)

  7. `screen_post_compose.go`, `screen_register.go`, `screen_password_change.go`, `screen_bio_edit.go`

End state: en mode is functional but still uses zh push glyphs.

**PR 3 — push glyph swap with width tests:**
- Wire `renderPushKind`/`kindLabel`/the `[鎖]`/`[箭]` badges/`爆` score in `screen_board_view.go:301` and `screen_article_view.go:573-577,665-690` through `i18n.PushGlyph` / `i18n.CommentsModeBadge` / `i18n.ScoreExploded`.

- Update `help.go` Article-view help entry from `"+ / - / =", "推 / 噓 / → (non-guest)"` to use locale glyphs.

This phasing isolates the highest-risk visual change (emoji widths in real terminals) into a small reviewable PR with a dedicated test.

## 9. Testing strategy

**`internal/i18n/i18n_test.go`** — three tests, all keyed off the public API:

1. **Key completeness**: iterate `zhTW`, assert every key exists in `en` (and vice-versa). Fail with the missing key list. Catches "translator forgot to add key X" at PR time.
2. **Format-arg parity**: for every key, parse out `%s`/`%d`/`%v`/`%q` directives in both locales' values; assert the count and types match. Catches "translator dropped the `%s`" — the Tf call would otherwise emit the `%!(MISSING)` token at runtime.
3. **Normalize fallback**: table-driven over `("", "fr", "zh", "zh-TW", "en")` → expect `Default, Default, Default, LocaleZHTW, LocaleEN`.

**`internal/i18n/glyphs_test.go`** — runewidth parity:

```go
func TestPushGlyph_WidthParity(t *testing.T) {
    for _, k := range []store.PushKind{store.PushKindPush, store.PushKindBoo, store.PushKindArrow} {
        zh := i18n.PushGlyph(i18n.LocaleZHTW, k)
        en := i18n.PushGlyph(i18n.LocaleEN, k)
        if runewidth.StringWidth(zh) != runewidth.StringWidth(en) {
            t.Errorf("kind %v: zh=%q (w=%d) vs en=%q (w=%d) — must match for column alignment",
                k, zh, runewidth.StringWidth(zh), en,
                runewidth.StringWidth(zh), runewidth.StringWidth(en))
        }
    }
    // Same for ScoreExploded.
}
```

This catches the case where someone "improves" a glyph to a 1-cell ASCII variant and breaks the article-list column math. Run under the same `runewidth` import the TUI uses (`github.com/mattn/go-runewidth`).

**TUI tests:**
- **Keybinding-parity tests** (`screen_*_test.go`) don't reference label text, so they're unaffected. Spot-checked `screen_user_settings_test.go` patterns; they assert NavigateMsg payloads, not strings.
- **`help_test.go`** asserts certain header strings are present in the rendered help overlay; update its assertions to either (a) use the i18n key directly (`i18n.T(i18n.Default, key)`) and assert that's a substring, or (b) do `setLocale(t, i18n.LocaleZHTW)` per test and assert the literal — option (a) is more refactor-resistant.
- **Render tests that grep for "[M]", "[鎖]", "[箭]"** in `screen_board_view_test.go:339,350-351,383` and `screen_article_view_test.go:889-890`: parameterize the test over locale, keep the existing zh assertion when locale is zh-TW, add an `[L]`/`[A]` assertion when locale is en. Construct the test User with `Locale: "en"` to drive the new path.
- **Locale-settings screen** new test: toggle, save, assert `*deps.User` updated in place AND DB row updated.
- **Webhook recipient-locale** new test: insert a recipient User with `Locale: "en"`, send a push as a zh-locale user, capture the Notify.Dispatch event via a recording dispatcher (similar to `chat.fakeSender`), assert `Title` is in English.

**Markdown round-trip regression** (in `internal/markdown/markdown_test.go` — extend existing test):
- Construct a Push, hand it to `Format` with the producing user's locale set to `en`, assert the output literally contains `推` (since `PushKind.Glyph()` is locale-independent).
- Then `Parse` the output and assert the parsed push kind round-trips. If this fails, the §6 invariant has been broken.

CI command stays `make test-race`. No new flaky surface.

## 11. Verification steps — manual + automated

**Automated (run before each PR merges):**
```bash
make test-race                    # full suite, race detector — all green
go test -race ./internal/i18n/... # focused
go test -race ./internal/tui/... -run TestLocaleSettings
go test -race ./internal/markdown/... -run TestRoundtripPushGlyphIsLocaleIndependent
```

**Manual end-to-end (after PR 3 merges):**
```bash
make hostkey                      # if not already done
make db-reset                     # fresh DB so migration 0010 runs cleanly
make run                          # boots :2222
```

In a second terminal:
```bash
ssh new@localhost -p 2222         # register a fresh user "alice", any password OK during register
# After registering, you land on the main menu in zh-TW (default).
# Verify: header "主選單 Main Menu", item "看板列表 Boards", help line in zh.

# Navigate: 個人設定 → 語言 Language
# Move cursor to English, Enter to select, Ctrl+S to save.
# Expect toast "✓ Locale saved" and the screen header to flip to English on next render.

# Esc back to main menu — now in English.
# Verify: "Main Menu" header, "Boards" item, "Online" item, English help line.

# Open a board, open an article.
# Verify: 推 column shows 👍 (in green), 噓 shows 👎 (in red), → unchanged.
# Score column: post 100 pushes (or doctor recommend_score in DB), verify shows 💥 not 爆.
# Pin an article (mod): [M] still shows.
# Lock comments: prefix shows [L] not [鎊]; article header says "[L] Comments locked".

# Push the article: + key. Verify the push input prompt's leading glyph is 👍.
```

In a third terminal:
```bash
ssh bob@localhost -p 2222         # register bob, leave at zh-TW default
# Have bob open an article alice authored, push it (+).
# Set up a webhook target on alice's account first (use https://requestbin.com).
# Verify alice's webhook receives "[BBS] bob 👍 your post" because alice's
# locale is en. Conversely have alice push bob's article — bob's webhook
# receives "[BBS] alice 推了你的文章" in zh.
```

**Markdown round-trip (proves §6 invariant):**
```bash
# As alice (en mode), find an article ID with pushes, then:
./sshbbs export --article 5 --include-pushes > /tmp/a.md
grep -E '^- (推|噓|→) \[' /tmp/a.md           # MUST find canonical zh glyphs
! grep -E '^- (👍|👎) \[' /tmp/a.md            # MUST NOT find emoji
./sshbbs import --file /tmp/a.md --board Welcome    # round-trip back in
# Verify the new imported article has the same push count and kinds in the DB.
```

If either grep behaves differently, PR 3 broke §6 — revert and audit `renderPushKind` callers for accidental writes back into `markdown.Format`.

**SQLite migration verification:**
```bash
sqlite3 data/bbs.db ".schema users" | grep -i locale
# Expect:  locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW'
sqlite3 data/bbs.db "SELECT user_id, locale FROM users;"
# Expect every existing row to show 'zh-TW' — DEFAULT applied to backfilled rows.
```

280	func parsePushLine(s string) (ParsedPush, bool) {
281		// Format: "<glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>"
282		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
283		if s == "" {
284			return ParsedPush{}, false
285		}
286	
287		// Glyph: take everything before the first whitespace.
288		wsIdx := strings.IndexAny(s, " \t")
289		if wsIdx <= 0 {
290			return ParsedPush{}, false
291		}
292		glyph := s[:wsIdx]
293		rest := strings.TrimLeft(s[wsIdx:], " \t")
294	
295		var kind store.PushKind
296		switch glyph {
297		case "推":
298			kind = store.PushKindPush
299		case "噓":
300			kind = store.PushKindBoo
301		case "→":
302			kind = store.PushKindArrow
303		default:
304			return ParsedPush{}, false
305		}
306	
307		if !strings.HasPrefix(rest, "[") {
308			return ParsedPush{}, false
309		}
```

```md
# English Mode (i18n: zh-TW + en)

## Context

The TUI currently hard-codes ~150 user-visible CJK strings across `internal/tui/` (headers, menu items, status flashes, errors, confirmations, help lines, placeholders). A handful of strings also live in `internal/store/pushes.go` (push glyphs, used by markdown export round-trip), `internal/store/boards.go` (seed content), and `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go` + `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:544` (webhook notification titles).

Product owner direction:
- Add a per-user locale preference (`zh-TW` default, `en` opt-in) toggled from User Settings.
- In `en` mode, render `推`/`噓` as `👍`/`👎` so column widths stay stable (1:1 cell-width parity); other labels can be plain English translations.
- Keep zh-TW as the default and the canonical-feeling locale.

Non-goals (record-only, may surface as TODO.md items later):
- Per-board locale overrides
- Locale beyond zh-TW + en (ja/ko/zh-CN)
- SSH `LANG` env detection
- Locale-aware date formatting

## Hard invariant

**`store.PushKind.Glyph()` (`internal/store/pushes.go:33-43`) stays canonical `推`/`噓`/`→` regardless of locale.** It is the serializer for `markdown.Format` (`internal/markdown/markdown.go:143`) and the parser at `internal/markdown/markdown.go:296-303` only recognises those three CJK glyphs. Localising it would silently break `sshbbs export` → `sshbbs import` round-trip (CLI path: `cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go:54`).

Locale-aware glyphs go through a new `internal/i18n/glyphs.go` and are consumed only by TUI render functions.

## Approach

### 1. New `internal/i18n` package (Go map literals, not JSON)

\```
internal/i18n/
  i18n.go      // Locale type, Valid, Normalize, T, Tf
  keys.go      // const Key... = "screen.main_menu.title" string identifiers
  zh_tw.go     // var zhTW = map[string]string{...}
  en.go        // var en   = map[string]string{...}
  glyphs.go    // PushGlyph / PushVerb / ScoreExploded / CommentsModeBadge
  i18n_test.go
  glyphs_test.go
\```

API:
\```go
type Locale string
const (
    LocaleZHTW Locale = "zh-TW"
    LocaleEN   Locale = "en"
    Default           = LocaleZHTW
)

func Valid(s string) bool
func Normalize(s string) Locale            // empty / unrecognised → Default
func T(loc Locale, key string) string      // missing in `en` → falls back to zh-TW; missing in both → «key» so it stands out
func Tf(loc Locale, key string, args ...any) string  // fmt.Sprintf wrapper

// Locale-aware glyphs (TUI only — never feed into markdown.Format)
func PushGlyph(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string         // 推/噓/→  vs  👍/👎/→
func PushVerb(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string          // for templated webhook titles
func ScoreExploded(loc Locale) string                       // 爆 vs 💥
func CommentsModeBadge(loc Locale, m store.CommentsMode) string  // [鎖]/[箭] vs [L]/[A]
\```

Why Go maps over embedded JSON: compile-time key safety via `keys.go` consts, no loader I/O, two locales fit comfortably in literals, mirrors the leaf-package style used by `internal/markdown`.

Key naming: flat dotted, grouped by screen — `screen.<basename>.<role>`, plus `common.*`, `error.*`, `notify.*`, `help.<screen>.*`. Both locales must declare the same set of keys with the same `%s`/`%d`/`%v` directive count (asserted by `i18n_test.go`).

### 2. Locale storage + plumbing

**Migration `internal/store/migrations/0010_add_user_locale.sql`:**
\```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW';
\```
Picked up automatically by `internal/store/migrate.go`'s `go:embed migrations/*.sql`.

**`internal/store/users.go` edits** (mirror the `Bio` precedent — bio was added the same way in migration `0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`):
- Add `Locale string` to `User` struct (after `Bio`).
- Append `, locale` to `userColumns` const (line 34-35).
- Append `&u.Locale` to `scanUser` Scan list (line 40-44).
- Add repo method `func (r *UserRepo) SetLocale(ctx, id int64, locale string) error` — takes `r.s.writeMu.Lock()` per `Store.writeMu` discipline; mirror `SetBio` shape. Caller validates via `i18n.Valid` first.

**`internal/tui/i18n.go` (new shim):**
\```go
func localeOf(d Deps) i18n.Locale {
    if d.User == nil { return i18n.Default }   // register / pre-login
    return i18n.Normalize(d.User.Locale)
}
\```
Each model file calls `m.deps` → `localeOf(m.deps)` (or stores it on the model when convenient).

**Refresh after locale change**: mirror `internal/tui/screen_bio_edit.go:79-83`:
\```go
if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
    *m.deps.User = *fresh   // in-place — every screen sharing Deps sees it
}
\```

### 3. New `screen_locale_settings.go` (dedicated chooser screen)

Two-row radio-style chooser (`[•] zh-TW (繁體中文) / [ ] English`), Enter/Space to select, `Ctrl+S` to commit, Esc to discard. NOT a textinput form, so:
- Add `ScreenLocaleSettings` to `internal/tui/messages.go` (append to the `Screen` iota — append-only is the safe pattern for the navigate switch).
- Add the navigate case in `internal/tui/root.go` (the single `Root.navigate` switch, per CLAUDE.md "NavigateMsg is the only legitimate way to swap screens").
- Insert as the 3rd entry in `internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go` items list (between "修改 Bio" and "通知設定 Notification settings"); extend the numeric-shortcut switch from `"1"-"4"` to `"1"-"5"`.
- **Do NOT add to `tui.isFormScreen`** in `internal/tui/help.go` — `?` should still pop the help overlay on this screen.
- Add `screenHelp[ScreenLocaleSettings]` entry in `help.go`.

### 4. Push / score / badge glyph swaps (en mode)

| Concept | zh-TW | en | Width parity |
|---|---|---|---|
| Push (+1) | `推` | `👍` | 2 = 2 ✓ |
| Boo (-1) | `噓` | `👎` | 2 = 2 ✓ |
| Arrow (0) | `→` | `→` | 1 = 1 (universal) |
| Score "exploded" (≥100) | `爆` | `💥` | 2 = 2 ✓ |
| Locked badge | `[鎖]` | `[L]` | 4 → 3 (acceptable; prefix, not column-aligned — `screen_board_view.go:324-326` runs through runewidth-aware `Truncate`) |
| Arrows-only badge | `[箭]` | `[A]` | 4 → 3 (same) |
| Pinned badge | `[M]` | `[M]` | 3 (universal) |

**Rewire** (rename existing helpers to take a `Locale` arg, delegate to `i18n.PushGlyph`):
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:665-675` — `renderPushKind(loc, k)` → `i18n.PushGlyph` + existing lipgloss styles.
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:677-690` — `kindLabel(loc, k)` → `i18n.PushVerb`.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:301-307` — `case score >= 100` returns `i18n.ScoreExploded(loc)`.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:318-323` — `[鎖]`/`[箭]` prefix → `i18n.CommentsModeBadge(loc, ...)`.
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:573-577` — `[箭] 僅開放箭頭` / `[鎖] 已關閉留言` headers → compose `CommentsModeBadge` + `i18n.T(loc, "screen.article_view.{arrows,locked}_label")`.

### 5. Webhook notification titles → recipient locale

Push/WB/mail webhook events should render in the **recipient's** locale (their phone, their language), not the sender's. Lookup happens at the call site — keeps `internal/notify/dispatcher.go` string-agnostic (no need to import `internal/i18n` from the dispatcher package).

Touched call sites:
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:539-547` — push notification: `Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.article.AuthorID)` → `i18n.Tf(recipientLoc, "notify.push.title", senderUserID, i18n.PushVerb(recipientLoc, m.pushKind))`.
- `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:13-24` (`notifyWB`) — recipient lookup, `i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.wb.title", fromUserID)`.
- `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:28-44` (`notifyMail`) — recipient lookup, `i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.mail.title", fromUserID, subject)`.

Graceful degradation: if `GetByID` fails (deleted-user race), fall back to `i18n.Default`.

### 6. String migration — phased over 3 PRs

**PR 1 — foundation (zh-only render still):** migration 0010, User struct + repo method, full `internal/i18n` package with all keys + glyph helpers + tests. `en.go` empty (every `T(en, key)` falls back to zh-TW so UI is bit-identical to today). Add `screen_locale_settings.go` + Root.navigate case + user-settings menu entry.

**PR 2 — populate `en.go` and convert hard-coded strings, screen by screen.** Recommended order (least state to most): `screen_main_menu.go` → `screen_user_settings.go` → `screen_locale_settings.go` → `screen_board_list.go` → `screen_board_view.go` → `screen_article_view.go` (excluding glyph rewires) → article_edit/export/post_compose → register/password_change/bio_edit → wb/mail/online/admin_users → notify_settings/board_banner_edit/board_splash → `help.go` keymap. Also localize webhook titles per §5.

**PR 3 — push glyph swap + width tests.** Rewire `renderPushKind`/`kindLabel`/`ScoreExploded`/`CommentsModeBadge` per §4. Add `glyphs_test.go` width-parity assertion. Update `help.go` Article-view help entry that hard-codes `推 / 噓 / →` to use locale glyphs.

This phasing isolates the highest-risk visual change (emoji widths in real terminals) into a small reviewable PR with a dedicated test.

### 7. Files modified / created

**Created:**
- `internal/store/migrations/0010_add_user_locale.sql`
- `internal/i18n/{i18n,keys,zh_tw,en,glyphs}.go`
- `internal/i18n/{i18n,glyphs}_test.go`

- `internal/tui/screen_locale_settings.go` + `_test.go`

**Modified:**

- `internal/store/pushes.go` (comment-only — reinforce the canonical-glyph invariant)
- `internal/tui/messages.go` (`ScreenLocaleSettings` const)

- `internal/tui/help.go` (register screen, localize section headings)
- `internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go` (3rd entry + numeric range)
- `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go` + `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:540` (recipient-locale lookup)

- Tests that grep for CJK headers/badges (e.g. `screen_board_view_test.go:339,350-351,383`, `screen_article_view_test.go:889-890`, `help_test.go`) — parameterize by locale or assert against `i18n.T(LocaleZHTW, key)`

**Untouched:**
- `internal/markdown/markdown.go`, `cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go` (round-trip layer — see Hard invariant)
- `internal/notify/dispatcher.go` (call sites pre-render strings; dispatcher stays string-agnostic)

### 8. Testing

`internal/i18n/i18n_test.go`:
1. **Key completeness** — every `zhTW` key exists in `en` and vice-versa; failure prints the missing key list.
2. **Format-arg parity** — for each key, count `%s`/`%d`/`%v`/`%q` in both locale values; mismatch fails (catches a translator dropping a `%s` which would emit `%!(MISSING)` at runtime).
3. **`Normalize` table-driven** — `("","fr","zh","zh-TW","en")` → `(Default, Default, Default, LocaleZHTW, LocaleEN)`.

`internal/i18n/glyphs_test.go`:
- **Width parity** using `github.com/mattn/go-runewidth` (already a dep via `internal/tui/runewidth.go`): assert `runewidth.StringWidth(PushGlyph(LocaleZHTW, k)) == runewidth.StringWidth(PushGlyph(LocaleEN, k))` for every `PushKind`. Same for `ScoreExploded`. Catches any future "improvement" to a 1-cell ASCII glyph that would silently break article-list column math.

`internal/tui/screen_locale_settings_test.go`:
- Toggle to `en`, `Ctrl+S`, assert `*deps.User` updated in place AND DB row updated via fresh `Users().GetByID`.

`internal/tui/notify_helpers_test.go` (extend or new):
- Insert a recipient User with `Locale: "en"`, send a push event, capture via a recording dispatcher (analogous to `chat.fakeSender` in `internal/chat/broker_test.go`), assert `Title` is in English.

`internal/markdown/markdown_test.go` (extend the round-trip test):
- `Format` a push originating from an `en`-locale user, assert the output literally contains `推` (NOT `👍`); then `Parse` and assert the kind round-trips.

CI: `make test-race` (the project's stated CI standard).

### 9. Verification end-to-end

Automated:
\```bash
make test-race
go test -race ./internal/i18n/...
go test -race ./internal/tui/... -run TestLocaleSettings
go test -race ./internal/markdown/... -run TestRoundtripPushGlyphCanonical
\```

Manual two-user smoke (after PR 3):
\```bash
make hostkey       # if not already done
make db-reset      # fresh DB so 0010 runs cleanly
make run
# new shell:
ssh new@localhost -p 2222   # register "alice"
# main menu in zh-TW (default). Navigate: 個人設定 → 語言 Language.
# Move cursor to English, Enter, Ctrl+S. Toast: "✓ Locale saved".
# Esc back to main menu — header now "Main Menu"; items "Boards", "Online", etc.
# Open a board, open an article: 推 column shows 👍 (green), 噓 shows 👎 (red), → unchanged.
# With recommend_score >= 100 (doctor in DB if needed), board list shows 💥 not 爆.
# Lock comments on article (mod): prefix shows [L], header says "[L] Comments locked".

# new shell:
ssh bob@localhost -p 2222   # register "bob", leave at zh-TW default.
# bob pushes alice's article. alice's webhook receives "[BBS] bob 👍 your post".
# alice pushes bob's article. bob's webhook receives "[BBS] alice 推了你的文章".
\```

Markdown round-trip (proves Hard invariant):
\```bash
./sshbbs export --article 5 --include-pushes > /tmp/a.md
grep -E '^- (推|噓|→) \[' /tmp/a.md      # MUST find canonical zh glyphs
! grep -E '^- (👍|👎) \[' /tmp/a.md       # MUST NOT find emoji
./sshbbs import --file /tmp/a.md --board Welcome   # round-trip back in
\```

Schema:
\```bash
sqlite3 data/bbs.db ".schema users" | grep -i locale
# Expect: locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW'
sqlite3 data/bbs.db "SELECT user_id, locale FROM users;"
# Every existing row should show 'zh-TW' (DEFAULT applied to backfill).
\```
