## Approved Plan:
# English Mode (i18n: zh-TW + en)

## Context

The TUI currently hard-codes ~150 user-visible CJK strings across `internal/tui/` (headers, menu items, status flashes, errors, confirmations, help lines, placeholders). A handful of strings also live in `internal/store/pushes.go` (push glyphs, used by markdown export round-trip), `internal/store/boards.go` (seed content), and `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go` + `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:544` (webhook notification titles).

Product owner direction:
- Add a per-user locale preference (`zh-TW` default, `en` opt-in) toggled from User Settings.
- In `en` mode, render `推`/`噓` as `👍`/`👎` so column widths stay stable (1:1 cell-width parity); other labels can be plain English translations.
- Keep zh-TW as the default and the canonical-feeling locale.

Non-goals (record-only, may surface as TODO.md items later):
- Per-board locale overrides
- Locale beyond zh-TW + en (ja/ko/zh-CN)
- SSH `LANG` env detection
- Locale-aware date formatting

## Hard invariant

**`store.PushKind.Glyph()` (`internal/store/pushes.go:33-43`) stays canonical `推`/`噓`/`→` regardless of locale.** It is the serializer for `markdown.Format` (`internal/markdown/markdown.go:143`) and the parser at `internal/markdown/markdown.go:296-303` only recognises those three CJK glyphs. Localising it would silently break `sshbbs export` → `sshbbs import` round-trip (CLI path: `cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go:54`).

Locale-aware glyphs go through a new `internal/i18n/glyphs.go` and are consumed only by TUI render functions.

## Approach

### 1. New `internal/i18n` package (Go map literals, not JSON)

```
internal/i18n/
  i18n.go      // Locale type, Valid, Normalize, T, Tf
  keys.go      // const Key... = "screen.main_menu.title" string identifiers
  zh_tw.go     // var zhTW = map[string]string{...}
  en.go        // var en   = map[string]string{...}
  glyphs.go    // PushGlyph / PushVerb / ScoreExploded / CommentsModeBadge
  i18n_test.go
  glyphs_test.go
```

API:
```go
type Locale string
const (
    LocaleZHTW Locale = "zh-TW"
    LocaleEN   Locale = "en"
    Default           = LocaleZHTW
)

func Valid(s string) bool
func Normalize(s string) Locale            // empty / unrecognised → Default
func T(loc Locale, key string) string      // missing in `en` → falls back to zh-TW; missing in both → «key» so it stands out
func Tf(loc Locale, key string, args ...any) string  // fmt.Sprintf wrapper

// Locale-aware glyphs (TUI only — never feed into markdown.Format)
func PushGlyph(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string         // 推/噓/→  vs  👍/👎/→
func PushVerb(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string          // for templated webhook titles
func ScoreExploded(loc Locale) string                       // 爆 vs 💥
func CommentsModeBadge(loc Locale, m store.CommentsMode) string  // [鎖]/[箭] vs [L]/[A]
```

Why Go maps over embedded JSON: compile-time key safety via `keys.go` consts, no loader I/O, two locales fit comfortably in literals, mirrors the leaf-package style used by `internal/markdown`.

Key naming: flat dotted, grouped by screen — `screen.<basename>.<role>`, plus `common.*`, `error.*`, `notify.*`, `help.<screen>.*`. Both locales must declare the same set of keys with the same `%s`/`%d`/`%v` directive count (asserted by `i18n_test.go`).

### 2. Locale storage + plumbing

**Migration `internal/store/migrations/0010_add_user_locale.sql`:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW';
```
Picked up automatically by `internal/store/migrate.go`'s `go:embed migrations/*.sql`.

**`internal/store/users.go` edits** (mirror the `Bio` precedent — bio was added the same way in migration `0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`):
- Add `Locale string` to `User` struct (after `Bio`).
- Append `, locale` to `userColumns` const (line 34-35).
- Append `&u.Locale` to `scanUser` Scan list (line 40-44).
- Add repo method `func (r *UserRepo) SetLocale(ctx, id int64, locale string) error` — takes `r.s.writeMu.Lock()` per `Store.writeMu` discipline; mirror `SetBio` shape. Caller validates via `i18n.Valid` first.

**`internal/tui/i18n.go` (new shim):**
```go

    if d.User == nil { return i18n.Default }   // register / pre-login
    return i18n.Normalize(d.User.Locale)
}
```

**Refresh after locale change**: mirror `internal/tui/screen_bio_edit.go:79-83`:

- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:539-547` — push notification: `Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.article.AuthorID)` → `i18n.Tf(recipientLoc, "notify.push.title", senderUserID, i18n.PushVerb(recipientLoc, m.pushKind))`.
- `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:13-24` (`notifyWB`) — recipient lookup, `i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.wb.title", fromUserID)`.
- `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go:28-44` (`notifyMail`) — recipient lookup, `i18n.Tf(loc, "notify.mail.title", fromUserID, subject)`.

Graceful degradation: if `GetByID` fails (deleted-user race), fall back to `i18n.Default`.

### 6. String migration — phased over 3 PRs

**PR 1 — foundation (zh-only render still):** migration 0010, User struct + repo method, full `internal/i18n` package with all keys + glyph helpers + tests. `en.go` empty (every `T(en, key)` falls back to zh-TW so UI is bit-identical to today). Add `screen_locale_settings.go` + Root.navigate case + user-settings menu entry.

**PR 2 — populate `en.go` and convert hard-coded strings, screen by screen.** Recommended order (least state to most): `screen_main_menu.go` → `screen_user_settings.go` → `screen_locale_settings.go` → `screen_board_list.go` → `screen_board_view.go` → `screen_article_view.go` (excluding glyph rewires) → article_edit/export/post_compose → register/password_change/bio_edit → wb/mail/online/admin_users → notify_settings/board_banner_edit/board_splash → `help.go` keymap. Also localize webhook titles per §5.

**PR 3 — push glyph swap + width tests.** Rewire `renderPushKind`/`kindLabel`/`ScoreExploded`/`CommentsModeBadge` per §4. Add `glyphs_test.go` width-parity assertion. Update `help.go` Article-view help entry that hard-codes `推 / 噓 / →` to use locale glyphs.

This phasing isolates the highest-risk visual change (emoji widths in real terminals) into a small reviewable PR with a dedicated test.

### 7. Files modified / created

**Created:**
- `internal/store/migrations/0010_add_user_locale.sql`
- `internal/i18n/{i18n,keys,zh_tw,en,glyphs}.go`
- `internal/i18n/{i18n,glyphs}_test.go`
- `internal/tui/i18n.go` (the `localeOf` shim)
- `internal/tui/screen_locale_settings.go` + `_test.go`

**Modified:**
- `internal/store/users.go` (struct + columns + scan + `SetLocale`)
- `internal/store/pushes.go` (comment-only — reinforce the canonical-glyph invariant)
- `internal/tui/messages.go` (`ScreenLocaleSettings` const)
- `internal/tui/root.go` (navigate case; localize the existing CJK error toasts at lines 167/181)
- `internal/tui/help.go` (register screen, localize section headings)
- `internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go` (3rd entry + numeric range)
- `internal/tui/notify_helpers.go` + `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:540` (recipient-locale lookup)
- Every `internal/tui/screen_*.go` containing CJK literals (~20 files; mechanical T() replacement)
- Tests that grep for CJK headers/badges (e.g. `screen_board_view_test.go:339,350-351,383`, `screen_article_view_test.go:889-890`, `help_test.go`) — parameterize by locale or assert against `i18n.T(LocaleZHTW, key)`

**Untouched:**
- `internal/markdown/markdown.go`, `cmd/sshbbs/cmd_import.go` (round-trip layer — see Hard invariant)
- `internal/notify/dispatcher.go` (call sites pre-render strings; dispatcher stays string-agnostic)
- `internal/store/migrate.go` (embed glob auto-picks `0010_*`)
- `internal/store/boards.go` seed titles (content, not UI chrome — admin can rename per deployment)

### 8. Testing

`internal/i18n/i18n_test.go`:
1. **Key completeness** — every `zhTW` key exists in `en` and vice-versa; failure prints the missing key list.
2. **Format-arg parity** — for each key, count `%s`/`%d`/`%v`/`%q` in both locale values; mismatch fails (catches a translator dropping a `%s` which would emit `%!(MISSING)` at runtime).
3. **`Normalize` table-driven** — `("","fr","zh","zh-TW","en")` → `(Default, Default, Default, LocaleZHTW, LocaleEN)`.

`internal/i18n/glyphs_test.go`:
- **Width parity** using `github.com/mattn/go-runewidth` (already a dep via `internal/tui/runewidth.go`): assert `runewidth.StringWidth(PushGlyph(LocaleZHTW, k)) == runewidth.StringWidth(PushGlyph(LocaleEN, k))` for every `PushKind`. Same for `ScoreExploded`. Catches any future "improvement" to a 1-cell ASCII glyph that would silently break article-list column math.

`internal/tui/screen_locale_settings_test.go`:
- Toggle to `en`, `Ctrl+S`, assert `*deps.User` updated in place AND DB row updated via fresh `Users().GetByID`.

`internal/tui/notify_helpers_test.go` (extend or new):
- Insert a recipient User with `Locale: "en"`, send a push event, capture via a recording dispatcher (analogous to `chat.fakeSender` in `internal/chat/broker_test.go`), assert `Title` is in English.

`internal/markdown/markdown_test.go` (extend the round-trip test):
- `Format` a push originating from an `en`-locale user, assert the output literally contains `推` (NOT `👍`); then `Parse` and assert the kind round-trips.

CI: `make test-race` (the project's stated CI standard).

### 9. Verification end-to-end

Automated:
```bash
make test-race
go test -race ./internal/i18n/...
go test -race ./internal/tui/... -run TestLocaleSettings
go test -race ./internal/markdown/... -run TestRoundtripPushGlyphCanonical
```

Manual two-user smoke (after PR 3):
```bash
make hostkey       # if not already done
make db-reset      # fresh DB so 0010 runs cleanly
make run
# new shell:
ssh new@localhost -p 2222   # register "alice"
# main menu in zh-TW (default). Navigate: 個人設定 → 語言 Language.
# Move cursor to English, Enter, Ctrl+S. Toast: "✓ Locale saved".
# Esc back to main menu — header now "Main Menu"; items "Boards", "Online", etc.
# Open a board, open an article: 推 column shows 👍 (green), 噓 shows 👎 (red), → unchanged.
# With recommend_score >= 100 (doctor in DB if needed), board list shows 💥 not 爆.
# Lock comments on article (mod): prefix shows [L], header says "[L] Comments locked".

# new shell:
ssh bob@localhost -p 2222   # register "bob", leave at zh-TW default.
# bob pushes alice's article. alice's webhook receives "[BBS] bob 👍 your post".
# alice pushes bob's article. bob's webhook receives "[BBS] alice 推了你的文章".
```

Markdown round-trip (proves Hard invariant):
```bash
./sshbbs export --article 5 --include-pushes > /tmp/a.md
grep -E '^- (推|噓|→) \[' /tmp/a.md      # MUST find canonical zh glyphs
! grep -E '^- (👍|👎) \[' /tmp/a.md       # MUST NOT find emoji
./sshbbs import --file /tmp/a.md --board Welcome   # round-trip back in
```

Schema:
```bash
sqlite3 data/bbs.db ".schema users" | grep -i locale
# Expect: locale TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zh-TW'
sqlite3 data/bbs.db "SELECT user_id, locale FROM users;"
# Every existing row should show 'zh-TW' (DEFAULT applied to backfill).
```

37	func scanUser(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*User, error) {
38		u := &User{}

42			&u.NumLogins, &u.NumPosts, &u.LastLoginAt, &u.LastHost, &u.CreatedAt,
43			&u.Role, &mustChange, &u.Bio,
44		)

49	func (r *UserRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash, nickname, email string) (*User, error) {
50		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
51		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
52		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
53			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, nickname, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
54			userID, passwordHash, nickname, email,
55		)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, err
58		}
59		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
60		if err != nil {
61			return nil, err
62		}
63		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
64	}

67		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id)
68		u, err := scanUser(row)
69		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
70			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
71		}
72		return u, err
73	}

76		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE user_id = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, userID)
77		u, err := scanUser(row)

131		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
132		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()

154	func (r *UserRepo) SetRole(ctx context.Context, id int64, role Role) error {
155		if !role.Valid() {
156			return ErrInvalidRole
157		}
158		r.s.writeMu.Lock()

166	// ListAll returns users ordered by id, paginated. limit<=0 defaults to 20.
167	func (r *UserRepo) ListAll(ctx context.Context, limit, offset int) ([]*User, error) {
168		if limit <= 0 {
169			limit = 20
170		}
171		if offset < 0 {
172			offset = 0
173		}
174		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
175			`SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`,
176			limit, offset,
177		)
178		if err != nil {
179			return nil, err
180		}
181		defer rows.Close()
182		var out []*User
183		for rows.Next() {
184			u, err := scanUser(rows)
185			if err != nil {
186				return nil, err
187			}
188			out = append(out, u)
189		}
190		return out, rows.Err()
191	}
192

 `./internal/store/migrations/0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`

```text
1	-- User settings (bio) plus a per-user notification system.
2	-- bio is a free-form profile blurb. Empty default keeps existing rows valid.
3	-- user_notif_prefs holds the four event toggles (push / wb / mail / reply)
4	-- plus only_when_offline. Row is upserted lazily; absence ≡ all-default.
5	-- user_notif_targets stores per-user webhook URLs that the dispatcher
6	-- POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to. Compatible with caronc/apprise-api's
7	-- /notify/<key> endpoint and any other simple webhook (Discord, ntfy.sh,
8	-- Slack, custom). The BBS deliberately does NOT parse apprise:// URLs —
9	-- that is delegated to whatever webhook target the user points us at.
10	
11	ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';
12	
13	CREATE TABLE user_notif_prefs (
14	    user_id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
15	    on_push           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 推/噓/→ on my article
16	    on_wb             INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 水球
17	    on_mail           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 站內信
18	    on_reply          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 文章被回 (Re:) — fired when someone posts a Re: reply targeting one of my articles
19	    only_when_offline INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0  -- skip dispatch if any live session exists
20	);
21	
22	CREATE TABLE user_notif_targets (
23	    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
24	    user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
25	    label      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
26	    url        TEXT NOT NULL,
27	    enabled    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
28	    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
29	);
30	CREATE INDEX idx_notif_targets_user ON user_notif_targets(user_id, enabled);

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/migrate.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"embed"
7		"fmt"
8		"sort"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11	)
12	
13	//go:embed migrations/*.sql
14	var migrationsFS embed.FS
15	
16	// apply runs every migration whose version number isn't already in
17	// schema_migrations. Filenames must be NNNN_name.sql; the leading integer is
18	// the version. Migrations are applied in ascending version order, each in
19	// its own transaction.
20	func apply(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {
21		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, `
22			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
23				version    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
24				applied_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
25			)
26		`); err != nil {
27			return fmt.Errorf("create schema_migrations: %w", err)
28		}
29	
30		applied, err := loadApplied(ctx, db)
31		if err != nil {
32			return err
33		}
34	
35		entries, err := migrationsFS.ReadDir("migrations")
36		if err != nil {
37			return fmt.Errorf("read embedded migrations: %w", err)
38		}
39		type migration struct {
40			version  int
41			filename string
42		}
43		var pending []migration
44		for _, e := range entries {
45			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".sql") {
46				continue
47			}
48			v, err := parseVersion(e.Name())
49			if err != nil {
50				return err
51			}
52			if !applied[v] {
53				pending = append(pending, migration{version: v, filename: e.Name()})
54			}
55		}
56		sort.Slice(pending, func(i, j int) bool { return pending[i].version < pending[j].version })
57	
58		for _, m := range pending {
59			body, err := migrationsFS.ReadFile("migrations/" + m.filename)
60			if err != nil {
61				return fmt.Errorf("read migration %s: %w", m.filename, err)
62			}
63			if err := runOne(ctx, db, m.version, string(body)); err != nil {
64				return fmt.Errorf("apply %s: %w", m.filename, err)
65			}
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	func loadApplied(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (map[int]bool, error) {
71		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, `SELECT version FROM schema_migrations`)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, fmt.Errorf("load applied migrations: %w", err)
74		}
75		defer rows.Close()
76		out := map[int]bool{}
77		for rows.Next() {
78			var v int
79			if err := rows.Scan(&v); err != nil {
80				return nil, err
81			}
82			out[v] = true
83		}
84		return out, rows.Err()
85	}
86	
87	func runOne(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, version int, body string) error {
88		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
89		if err != nil {
90			return err
91		}
92		defer tx.Rollback()
93		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, body); err != nil {
94			return err
95		}
96		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO schema_migrations(version) VALUES (?)`, version); err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		return tx.Commit()
100	}
101	
102	func parseVersion(filename string) (int, error) {
103		// "0001_init.sql" -> 1
104		idx := strings.IndexByte(filename, '_')
105		if idx <= 0 {
106			return 0, fmt.Errorf("migration filename missing version prefix: %s", filename)
107		}
108		v, err := strconv.Atoi(filename[:idx])
109		if err != nil {
110			return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse version in %s: %w", filename, err)
111		}
112		return v, nil
113	}
114
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// NotifyPrefs is the per-user notification toggle bag. The boolean fields map
12	// 1:1 to the `on_*` columns in user_notif_prefs. Defaults (all event toggles
13	// on, only_when_offline off) are returned when the row is absent — the row is
14	// upserted lazily on the first SetPrefs call.
15	type NotifyPrefs struct {
16		OnPush          bool
17		OnWB            bool
18		OnMail          bool
19		OnReply         bool
20		OnlyWhenOffline bool
21	}
22	
23	// DefaultNotifyPrefs is what GetPrefs returns for users that have never
24	// touched the notify settings screen. Mirrors the column DEFAULTs in
25	// migrations/0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql.
26	func DefaultNotifyPrefs() NotifyPrefs {
27		return NotifyPrefs{OnPush: true, OnWB: true, OnMail: true, OnReply: true}
28	}
29	
30	// NotifyTarget is one configured webhook URL for a user. The dispatcher
31	// POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to URL when an event fires; Enabled lets the
32	// user pause a target without losing it.
33	type NotifyTarget struct {
34		ID        int64
35		UserID    int64
36		Label     string
37		URL       string
38		Enabled   bool
39		CreatedAt time.Time
40	}
41	
42	// NotifyRepo owns the user_notif_prefs and user_notif_targets tables.
43	type NotifyRepo struct{ s *Store }
44	
45	var (
46		ErrNotifyTargetNotFound = errors.New("notify target not found")
47		ErrInvalidNotifyURL     = errors.New("notify target url must be http(s)://...")
48	)
49	
50	// ValidateNotifyURL enforces the bare minimum: scheme must be http or https.
51	// Anything beyond that (DNS, reachability) is the dispatcher's problem at
52	// delivery time — failing here would block legitimate self-hosted endpoints
53	// behind VPNs / private DNS.
54	func ValidateNotifyURL(u string) error {
55		u = strings.TrimSpace(u)
56		if strings.HasPrefix(u, "http://") || strings.HasPrefix(u, "https://") {
57			return nil
58		}
59		return ErrInvalidNotifyURL
60	}
61	
62	// GetPrefs returns the user's stored prefs, or DefaultNotifyPrefs() when
63	// the row is absent.
64	func (r *NotifyRepo) GetPrefs(ctx context.Context, userID int64) (NotifyPrefs, error) {
65		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
66			`SELECT on_push, on_wb, on_mail, on_reply, only_when_offline
67			 FROM user_notif_prefs WHERE user_id = ?`, userID,
68		)
69		var onPush, onWB, onMail, onReply, onlyOffline int64
70		err := row.Scan(&onPush, &onWB, &onMail, &onReply, &onlyOffline)
71		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
72			return DefaultNotifyPrefs(), nil
73		}
74		if err != nil {
75			return NotifyPrefs{}, err
76		}
77		return NotifyPrefs{
78			OnPush:          onPush != 0,
79			OnWB:            onWB != 0,
80			OnMail:          onMail != 0,
81			OnReply:         onReply != 0,
82			OnlyWhenOffline: onlyOffline != 0,
83		}, nil
84	}
85	
86	// SetPrefs upserts the prefs row for a user.
87	func (r *NotifyRepo) SetPrefs(ctx context.Context, userID int64, p NotifyPrefs) error {
88		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
89		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
90		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
91			`INSERT INTO user_notif_prefs
92			   (user_id, on_push, on_wb, on_mail, on_reply, only_when_offline)
93			 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
94			 ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE SET
95			   on_push=excluded.on_push,
96			   on_wb=excluded.on_wb,
97			   on_mail=excluded.on_mail,
98			   on_reply=excluded.on_reply,
99			   only_when_offline=excluded.only_when_offline`,
100			userID, b2i(p.OnPush), b2i(p.OnWB), b2i(p.OnMail), b2i(p.OnReply), b2i(p.OnlyWhenOffline),
101		)
102		return err
103	}
104	
105	// ListTargets returns every target row for a user, oldest-first so the
106	// settings screen renders a stable list.
107	func (r *NotifyRepo) ListTargets(ctx context.Context, userID int64) ([]*NotifyTarget, error) {
108		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
109			`SELECT id, user_id, label, url, enabled, created_at
110			 FROM user_notif_targets WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, userID,
111		)
112		if err != nil {
113			return nil, err
114		}
115		defer rows.Close()
116		var out []*NotifyTarget
117		for rows.Next() {
118			t := &NotifyTarget{}
119			var enabled int64
120			if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.UserID, &t.Label, &t.URL, &enabled, &t.CreatedAt); err != nil {
121				return nil, err
122			}
123			t.Enabled = enabled != 0
124			out = append(out, t)
125		}
126		return out, rows.Err()
127	}
128	
129	// ListEnabledTargets is the dispatcher's hot path: only enabled rows.
130	func (r *NotifyRepo) ListEnabledTargets(ctx context.Context, userID int64) ([]*NotifyTarget, error) {
131		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
132			`SELECT id, user_id, label, url, enabled, created_at
133			 FROM user_notif_targets WHERE user_id = ? AND enabled = 1 ORDER BY id ASC`, userID,
134		)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, err
137		}
138		defer rows.Close()
139		var out []*NotifyTarget
140		for rows.Next() {
141			t := &NotifyTarget{}
142			var enabled int64
143			if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.UserID, &t.Label, &t.URL, &enabled, &t.CreatedAt); err != nil {
144				return nil, err
145			}
146			t.Enabled = enabled != 0
147			out = append(out, t)
148		}
149		return out, rows.Err()
150	}
151	
152	// AddTarget validates the URL and inserts a new row. Returns the assigned id.
153	func (r *NotifyRepo) AddTarget(ctx context.Context, userID int64, label, url string) (int64, error) {
154		if err := ValidateNotifyURL(url); err != nil {
155			return 0, err
156		}
157		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
158		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
159		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
160			`INSERT INTO user_notif_targets (user_id, label, url) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`,
161			userID, strings.TrimSpace(label), strings.TrimSpace(url),
162		)
163		if err != nil {
164			return 0, err
165		}
166		return res.LastInsertId()
167	}
168	
169	// UpdateTarget mutates label/url/enabled in place. URL is validated.
170	// requesterUserID is checked against the row's user_id so a malicious
171	// caller can't update someone else's target by guessing the id.
172	func (r *NotifyRepo) UpdateTarget(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64, label, url string, enabled bool) error {
173		if err := ValidateNotifyURL(url); err != nil {
174			return err
175		}
176		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
177		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
178		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
179			`UPDATE user_notif_targets SET label = ?, url = ?, enabled = ?
180			 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
181			strings.TrimSpace(label), strings.TrimSpace(url), b2i(enabled), id, requesterUserID,
182		)
183		if err != nil {
184			return err
185		}
186		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
187		if n == 0 {
188			return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
189		}
190		return nil
191	}
192	
193	// SetTargetEnabled is the lightweight toggle path used by the `t` shortcut
194	// in the settings screen — separate from UpdateTarget to avoid re-asserting
195	// label/url validation on every keystroke.
196	func (r *NotifyRepo) SetTargetEnabled(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64, enabled bool) error {
197		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
198		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
199		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
200			`UPDATE user_notif_targets SET enabled = ?
201			 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
202			b2i(enabled), id, requesterUserID,
203		)
204		if err != nil {
205			return err
206		}
207		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
208		if n == 0 {
209			return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
210		}
211		return nil
212	}
213	
214	// DeleteTarget removes a row, gated on user_id so the caller can only
215	// delete their own targets.
216	func (r *NotifyRepo) DeleteTarget(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64) error {
217		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
218		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
219		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
220			`DELETE FROM user_notif_targets WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
221			id, requesterUserID,
222		)
223		if err != nil {
224			return err
225		}
226		n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
227		if n == 0 {
228			return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
229		}
230		return nil
231	}
232	
233	func b2i(b bool) int64 {
234		if b {
235			return 1
236		}
237		return 0
238	}
239

1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14		ScreenWBThread
15		ScreenOnline
16		ScreenMailInbox
17		ScreenMailThread
18		ScreenMailCompose
19		ScreenPasswordChange
20		ScreenAdminUsers
21		ScreenArticleEdit
22		ScreenArticleExport
23		ScreenBoardSplash
24		ScreenBoardBannerEdit
25		ScreenUserSettings
26		ScreenBioEdit
27		ScreenNotifySettings
28	)
29	
30	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
31	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
32	type NavigateMsg struct {
33		To           Screen
34		BoardID      int64
35		ArticleID    int64
36		Recipient          string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
37		MailID             int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
38		MailThreadID       int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
39		CounterpartyUserID int64  // for ScreenWBThread: which 1-to-1 thread to open
40	}
41	
42	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
43	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
44	
45	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
46	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
47	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
48		ID         int64
49		FromUserID string
50		Body       string
51	}
52	
53	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
54	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
55	// re-render.
56	type PushAddedMsg struct {
57		ArticleID  int64
58		UserUserID string
59		Kind       string
60		Body       string
61	}
62	
63	// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
64	// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
65	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
66	// counter from DB.
67	type MailIncomingMsg struct {
68		ID         int64
69		ThreadID   int64
70		FromUserID string
71		Subject    string
72	}
73	
74	// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
75	// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
76	// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
77	type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
78		BoardID      int64
79		ArticleID    int64
80		AuthorUserID string
81		Title        string
82	}
83	
84	// ArticleUpdatedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when an article's
85	// title or body is edited. Article-view models filter by ArticleID and
86	// re-fetch from DB so they don't render stale text.
87	type ArticleUpdatedMsg struct {
88		ArticleID int64
89	}
90	
91	// ArticlePinChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod pins or unpins an article.
92	// Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article list so the
93	// pinned-first ordering and the [M] marker stay current across all live
94	// sessions viewing the same board. ArticleID + Pinned are advisory; the
95	// re-fetch is the source of truth.
96	type ArticlePinChangedMsg struct {
97		BoardID   int64
98		ArticleID int64
99		Pinned    bool
100	}
101	
102	// ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod changes an article's
103	// comments_mode (open / arrows_only / locked). Article-view models filter
104	// by ArticleID; board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch their list
105	// so the [鎖]/[箭] badges stay current. Mode is the new CommentsMode string;
106	// receivers should re-fetch from DB rather than trust the payload.
107	type ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg struct {
108		BoardID   int64
109		ArticleID int64
110		Mode      string // store.CommentsMode value, kept as string for tui-package isolation
111	}
112

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/root.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/notify"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
16	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
17	type Deps struct {
18		Store              *store.Store
19		Broker             *chat.Broker    // nil during register
20		Notify             *notify.Manager // may be nil in tests; call sites must guard
21		User               *store.User     // nil iff IsRegister
22		IsRegister         bool
23		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
24	}
25	
26	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
27	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
28	// and the toast footer.
29	type Root struct {
30		deps   Deps
31		sub    tea.Model
32		width  int
33		height int
34	
35		toast      string
36		toastUntil time.Time
37	
38		// activeScreen tracks which Screen the current sub corresponds to, so
39		// the help overlay can render a context-aware keymap. Set in NewRoot
40		// for the bootstrap sub and in navigate for every subsequent swap.
41		activeScreen Screen
42		// helpVisible is true while the help overlay should render in place of
43		// the sub view. Toggled on '?' and dismissed by any key.
44		helpVisible bool
45	}
46	
47	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
48	
49	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
50	//
51	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
52	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
53	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
54	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
55		r := Root{deps: deps}
56		switch {
57		case deps.IsRegister:
58			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
59			// No Screen const for register; help is suppressed via deps.IsRegister.
60		case deps.MustChangePassword:
61			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
62			r.activeScreen = ScreenPasswordChange
63		default:
64			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
65			r.activeScreen = ScreenMainMenu
66		}
67		return r
68	}
69	
70	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
71		return m.sub.Init()
72	}
73	
74	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
75		switch msg := msg.(type) {
76		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
77			m.width = msg.Width
78			m.height = msg.Height
79			var cmd tea.Cmd
80			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
81			return m, cmd
82	
83		case tea.KeyMsg:
84			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
85			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
86				return m, tea.Quit
87			}
88			// Help overlay: any key dismisses, swallow the keypress so the
89			// sub doesn't act on it. ctrl+c above is the deliberate exception.
90			if m.helpVisible {
91				m.helpVisible = false
92				return m, nil
93			}
94			// '?' opens the help overlay on screens that aren't typing-forms.
95			if msg.String() == "?" && helpAvailable(m.deps, m.activeScreen) {
96				m.helpVisible = true
97				return m, nil
98			}
99			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
100			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
101				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
102			}
103	
104		case NavigateMsg:
105			return m.navigate(msg)
106	
107		case ErrorMsg:
108			if msg.Err != nil {
109				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
110			}
111			return m, nil
112	
113		case WBIncomingMsg:
114			// Suppress the toast when the active screen is a wbThreadModel
115			// pointed at this exact counterparty — the thread itself appends
116			// the message, so the toast would be redundant noise.
117			showToast := true
118			if m.activeScreen == ScreenWBThread {
119				if tm, ok := m.sub.(wbThreadModel); ok && tm.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
120					showToast = false
121				}
122			}
123			if showToast {
124				m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
125			}
126			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect. (The
127			// thread also marks it read — both paths are idempotent.)
128			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
129				go func(id int64) {
130					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
131				}(msg.ID)
132			}
133			// fall through so the active screen can also react
134		}
135	
136		var cmd tea.Cmd
137		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
138		return m, cmd
139	}
140	
141	func (m Root) View() string {
142		if m.helpVisible {
143			// Replace the sub view entirely so the keymap reads cleanly. Toast
144			// suppressed too — would distract from a help screen.
145			return renderHelp(m.activeScreen)
146		}
147		v := m.sub.View()
148		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
149			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
150		}
151		return v
152	}
153	
154	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
155		m.toast = s
156		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
157	}
158	
159	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
160	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
161	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
162		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
163			return nil
164		}
165		switch to {
166		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
167			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
168		}
169		return nil
170	}
171	
172	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
173	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
174	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
175		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
176			return nil
177		}
178		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
179			return nil
180		}
181		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
182	}
183	
184	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
185		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
186			return m, cmd
187		}
188		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
189			return m, cmd
190		}
191		var sub tea.Model
192		switch n.To {
193		case ScreenMainMenu:
194			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
195		case ScreenBoardList:
196			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
197		case ScreenBoardView:
198			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
199		case ScreenArticleView:
200			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
201		case ScreenPostCompose:
202			// n.ArticleID, when non-zero, identifies the parent article — this
203			// path is the "Re: reply" target. screen_article_view's R key sets
204			// it; new-post entry points (e.g., board view's `p` key) leave it 0.
205			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID, n.ArticleID)
206		case ScreenWBInbox:
207			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
208		case ScreenWBCompose:
209			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
210		case ScreenWBThread:
211			sub = newWBThreadModel(m.deps, n.CounterpartyUserID)
212		case ScreenOnline:
213			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
214		case ScreenMailInbox:
215			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
216		case ScreenMailThread:
217			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
218		case ScreenMailCompose:
219			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
220		case ScreenPasswordChange:
221			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
222		case ScreenAdminUsers:
223			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
224		case ScreenArticleEdit:
225			sub = newArticleEditModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
226		case ScreenArticleExport:
227			sub = newArticleExportModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
228		case ScreenBoardSplash:
229			sub = newBoardSplashModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
230		case ScreenBoardBannerEdit:
231			sub = newBoardBannerEditModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
232		case ScreenUserSettings:
233			sub = newUserSettingsModel(m.deps)
234		case ScreenBioEdit:
235			sub = newBioEditModel(m.deps)
236		case ScreenNotifySettings:
237			sub = newNotifySettingsModel(m.deps)
238		default:
239			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
240			return m, nil
241		}
242		m.sub = sub
243		m.activeScreen = n.To
244		cmd := sub.Init()
245		if m.width > 0 {
246			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
247			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
248			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
249			if cmd2 != nil {
250				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
251			}
252		}
253		return m, cmd
254	}
255
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	)
9	
10	// userSettingsModel is the "個人設定" sub-menu reached from the main menu.
11	// Three entries: change password, edit bio, edit notification settings.
12	// Layout and key handling mirror mainMenuModel deliberately so muscle memory
13	// transfers across BBS-internal menus.
14	type userSettingsModel struct {
15		deps   Deps
16		items  []menuItem
17		cursor int
18	}
19	
20	func newUserSettingsModel(deps Deps) userSettingsModel {
21		items := []menuItem{
22			{label: "修改密碼 Change password", hint: "current → new → confirm", to: ScreenPasswordChange},
23			{label: "修改 Bio Edit bio", hint: "free-form profile blurb", to: ScreenBioEdit},
24			{label: "通知設定 Notification settings", hint: "webhook targets + per-event toggles", to: ScreenNotifySettings},
25			{label: "返回 Back", hint: "main menu", to: ScreenMainMenu},
26		}
27		return userSettingsModel{deps: deps, items: items}
28	}
29	
30	func (m userSettingsModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
31	
32	func (m userSettingsModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
33		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
34			switch k.String() {
35			case "esc", "q", "left", "h", "backspace":
36				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
37			case "Q":
38				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
39			case "up", "k":
40				if m.cursor > 0 {
41					m.cursor--
42				}
43				return m, nil
44			case "down", "j":
45				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
46					m.cursor++
47				}
48				return m, nil
49			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
50				it := m.items[m.cursor]
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
52			case "1", "2", "3", "4":
53				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
54				if idx < len(m.items) {
55					it := m.items[idx]
56					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
57				}
58			}
59		}
60		return m, nil
61	}
62	
63	func (m userSettingsModel) View() string {
64		u := m.deps.User
65		var b strings.Builder
66		b.WriteString("\n")
67		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 個人設定 User Settings "))
68		b.WriteString("\n\n")
69	
70		if u != nil {
71			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("帳號 %s · 角色 %s", u.UserID, u.Role)))
72			b.WriteString("\n")
73			bio := strings.TrimSpace(u.Bio)
74			if bio == "" {
75				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(尚未填寫 bio)"))
76			} else {
77				// Show only the first line of bio in the header so the menu
78				// itself stays compact; the bio-edit screen owns the full view.
79				firstLine := bio
80				if i := strings.IndexByte(firstLine, '\n'); i >= 0 {
81					firstLine = firstLine[:i]
82				}
83				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio: ") + Truncate(firstLine, 60))
84			}
85			b.WriteString("\n\n")
86		}
87	
88		for i, it := range m.items {
89			marker := "  "
90			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
91			if i == m.cursor {
92				marker = "▸ "
93				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-40s ", i+1, it.label))
94			}
95			b.WriteString(marker + row)
96			if it.hint != "" {
97				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
98			}
99			b.WriteString("\n")
100		}
101	
102		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-4 jump · Esc/←/h back"))
103		b.WriteString("\n")
104		return b.String()
105	}
106

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
11	)
12	
13	// bioEditModel is a single-textarea form screen reached from the user
14	// settings menu. Form-screen convention (CLAUDE.md): no h/l navigation —
15	// those keys must remain available for text editing.
16	type bioEditModel struct {
17		deps    Deps
18		body    textarea.Model
19		width   int
20		height  int
21		err     string
22		success bool
23	}
24	
25	func newBioEditModel(deps Deps) bioEditModel {
26		ta := textarea.New()
27		ta.Placeholder = "你的 bio…（最多 1024 字元，可換行）"
28		ta.CharLimit = 4096 // generous client-side; auth.ValidateBio is the source of truth (rune count)
29		ta.SetWidth(72)
30		ta.SetHeight(10)
31		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Bio != "" {
32			ta.SetValue(deps.User.Bio)
33		}
34		ta.Focus()
35		return bioEditModel{deps: deps, body: ta}
36	}
37	
38	func (m bioEditModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textarea.Blink }
39	
40	func (m bioEditModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
41		switch msg := msg.(type) {
42		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
43			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
44			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-8))
45			m.body.SetHeight(max(8, msg.Height-12))
46			return m, nil
47	
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			if m.success {
50				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
51			}
52			switch msg.String() {
53			case "esc":
54				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
55			case "ctrl+s":
56				return m.submit()
57			}
58		}
59		var cmd tea.Cmd
60		m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
61		return m, cmd
62	}
63	
64	func (m bioEditModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
65		bio := strings.TrimRight(m.body.Value(), "\n")
66		if err := auth.ValidateBio(bio); err != nil {
67			m.err = err.Error()
68			return m, nil
69		}
70		if m.deps.User == nil {
71			m.err = "internal error: no user"
72			return m, nil
73		}
74		ctx := context.Background()
75		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().SetBio(ctx, m.deps.User.ID, bio); err != nil {
76			m.err = err.Error()
77			return m, nil
78		}
79		// Refresh User in place so the settings screen header and any future
80		// reads see the canonical bio without a re-login.
81		if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
82			*m.deps.User = *fresh
83		}
84		m.success = true
85		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
86	}
87	
88	func (m bioEditModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 修改 Bio Edit bio "))
92		b.WriteString("\n\n")
93	
94		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio (free-form, ≤ 1024 chars; newlines OK):") + "\n")
95		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
96	
97		if m.err != "" {
98			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
99		}
100		if m.success {
101			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ saved"))
102		}
103		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel"))
104		b.WriteString("\n")
105		return b.String()
106	}
107

244	// renderHelp builds the full-screen help overlay for the given screen.
245	// Includes per-screen entries (or a fallback message) plus the globals.
246	func renderHelp(s Screen) string {
247		var b strings.Builder
248		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 鍵盤捷徑說明 Help ") + "\n\n")
249	
250		sections, ok := screenHelp[s]
251		if !ok {
252			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no per-screen keymap registered for this screen)") + "\n\n")
253		} else {
254			for i, sec := range sections {
255				if i > 0 {
256					b.WriteString("\n")
257				}
258				if sec.Heading != "" {
259					b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  "+sec.Heading) + "\n")
260				}
261				for _, e := range sec.Entries {
262					b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
263				}
264			}
265			b.WriteString("\n")
266		}
267	
268		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  Global") + "\n")
269		for _, e := range helpGlobals {
270			b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
271		}
272	
273		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to dismiss"))
274	
275		return helpBox.Render(b.String())
276	}
277	
278	// formatHelpRow pads keys to a stable column width so the descriptions
279	// align — uses the runewidth-aware PadRight because keys often contain
280	// arrows / CJK glyphs.
281	func formatHelpRow(e HelpEntry) string {
282		const keysW = 22
283		return fmt.Sprintf("    %s  %s", PadRight(e.Keys, keysW), e.Desc)
284	}

1	package tui

3	import (
4		"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
5		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
6	)
7	
8	// Force lipgloss's package-level renderer to a profile that supports
9	// background colors. The default renderer auto-detects from os.Stdout,
10	// which is wrong for a server: stdout might be redirected to a log file
11	// (e.g. `./sshbbs >server.log 2>&1 &`), making termenv pick `Ascii` and
12	// silently strip every Background() escape we render. The actual SSH
13	// session renders into the client's terminal (capable of TrueColor in
14	// every modern client), so committing to TrueColor here is safe and
15	// matches what wish's per-session renderer would pick anyway.
16	func init() {
17		lipgloss.SetColorProfile(termenv.TrueColor)
18	}
19	
20	var (
21		StyleHeader = lipgloss.NewStyle().
22				Bold(true).
23				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("231")).
24				Background(lipgloss.Color("63")).
25				Padding(0, 1)
26

29				Background(lipgloss.Color("236")).
30				Padding(0, 1)

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// ErrPushNotFound is returned by PushRepo lookups (Delete, future GetByID)
12	// when the row is missing. ErrPermissionDenied is shared with articles.
13	var ErrPushNotFound = errors.New("push not found")
14	
15	// ErrCommentsLocked / ErrCommentsArrowsOnly are returned by PushRepo.Create
16	// when the parent article's comments_mode rejects the requested kind. The
17	// store-side check is the authoritative gate (TUI also gates early for nicer
18	// errors, but multiple sessions can race against a mode change so the store
19	// is the source of truth). See ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.
20	var (
21		ErrCommentsLocked     = errors.New("comments are locked on this article")
22		ErrCommentsArrowsOnly = errors.New("only arrow comments are allowed on this article")
23	)
24	
25	type PushKind string
26	
27	const (
28		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
29		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
30		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
31	)
32	
33	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
34		switch k {
35		case PushKindPush:
36			return "推"
37		case PushKindBoo:
38			return "噓"
39		case PushKindArrow:
40			return "→"
41		}
42		return "?"
43	}
44	
45	type Push struct {
46		ID          int64
47		ArticleID   int64
48		UserID      int64
49		UserUserID  string
50		Kind        PushKind
51		Body        string
52		CreatedAt   time.Time
53	}
54	
55	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
56	
57	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
58	
59	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
60		p := &Push{}
61		var kind string
62		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
63			return nil, err
64		}
65		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
66		return p, nil
67	}
68	
69	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
70		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
71			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
72		)
73		if err != nil {
74			return nil, err
75		}
76		defer rows.Close()
77		var out []*Push
78		for rows.Next() {
79			p, err := scanPush(rows)
80			if err != nil {
81				return nil, err
82			}
83			out = append(out, p)
84		}
85		return out, rows.Err()
86	}
87	
88	// scoreDelta returns the recommend_score contribution of a push of the
89	// given kind. Positive on add; negate on delete. Centralized so Create
90	// and Delete agree on the algebra.
91	func scoreDelta(kind PushKind) (int, error) {
92		switch kind {
93		case PushKindPush:
94			return 1, nil
95		case PushKindBoo:
96			return -1, nil
97		case PushKindArrow:
98			return 0, nil
99		}
100		return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
101	}
102	
103	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the
104	// article (push: +1, boo: -1, arrow: 0). Both writes happen under the
105	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
106	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
107	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
108		delta, err := scoreDelta(kind)
109		if err != nil {
110			return nil, err
111		}
112	
113		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
114		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
115		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, err
118		}
119		defer tx.Rollback()
120	
121		// Gate on the parent article's comments_mode inside the same tx; with
122		// writeMu held, the SELECT sees the latest committed value. This is the
123		// authoritative check — TUI gates early for nicer errors but races
124		// between sessions are resolved here.
125		var modeStr string
126		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
127			`SELECT comments_mode FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
128		).Scan(&modeStr)
129		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
130			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
131		}
132		if err != nil {
133			return nil, err
134		}
135		switch CommentsMode(modeStr) {
136		case CommentsModeLocked:
137			return nil, ErrCommentsLocked
138		case CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
139			if kind != PushKindArrow {
140				return nil, ErrCommentsArrowsOnly
141			}
142		}
143	
144		res, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
145			`INSERT INTO pushes (article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
146			articleID, userID, userUserID, string(kind), body,
147		)
148		if err != nil {
149			return nil, err
150		}
151		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
152		if err != nil {
153			return nil, err
154		}
155		if delta != 0 {
156			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
157				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`,
158				delta, articleID,
159			); err != nil {
160				return nil, err
161			}
162		}
163		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
164			return nil, err
165		}
166	
167		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, id)
168		return scanPush(row)
169	}
170	
171	// Delete hard-deletes a push and reverts its score contribution on the
172	// parent article. Permission: requester must be the push author OR have
173	// a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrPermissionDenied / ErrPushNotFound.
174	// Mirrors Create's writeMu+tx discipline so concurrent deletes can't
175	// double-revert the score.
176	func (r *PushRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, pushID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
177		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
178		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
179		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
180		if err != nil {
181			return err
182		}
183		defer tx.Rollback()
184	
185		var authorID, articleID int64
186		var kindStr string
187		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
188			`SELECT user_id, article_id, kind FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID,
189		).Scan(&authorID, &articleID, &kindStr)
190		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
191			return ErrPushNotFound
192		}
193		if err != nil {
194			return err
195		}
196		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
197			return ErrPermissionDenied
198		}
199		delta, err := scoreDelta(PushKind(kindStr))
200		if err != nil {
201			return err
202		}
203		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID); err != nil {
204			return err
205		}
206		if delta != 0 {
207			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
208				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score - ? WHERE id = ?`,
209				delta, articleID,
210			); err != nil {
211				return err
212			}
213		}
214		return tx.Commit()
215	}
216

26		deps := Deps{Store: st, User: u}

38		}
39		// Cmd should be a NavigateMsg back to settings.
40		nav, ok := runCmd(cmd).(NavigateMsg)
41		if !ok || nav.To != ScreenUserSettings {
42			t.Errorf("nav = %+v, want ScreenUserSettings", nav)
43		}
44		// In-memory User pointer is updated.
45		if u.Bio != "hello\nworld" {
46			t.Errorf("in-memory bio = %q, want refreshed", u.Bio)
47		}
48		// DB is updated.
49		fresh, _ := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), u.ID)
50		if fresh.Bio != "hello\nworld" {
51			t.Errorf("DB bio = %q", fresh.Bio)
52		}
53	}

11	// CommentsMode controls who can leave 推/噓/→ pushes on an article. The
12	// values are persisted as TEXT in articles.comments_mode (see migration
13	// 0008). States are mutually exclusive — 'locked' is strictly stronger
14	// than 'arrows_only', so a single-column enum keeps the precedence rule
15	// unambiguous. Mod-only mutator: ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.
16	type CommentsMode string
17	
18	const (
19		CommentsModeOpen       CommentsMode = "open"        // default — push, boo, arrow all allowed
20		CommentsModeArrowsOnly CommentsMode = "arrows_only" // only neutral arrow comments
21		CommentsModeLocked     CommentsMode = "locked"      // all pushes rejected
22	)
23	
24	func (m CommentsMode) Valid() bool {
25		switch m {
26		case CommentsModeOpen, CommentsModeArrowsOnly, CommentsModeLocked:
27			return true
28		}
29		return false
30	}
31	
32	type Article struct {
33		ID             int64
34		BoardID        int64
35		AuthorID       int64
36		AuthorUserID   string
37		Title          string
38		Body           string
39		RecommendScore int64
40		Filemode       int64

```text
1	// Package storetest provides shared helpers for tests that need a fresh
2	// SQLite store. It lives in its own package so any internal package can
3	// import it without creating cycles.
4	package storetest
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"testing"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// New opens a fresh on-disk SQLite store in t.TempDir(), runs migrations,
15	// and registers a Cleanup to close it. Use it whenever you'd write
16	// `store.Open` in a test — never share stores across tests.
17	func New(tb testing.TB) *store.Store {
18		tb.Helper()
19		dir := tb.TempDir()
20		st, err := store.Open(filepath.Join(dir, "test.db"))
21		if err != nil {
22			tb.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
23		}
24		tb.Cleanup(func() { _ = st.Close() })
25		return st
26	}
27	
28	// MustUser inserts a user with a placeholder password hash and returns it.
29	// Convenient for tests that need a logged-in identity but don't care about auth.
30	func MustUser(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, userID, nickname string) *store.User {
31		tb.Helper()
32		u, err := st.Users().Create(context.Background(), userID, "$2a$12$placeholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholder.", nickname, "")
33		if err != nil {
34			tb.Fatalf("create user %q: %v", userID, err)
35		}
36		return u
37	}
38	
39	// MustBoard returns a seeded board by name, failing the test if not found.
40	// Useful with the default Welcome / Test / ChitChat boards which
41	// SeedDefaults / migration 0002 create automatically.
42	func MustBoard(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, name string) *store.Board {
43		tb.Helper()
44		b, err := st.Boards().GetByName(context.Background(), name)
45		if err != nil {
46			tb.Fatalf("get board %q: %v", name, err)
47		}
48		return b
49	}
50
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"regexp"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode/utf8"
7	
8		"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
9	)
10	
11	// PadRight pads s with spaces on the right so its display width equals w.
12	// If s is wider than w, it's truncated. Critical for table-like rendering
13	// with mixed ASCII + CJK strings — lipgloss's automatic padding miscounts
14	// double-width glyphs in some terminals.
15	func PadRight(s string, w int) string {
16		if w <= 0 {
17			return ""
18		}
19		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
20		if cw == w {
21			return s
22		}
23		if cw > w {
24			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
25		}
26		return s + strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw)
27	}
28	
29	// PadLeft is PadRight's mirror, padding before the string.
30	func PadLeft(s string, w int) string {
31		if w <= 0 {
32			return ""
33		}
34		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
35		if cw == w {
36			return s
37		}
38		if cw > w {
39			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
40		}
41		return strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw) + s
42	}
43	
44	// Truncate cuts s to display-width w, appending "…" if truncation occurred.
45	func Truncate(s string, w int) string {
46		if runewidth.StringWidth(s) <= w {
47			return s
48		}
49		if w <= 1 {
50			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
51		}
52		return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "…")
53	}
54	
55	// Width returns the display width of s.
56	func Width(s string) int { return runewidth.StringWidth(s) }
57	
58	// ansiCSIRe matches a CSI escape sequence: ESC '[' optional-params letter.
59	// SGR (color), cursor moves, and most other terminal control codes used by
60	// banners follow this shape. Other escape kinds (OSC, DCS) are left
61	// untouched — banners almost never carry them.
62	var ansiCSIRe = regexp.MustCompile("\x1b\\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]")
63	
64	// stripCSI returns s with CSI escape sequences removed. Use it for any
65	// width / column calculation that involves banner content (or any other
66	// string that may carry inline color codes). The four helpers above
67	// intentionally do NOT call this — they're the article-list table path
68	// where input is plain text, and stripping every line would just be
69	// overhead.
70	func stripCSI(s string) string {
71		return ansiCSIRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
72	}
73	
74	// bannerVisualWidth returns the display width of s with CSI escapes
75	// excluded from the count.
76	func bannerVisualWidth(s string) int {
77		return runewidth.StringWidth(stripCSI(s))
78	}
79	
80	// bannerTruncate clips s to display-width w, preserving inline CSI runs
81	// (color codes are zero-width). On overflow the result ends with "…"
82	// (which counts toward the budget). If the input contained any CSI, the
83	// result is suffixed with `\x1b[0m` so a forgotten reset doesn't bleed
84	// into subsequent terminal output.
85	func bannerTruncate(s string, w int) string {
86		if w <= 0 {
87			return ""
88		}
89		if bannerVisualWidth(s) <= w {
90			return ensureANSIReset(s)
91		}
92		budget := w - 1 // reserve one cell for "…"
93		var out strings.Builder
94		visible := 0
95		cursor := 0
96		for _, loc := range ansiCSIRe.FindAllStringIndex(s, -1) {
97			if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:loc[0]], &visible, budget) {
98				out.WriteString("…")
99				return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
100			}
101			out.WriteString(s[loc[0]:loc[1]])
102			cursor = loc[1]
103		}
104		if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:], &visible, budget) {
105			out.WriteString("…")
106			return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
107		}
108		out.WriteString("…")
109		return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
110	}
111	
112	func writeBodyUpToBudget(out *strings.Builder, body string, visible *int, budget int) bool {
113		for i := 0; i < len(body); {
114			r, size := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(body[i:])
115			rw := runewidth.RuneWidth(r)
116			if *visible+rw > budget {
117				return false
118			}
119			out.WriteRune(r)
120			*visible += rw
121			i += size
122		}
123		return true
124	}
125	
126	func ensureANSIReset(s string) string {
127		if strings.Contains(s, "\x1b[") && !strings.HasSuffix(s, "\x1b[0m") {
128			return s + "\x1b[0m"
129		}
130		return s
131	}
132
```
</details></tool-use>

```go
// Package i18n is the BBS's tiny translation layer. Two locales for now —
// zh-TW (default, canonical) and en — with strings declared as Go map
// literals per locale (see zh_tw.go, en.go). The English table is allowed
// to be partial; missing keys fall back to the zh-TW table so a half-done
// translation never empties the UI.
//
// Glyph-level locale switches (push kind 推/噓/→ → 👍/👎/→, score 爆 → 💥,
// comments-mode badges [鎖]/[箭] → [L]/[A]) live in glyphs.go and are kept
// out of the generic key/value table because they're consumed by the TUI
// render path and have a width-parity invariant the tests assert.
//
// Hard rule: callers in internal/markdown and the markdown export pipeline
// MUST NOT consult this package. The export format is canonical zh-TW so
// the parser at internal/markdown/parsePushLine round-trips. See
// internal/store/pushes.go's Glyph() doc for the invariant.
package i18n

import "fmt"

// Locale is the BCP-47-ish tag persisted in users.locale. Keep the set
// small and explicit so a typo in the DB or a stale row doesn't silently
// switch a user to an unrecognised language — Normalize maps unknowns
// back to Default.
type Locale string

const (
	LocaleZHTW Locale = "zh-TW"
	LocaleEN   Locale = "en"

	// Default is the fallback locale. Empty/unrecognised values from the
	// DB resolve here, and the en table falls back here for missing keys.
	Default Locale = LocaleZHTW
)

// Valid reports whether s names a recognised locale. The locale-settings
// screen calls this before persisting; everywhere else uses Normalize.
func Valid(s string) bool {
	switch Locale(s) {
	case LocaleZHTW, LocaleEN:
		return true
	}
	return false
}

// Normalize coerces a raw string (typically users.locale or the empty
// string from a nil User on the register screen) to a known locale.
// Unrecognised values become Default rather than erroring — a stale row
// from a future "this locale was removed" migration shouldn't break the UI.
func Normalize(s string) Locale {
	if Valid(s) {
		return Locale(s)
	}
	return Default
}

// tableFor returns the string table for loc, or nil if loc isn't one we
// know about. The lookup goes through this rather than a public map so
// callers can't mutate the tables at runtime.
func tableFor(loc Locale) map[string]string {
	switch loc {
	case LocaleZHTW:
		return zhTW
	case LocaleEN:
		return en
	}
	return nil
}

// T looks up key in loc's table, falling back to Default's table when the
// key is missing or empty in loc. If both tables miss, the key is returned
// wrapped in «» so an unlocalised render is grep-able in the running UI
// (instead of crashing or rendering an empty string).
func T(loc Locale, key string) string {
	if tbl := tableFor(loc); tbl != nil {
		if v, ok := tbl[key]; ok && v != "" {
			return v
		}
	}
	if loc != Default {
		if tbl := tableFor(Default); tbl != nil {
			if v, ok := tbl[key]; ok && v != "" {
				return v
			}
		}
	}
	return "«" + key + "»"
}

// Tf is fmt.Sprintf over T. Format directives (%s, %d, etc.) live inside
// the locale string so word order can flow naturally per language —
// ("%s 推了你的文章" vs "%s 👍 your post"). i18n_test.go asserts both
// locales declare the same directive count so a translator dropping a %s
// is caught at test time, not when a notification fires.
func Tf(loc Locale, key string, args ...any) string {
	return fmt.Sprintf(T(loc, key), args...)
}

```

package i18n

// Translation keys. Naming convention: dotted, screen-grouped, lower-case.
// Top namespaces: common.* (cross-screen primitives), screen.<name>.*
// (per-screen labels), error.* (error toasts), notify.* (webhook titles),
// help.<screen>.* (help-overlay rows).
//
// PR 1 declares only the keys the locale-settings screen needs, plus the
// new "Locale" entry on the user-settings sub-menu. PR 2 expands this
// list as it converts the rest of the screens; until then existing
// hard-coded CJK strings stay where they are.
const (
	CommonBack = "common.back"

	ScreenUserSettingsLocale     = "screen.user_settings.locale"
	ScreenUserSettingsLocaleHint = "screen.user_settings.locale_hint"

	ScreenLocaleSettingsTitle      = "screen.locale_settings.title"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsIntro      = "screen.locale_settings.intro"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsOptionZH   = "screen.locale_settings.option_zh_label"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsOptionEN   = "screen.locale_settings.option_en_label"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsNoteGlyphs = "screen.locale_settings.note_glyphs"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsDirty      = "screen.locale_settings.dirty_marker"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsFlashSaved = "screen.locale_settings.flash_saved"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsHelpLine   = "screen.locale_settings.help_line"
	ScreenLocaleSettingsHeaderHelp = "screen.locale_settings.header_help"
)

package i18n

import "github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"

// Glyph helpers. These are the locale-dependent UI substitutions: same
// concept, different glyph per locale, with display width preserved so
// column-aligned tables (board view, article push list) don't shift.
//
// CRITICAL: these functions are TUI-only. Do not use them when writing
// to disk — markdown export goes through store.PushKind.Glyph(), which
// is canonical zh-TW so internal/markdown.parsePushLine round-trips.
//
// Width parity (each pair must measure the same under
// github.com/mattn/go-runewidth, asserted by glyphs_test.go):
//   推  (2)  ↔ 👍 (2)
//   噓  (2)  ↔ 👎 (2)
//   →   (1)  ↔  →  (1)   — universal arrow, kept as-is
//   爆  (2)  ↔ 💥 (2)
//   [鎖] (4) ↔ [L] (3)   — prefix, not column-aligned; 1-cell shrink OK
//   [箭] (4) ↔ [A] (3)   — same
//   [M]  (3) ↔ [M] (3)   — universal pin marker, no swap

// PushGlyph returns the locale-appropriate glyph for a push kind, unstyled.
// Caller wraps with lipgloss styles in the TUI render path so this package
// stays free of UI dependencies.
func PushGlyph(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string {
	if loc == LocaleEN {
		switch k {
		case store.PushKindPush:
			return "👍"
		case store.PushKindBoo:
			return "👎"
		case store.PushKindArrow:
			return "→"
		}
	}
	// Default + zh-TW + unknown → canonical CJK glyph from the store
	// layer. Keeping this delegation means the canonical-glyph invariant
	// has a single source of truth.
	return k.Glyph()
}

// PushVerb returns the glyph used inside webhook notification titles
// (e.g. "[BBS] alice 推了你的文章" / "[BBS] alice 👍 your post"). Currently
// identical to PushGlyph — kept as a separate function so a future change
// to en mode (e.g. spelled-out "upvoted"/"downvoted") doesn't need to
// touch every render call site.
func PushVerb(loc Locale, k store.PushKind) string {
	return PushGlyph(loc, k)
}

// ScoreExploded returns the glyph shown in the board-view score column
// when an article's recommend_score crosses the threshold (≥ 100, see
// internal/tui/screen_board_view.go). Empty string is never returned —
// callers gate on the threshold themselves.
func ScoreExploded(loc Locale) string {
	if loc == LocaleEN {
		return "💥"
	}
	return "爆"
}

// CommentsModeBadge returns the bracketed comments-mode marker shown as
// a prefix on board-list rows and inline in the article header. Returns
// the empty string for CommentsModeOpen (the default — no badge).
func CommentsModeBadge(loc Locale, m store.CommentsMode) string {
	switch m {
	case store.CommentsModeLocked:
		if loc == LocaleEN {
			return "[L]"
		}
		return "[鎖]"
	case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
		if loc == LocaleEN {
			return "[A]"
		}
		return "[箭]"
	}
	return ""
}

```

package i18n

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"
)

func TestNormalize(t *testing.T) {
	cases := []struct {
		in   string
		want Locale
	}{
		{"", Default},
		{"zh-TW", LocaleZHTW},
		{"en", LocaleEN},
		{"fr", Default},     // unrecognised → fallback
		{"zh", Default},     // close-but-not-it → fallback
		{"zh-CN", Default},  // not declared yet
		{"EN", Default},     // case-sensitive on purpose; users.locale stores canonical
	}
	for _, tc := range cases {
		if got := Normalize(tc.in); got != tc.want {
			t.Errorf("Normalize(%q) = %q, want %q", tc.in, got, tc.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestValid(t *testing.T) {
	cases := map[string]bool{
		"zh-TW": true,
		"en":    true,
		"":      false,
		"fr":    false,
	}
	for in, want := range cases {
		if got := Valid(in); got != want {
			t.Errorf("Valid(%q) = %v, want %v", in, got, want)
		}
	}
}

// Every key declared in keys.go (or as any other constant we export) must
// resolve to a non-empty zh-TW translation. zh-TW is the canonical table
// — a missing key here means T() falls all the way through to «key» and
// the UI shows raw key syntax.
func TestZhTW_KeysAllNonEmpty(t *testing.T) {
	for k, v := range zhTW {
		if strings.TrimSpace(v) == "" {
			t.Errorf("zhTW[%q] is empty — every key needs a canonical zh-TW value", k)
		}
	}
}

// en is allowed to be partial during the PR 2 rollout — missing keys
// fall back to zh-TW. But every key that IS in en MUST also be in zhTW;
// an en-only key would silently be unreachable when a zh user reads it.
func TestEN_KeysExistInZhTW(t *testing.T) {
	for k := range en {
		if _, ok := zhTW[k]; !ok {
			t.Errorf("en[%q] has no zhTW counterpart — every key must exist in the canonical table", k)
		}
	}
}

// When a key is present in BOTH tables, fmt directives must match. A
// translator dropping a "%s" would otherwise emit "%!(MISSING)" only
// when that specific notification fires.
func TestFormatDirectiveParity(t *testing.T) {
	for k, zh := range zhTW {
		env, ok := en[k]
		if !ok || env == "" {
			continue
		}
		zhCount := countDirectives(zh)
		enCount := countDirectives(env)
		if zhCount != enCount {
			t.Errorf("key %q: zh has %d fmt directives (%q), en has %d (%q) — counts must match",
				k, zhCount, zh, enCount, env)
		}
	}
}

// countDirectives counts %s/%d/%v/%q/%t (the subset we actually use).
// Doubled %% is intentionally counted as zero (it's a literal percent).
// This is a loose linter — sufficient for catching "translator forgot
// the %s" without needing to reimplement fmt's directive parser.
func countDirectives(s string) int {
	n := 0
	for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
		if s[i] != '%' || i+1 >= len(s) {
			continue
		}
		switch s[i+1] {
		case '%':
			i++ // skip literal %%
		case 's', 'd', 'v', 'q', 't':
			n++
			i++
		}
	}
	return n
}

// Spot-check the public T() API: a known zh-only key returns zh in en
// mode (fallback path), and a totally unknown key returns the «key»
// sentinel.
func TestT_FallbackBehavior(t *testing.T) {
	// Add a temporary en-empty key by reading one we know is zh-only:
	// CommonBack is in both, so fallback isn't observable. Use a key
	// that we deliberately leave in zh only by running with a nonsense
	// key to test the «key» sentinel.
	got := T(LocaleEN, "definitely.not.a.real.key")
	if got != "«definitely.not.a.real.key»" {
		t.Errorf("T(en, unknown) = %q, want sentinel", got)
	}
	if got := T(LocaleZHTW, CommonBack); !strings.Contains(got, "返回") {
		t.Errorf("T(zh, CommonBack) = %q, want zh value", got)
	}
	if got := T(LocaleEN, CommonBack); got != "Back" {
		t.Errorf("T(en, CommonBack) = %q, want \"Back\"", got)
	}
}

```

package i18n

import (
	"testing"

	"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

// TestPushGlyph_WidthParity guards the en-mode emoji swap: 推→👍, 噓→👎,
// →→→, 爆→💥. Each pair must measure the same display width under
// runewidth, otherwise the board-list and article-view push columns
// shift by one cell when a user toggles locale. Catches a future
// "improvement" to a 1-cell ASCII glyph that would silently break
// the table layout.
func TestPushGlyph_WidthParity(t *testing.T) {
	for _, k := range []store.PushKind{
		store.PushKindPush,
		store.PushKindBoo,
		store.PushKindArrow,
	} {
		zh := PushGlyph(LocaleZHTW, k)
		en := PushGlyph(LocaleEN, k)
		zw := runewidth.StringWidth(zh)
		ew := runewidth.StringWidth(en)
		if zw != ew {
			t.Errorf("kind %q: zh=%q (w=%d) vs en=%q (w=%d) — widths must match for column alignment",
				k, zh, zw, en, ew)
		}
	}
}

func TestScoreExploded_WidthParity(t *testing.T) {
	zh := ScoreExploded(LocaleZHTW)
	en := ScoreExploded(LocaleEN)
	zw := runewidth.StringWidth(zh)
	ew := runewidth.StringWidth(en)
	if zw != ew {
		t.Errorf("ScoreExploded: zh=%q (w=%d) vs en=%q (w=%d) — widths must match", zh, zw, en, ew)
	}
	if zw != 2 {
		t.Errorf("ScoreExploded zh-TW width = %d, want 2 (column reserves 2 cells for this glyph)", zw)
	}
}

// Canonical zh-TW glyph delegation: PushGlyph(zh, k) must equal
// store.PushKind.Glyph(). Locks in the round-trip invariant — if anyone
// edits PushGlyph and breaks this, markdown export silently changes too.
func TestPushGlyph_ZHIsCanonical(t *testing.T) {
	for _, k := range []store.PushKind{
		store.PushKindPush,
		store.PushKindBoo,
		store.PushKindArrow,
	} {
		got := PushGlyph(LocaleZHTW, k)
		want := k.Glyph()
		if got != want {
			t.Errorf("kind %q: PushGlyph(zh) = %q, store.Glyph() = %q — must match for canonical round-trip", k, got, want)
		}
	}
}

func TestCommentsModeBadge(t *testing.T) {
	cases := []struct {
		mode   store.CommentsMode
		zh, en string
	}{
		{store.CommentsModeOpen, "", ""},
		{store.CommentsModeLocked, "[鎖]", "[L]"},
		{store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly, "[箭]", "[A]"},
	}
	for _, tc := range cases {
		if got := CommentsModeBadge(LocaleZHTW, tc.mode); got != tc.zh {
			t.Errorf("CommentsModeBadge(zh, %q) = %q, want %q", tc.mode, got, tc.zh)
		}
		if got := CommentsModeBadge(LocaleEN, tc.mode); got != tc.en {
			t.Errorf("CommentsModeBadge(en, %q) = %q, want %q", tc.mode, got, tc.en)
		}
	}
}

```go
package tui

import "github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/i18n"

// localeOf returns the active UI locale for a Deps. Centralises the
// nil-User guard so screens that may render before login (register,
// password-change-on-first-login) don't have to spell it out — they
// just call localeOf(m.deps) and get Default.
//
// Also runs the value through i18n.Normalize so a stale or junk
// users.locale row falls back gracefully instead of mis-rendering as
// the «key» sentinel everywhere.
func localeOf(d Deps) i18n.Locale {
	if d.User == nil {
		return i18n.Default
	}
	return i18n.Normalize(d.User.Locale)
}

```
